188. `check_test_imports_use_tac_not_src_tac` — FIX-WAVE-10 R10-1 silent-test-CI-evasion self-protect 2026-05-13 + FIX-WAVE-11 R11-1 META-class scope expansion 2026-05-13. Refuses `.py` files under `src/tac/tests/`, `tests/`, `src/tac/`, `experiments/`, `tools/`, and `scripts/` that import via the non-canonical `from src.tac.*` / `import src.tac.*` syntax (real import statements line-anchored) OR reference `src.tac.*` inside quoted string literals (subprocess `python -c` / exec invocations). The canonical pytest config (`pyproject.toml` `[tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = ["tests", "src/tac/tests"]`) installs `tac` as the canonical package name; `src.tac.*` works only when the CWD-anchored `src/` is on `sys.path` (e.g. `PYTHONPATH=<repo> pytest <file>`). Default `pytest` from the repo root collects from testpaths but raises `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'` at import time, so files inside configured testpaths fail default collection; files outside testpaths can remain silently uncollected — high-impact manual-vs-CI divergence. Bug class: R10 review caught 3 test files: (a) `src/tac/substrates/_shared/test_trainer_skeleton.py` outside testpaths AND using `src.tac.*` — silently uncollected regardless of import syntax; (b) `src/tac/tests/test_categorical_substrate.py` + (c) `src/tac/tests/test_anr_token_renderer.py` in correct path BUT `src.tac.*` imports ERROR at collection. R11 review caught the META-class scope expansion: 2 RUNTIME training entry points (`experiments/train_anr_token_renderer.py`, `experiments/train_categorical_renderer.py`) self-patching `sys.path` to repo-root with `from src.tac.*` runtime imports (6 violations across both files) + 1 subprocess string-literal invocation (`tools/dispatch_readiness_apogee_int6.py:95` calling `python -c "from src.tac.preflight import ..."`). Companion fixes: (a) `git mv` to `src/tac/tests/test_trainer_skeleton.py`; (b-c) rewrite the 3 offending test import lines to canonical `from tac.* import ...`; (d) FIX-WAVE-11 rewrites all 7 R11-1 surfaces — 6 runtime imports + 1 subprocess string-literal — to canonical `tac.*` syntax and repoints the 2 training entry points' `sys.path` patch from `parent.parent` to `parent.parent / "src"` so canonical `tac.*` resolves under README's `PYTHONPATH=src:upstream` entry point. Detection: line-anchored regex `^\s*(?:from\s+src\.tac(?:\.|\s)|import\s+src\.tac(?:\.|\s|$))` matches real import statements only — docstring/comment mentions of `src/tac/...` paths are NOT flagged. A secondary string-literal regex `['\"].*?(?:from\s+src\.tac\.|import\s+src\.tac\.).*?['\"]` catches subprocess invocations. Acceptance: same-line `# SRC_TAC_IMPORT_OK:<reason>` waiver (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected); `__pycache__` excluded; only `.py` files scanned; exempt path markers `experiments/results/`, `_intake_`, `.omx/oss_export/`, `vendored`, `build/lib/`, `reports/raw/`; self-exempt files `src/tac/preflight.py` (regex/docstring signature carriers) and the test file itself (fixture strings contain verbatim violation samples). Sister of `check_test_files_imports_resolve` (catalog letter I; broken-import detection) — together they extinct the silent-test-CI-evasion class bidirectionally (broken-import → ImportError at collect; non-canonical-import → ModuleNotFoundError at collect; both prevent canonical CI coverage). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (test/runtime corpus clean; R10 drove 3 test violations to 0; R11 drove 7 runtime/subprocess surfaces to 0). 26 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_188_test_imports_canonical.py` covering positive (top-level / indented / multi-line / multiple violations / multiple files / runtime / subprocess), negative (canonical `tac.*` allowed / docstring mentions / string literals not as exec invocation / empty corpus / missing dirs), waiver mechanism (same-line accepted / placeholder rejected / preceding-line not accepted), exempt-marker behavior (experiments/results/ + _intake_ skipped), strict mode (raises on violation / silent on clean), live-repo regression guard, self-exempt files (preflight signatures + test fixtures), and edge cases (__pycache__ excluded / nested subdirs scanned / non-.py files ignored / outside scan roots ignored). Per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable + the "FIX-WAVE under-scope" META-pattern: R11 diagnosed that R10's narrow-scope fix left adjacent surfaces active; FIX-WAVE-11 extincts the META-class structurally across all 4 scan-dir surfaces. Memory: `feedback_fix_wave_10_r10_findings_LANDED_20260513.md` + `feedback_fix_wave_11_r11_findings_LANDED_20260513.md`.

189. `check_shell_empty_arrays_guarded_under_set_u` — Codex substrate-dispatch actuator harden 2026-05-13. Refuses substrate smoke-before-full operator wrappers (`scripts/operator_authorize_substrate_*_modal_a100_dispatch.sh`) that enable nounset (`set -u`, `set -euo pipefail`, or `set -o nounset`) and then expand an optional array as `"${ARR[@]}"` without guarding the empty case. Bug class: macOS bash 3.2 raises `unbound variable` for an empty declared array under `set -u`; `bash -n` cannot catch this runtime trap. Anchor: `scripts/operator_authorize_substrate_siren_modal_a100_dispatch.sh --dry-run` failed before any Modal spend with `SMOKE_ARGS[@]: unbound variable`, blocking the SIREN score-lowering canary actuator despite local readiness being green. Companion fix rewrites all 13 substrate smoke-before-full wrappers to use `${ARR[@]+"${ARR[@]}"}` and adds `--dry-run` support to `tools/run_modal_smoke_before_full.py`, so operator dry-runs resolve recipes and planned smoke/full phases without spawning Modal work. Same-line waiver: `# SHELL_EMPTY_ARRAY_OK:<reason>` for arrays proven non-empty. STRICT-from-byte-one; live count at landing: 0. Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_189_shell_empty_array_guard.py` plus dry-run subprocess coverage across every `operator_authorize_substrate_*_modal_a100_dispatch.sh` wrapper in `src/tac/tests/test_operator_authorize_scripts.py`. Memory/ledger: `.omx/research/siren_readiness_cpu_smoke_recheck_20260513_codex.md`.

190. `check_substrate_trainer_does_not_hardcode_hardware_substrate` — SIREN pre-dispatch audit harden 2026-05-13; C6 post-train artifact-crash harden 2026-05-14. Refuses `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` trainers that write a literal `hardware_substrate="linux_x86_64_<gpu>"` into `ContestResult` posterior rows instead of dynamically resolving the actual dispatch substrate, and refuses canonical `detect_hardware_substrate(...)` / `_canon_detect_hardware_substrate(...)` calls missing the required `substrate_tag=` keyword. Bug class: SIREN and sister substrate recipes can target A100, 4090, H100, A10G, or L40S while the trainer silently labels every successful anchor as T4, corrupting cost-band posteriors, GPU/CPU gap analysis, and apples-to-apples architecture comparisons; separately, C6 reached post-training stats emission then crashed because the canonical helper was called with no `substrate_tag`. Companion fix adds the canonical `tac.substrates._shared.trainer_skeleton.detect_hardware_substrate(...)` helper and migrates the substrate trainers to use provenance/env/`nvidia-smi` detection with explicit `linux_x86_64_unknown_cuda` fallback rather than false precision. Same-line waiver: `# HARDWARE_SUBSTRATE_HARDCODE_OK:<reason>` for rare fixtures that intentionally pin a known substrate or reviewed non-runtime detector fixture. STRICT-from-byte-one; live count at landing and 2026-05-14 extension: 0. Dedicated tests: `src/tac/tests/test_check_190_substrate_trainer_hardware_substrate.py`.

191. `check_modal_dispatch_threads_sentinel_files_per_catalog_166` — SIREN pre-dispatch audit harden 2026-05-13. No waiver; these tokens are the runtime contract. STRICT-from-byte-one; live count at landing: 0. Dedicated tests are in `src/tac/tests/test_operator_authorize_scripts.py` plus the Catalog #166 Modal hardening suite.

192. `check_macos_cpu_advisory_not_promoted_without_linux_verification` — macOS-CPU first-class advisory proxy wiring 2026-05-13 (operator routing "training is the real roadblock; we can prepare and run things on macos and cpu"). Per CLAUDE.md PR107 empirical calibration (M5 Max `0.19664189` matched GHA Linux x86_64 `0.1966358879` within `6e-6`) macOS-CPU is wired as a FREE first-class advisory proxy that participates in autopilot dispatch ranking BEFORE any GPU spend. Per CLAUDE.md "Submission auth eval — BOTH CPU AND CUDA, ON 1:1 CONTEST-COMPLIANT HARDWARE" non-negotiable, macOS-CPU is NEVER 1:1 contest-compliant — promotion REQUIRES a paired `[contest-CPU GHA Linux x86_64]` anchor. This STRICT gate refuses any persisted artifact under `.omx/state/`, `experiments/results/`, or `reports/` (JSON / JSONL) that combines `evidence_grade="macOS-CPU-advisory"` (or `evidence_tag` carrying `[macOS-CPU advisory`) with `score_claim=True`, `promotion_eligible=True`, or `ready_for_exact_eval_dispatch=True`. Whole-file waiver `# MACOS_CPU_ADVISORY_PROMOTED_OK:<reason>` for the rare deliberate post-paired-Linux-verification case. Sister of Catalog #127 (`check_authoritative_tag_requires_custody_metadata`) — #127 enforces the tag/axis/hardware-substrate triple at CALL sites; #192 enforces the same contract at PERSISTED ARTIFACT level. Companion landings: `tac.optimization.macos_cpu_advisory_signal` (canonical module, mirrors `mps_research_signal`), `tools/build_macos_cpu_advisory_signal_manifest.py` (CLI manifest builder), `tools/score_macos_cpu_advisory_proxy.py` (smoke ranker that runs `inflate.sh` + `upstream/evaluate.py --device cpu` on Darwin ARM64 and emits a non-promotable advisory manifest row), and an autopilot-loop `load_candidates_from_macos_cpu_advisory_manifest` + `tag_halt_events_with_proxy_evidence` wire-in (`tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py`). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (no advisory rows exist yet; the manifest builder refuses to write a promoted row at construction time). 62 dedicated tests across `src/tac/tests/test_macos_cpu_advisory_signal_manifest.py` (25 tests), `src/tac/tests/test_score_macos_cpu_advisory_proxy_cli.py` (8 tests), `src/tac/tests/test_check_192_macos_cpu_advisory_not_promoted.py` (18 tests), and `src/tac/tests/test_macos_cpu_autopilot_consumption.py` (11 tests). Memory: `feedback_macos_cpu_autopilot_wiring_landed_20260513.md`. Lane: `lane_macos_cpu_autopilot_wiring_20260513`.

193. `check_substrate_trainers_do_not_use_finite_auth_eval_parser_for_claims` — SIREN/A1 dispatch review harden 2026-05-13. 197. `check_full_cpu_requires_explicit_advisory_waiver` — Time-traveler L5 autonomy `--full-cpu` mode self-protection 2026-05-13 (operator approved). Refuses `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` / `experiments/train_renderer*.py` / `experiments/train_*.py` trainers that declare a `--full-cpu` argparse argument WITHOUT the coupled `--advisory-cpu-explicitly-waived` sister flag AND a coupled-flag validator function (name pattern `_validate_full_cpu_flags` or `validate_full_cpu_flags`) called from main. Bug class: `--full-cpu` opens a non-smoke CPU path that bypasses the canonical `device_or_die` gate (CLAUDE.md "MPS auth eval is NOISE" + "Submission auth eval — BOTH CPU AND CUDA, ON 1:1 CONTEST-COMPLIANT HARDWARE"); without the coupled waiver flag + validator the bypass produces non-promotable artifacts that may leak into autopilot ranking. The device-or-die-gate-bypass-via-uncoupled-flags bug class is extincted at THREE surfaces: CLI/source (#197), in-memory result-object (#127), persisted artifact (#192). Same-line waiver: `# FULL_CPU_COUPLED_FLAGS_OK:<reason>` on the argparse `add_argument("--full-cpu", ...)` line for the rare trainer with an alternate attestation mechanism (must document the alternative in the waiver reason). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0 (only `experiments/train_substrate_time_traveler_l5_autonomy.py` uses `--full-cpu`; it carries both coupling layers). 10 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_197_full_cpu_coupled_flags.py` + 16 trainer-mode tests in `src/tac/tests/test_train_time_traveler_full_cpu_mode.py`. Memory: `feedback_time_traveler_full_cpu_mode_landed_20260513.md`. Sister of Catalog #127 (`check_authoritative_tag_requires_custody_metadata`) + Catalog #192 (`check_macos_cpu_advisory_not_promoted_without_linux_verification`).

199. `check_operator_authorize_bypass_requires_session_budget` — UNBLOCK + REVIEW-FIX + RETRY DISPATCH self-protection 2026-05-13 (operator approved). Refuses .py/.sh callers under `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, `src/tac/` (excluding `experiments/results/`, `_intake_`, `.omx/oss_export/`, `vendored`, `/tests/`, `/test_` markers + canonical `tools/operator_authorize.py` + `src/tac/preflight.py` self-exempt) that set the env var `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CONFIRMED_VIA_SESSION_DIRECTIVE` without ALSO setting `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_SESSION_BUDGET_USD` within a 30-line callsite window. Bug class: the orchestrator failure mode anchored 2026-05-13 (BLOCKED report `feedback_5_parallel_substrate_dispatch_orchestrator_BLOCKED_20260513`) showed `tools/operator_authorize.py::_confirm()` raises EOFError when invoked from `subprocess.run(capture_output=True)` (stdin closed) — `_confirm` returns `default_yes=False` and dispatch is silently aborted with "aborted - no dispatch fired". The fix lands a paired-env-var bypass in `_confirm()`: when BOTH `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CONFIRMED_VIA_SESSION_DIRECTIVE=1` AND `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_SESSION_BUDGET_USD=<float>` are set, the prompt is bypassed and a LOUD `[OPERATOR-AUTHORIZE BYPASS ACTIVE]` banner is logged to stderr with the explicit budget cap. Bare CONFIRMED=1 without BUDGET raises `SystemExit(11)` at runtime to prevent partial-config bypass. The paired-budget requirement forces the operator to explicitly acknowledge the session-wide envelope; CONFIRMED-alone would be a blanket auto-approve safety regression. Same-line waiver `# OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_BYPASS_OK:<reason>` for rare callers that combine the directive with a built-in cap. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0. 18 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_198_operator_authorize_bypass.py` covering live-repo regression, positive .py/.sh detection (CONFIRMED without BUDGET), inline-VAR shell form, negative both-vars accepted, comment-only mention not flagged, same-line waiver opt-out, canonical-tool + preflight self-exempt, test files exempt, out-of-window budget rejected, strict-mode-raises, plus 5 runtime subprocess tests for `_session_directive_bypass_active()` helper. Memory: `feedback_unblock_review_fix_retry_dispatch_landed_20260513.md`. Sister of Catalog #143 (`check_paid_job_register_before_submit`) + Catalog #167 (`check_substrate_dispatch_uses_smoke_before_full_pattern`) — together they keep the operator-authorize → smoke-before-full → paid-job chain coherent under non-interactive subprocess invocation.

201. `check_modal_sentinel_files_are_in_mount_set` — PR95++ Modal smoke [redacted-modal-call-id] @ 2026-05-13 21:59:30 UTC returned rc=13 (`MISSING_WORKER`) after `tools/operator_authorize.py::_modal_sentinel_files` appended the recipe YAML path under `.omx/operator_authorize_recipes/<recipe>.yaml` to the `--sentinel-files` list passed to Modal dispatch. Catalog #166's worker-side hash ledger then refused the dispatch because `.omx/` is NOT one of the canonical mount-set prefixes declared by `src/tac/deploy/modal/mount_manifest.py::STRUCTURAL_MINIMUM_DIRS` (`src/`, `scripts/`, `upstream/`, `submissions/`, `experiments/`, `tools/`, or exactly `pyproject.toml`) — so the worker had no matching sentinel to hash and surfaced `MISSING_WORKER`. The dispatch failed BEFORE training started, blocking ALL substrate dispatches through `operator_authorize.py` until the fix landed. Bug class: any sentinel path outside the Modal mount set ALWAYS produces `MISSING_WORKER` and refuses dispatch rc=13. Companion runtime fix in `tools/operator_authorize.py` (1) removes the recipe-YAML `.append` call, (2) adds a module-level `_MODAL_MOUNT_SET_PREFIXES` tuple mirroring `STRUCTURAL_MINIMUM_DIRS`, and (3) adds a runtime filter `_path_under_modal_mount_set(rel)` that drops + WARNs on any raw_paths entry outside the mount set. This STRICT preflight gate is the structural self-protection per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable: it parses `tools/operator_authorize.py::_modal_sentinel_files` via AST and refuses any string-literal sentinel path (in the seed `raw_paths` list literal OR in a `raw_paths.append("<lit>")` call) that is NOT under one of the canonical mount-set prefixes. Dynamic expressions (`recipe.remote_driver`, `str(value)`, `entry.strip()`, etc.) are NOT statically checkable and are silently skipped — only `ast.Constant` string literals are flagged. Same-line waiver `# SENTINEL_OUTSIDE_MOUNT_OK:<reason>` honored on the literal's source line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected). Sister of Catalog #166 (`check_modal_dispatch_verifies_worker_source_matches_head`, the worker-side hash check that surfaces the failure rc=13) and Catalog #153 (`check_modal_dispatcher_uses_canonical_mount_builder`, the canonical mount builder that defines `STRUCTURAL_MINIMUM_DIRS`) — together the three gates close the mount-vs-sentinel parity bug class at design (#153) → source (#201) → dispatch (#166). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0. 30 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_201_modal_sentinel_files_in_mount_set.py` covering positive (`.omx/` / `.ralph/` / `configs/` / `docs/` / `cuda/` / `runtime-rs/` literals), positive (seed-list `ast.Assign` AND `ast.AnnAssign` shapes per Catalog #168), positive (multi-violation seed-list + mixed seed+append), negative (each of the 6 canonical mount-set prefixes accepted), negative (`pyproject.toml` exact-match accepted; `pyproject.toml.bak` substring rejected), negative (dynamic Attribute / Call / method-call expressions skipped), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, `<reason>` placeholder rejected, off-line waiver not honored), strict mode (raises `PreflightError` on violations; silent on clean), missing target file / missing function / syntax-error target all silent, plus live-repo regression guard. Memory: `feedback_catalog_201_modal_sentinel_mount_parity_landed_20260513.md`.

202. `check_catalog_202_bypass_requires_paired_env_attestation` — Git-transactional dirty-tree dispatch unblocking harden 2026-05-13. Refuses .py/.sh callers under `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, `src/tac/` (excluding generated/intake/test surfaces plus canonical `tools/operator_authorize.py` and `src/tac/preflight.py`) that set `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_SKIP_WHOLE_TREE_CLEAN_CHECK` without ALSO setting `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_TRUSTED_SENTINELS_CLEAN_VERIFIED` within a 30-line callsite window. Bug class: Catalog #202's `--require-clean-head` bypass is useful for trusted sentinel-clean Modal dispatches in dirty shared worktrees, but intent alone would silently turn off the whole-tree clean check without an explicit operator attestation that the Catalog #166 sentinel set is clean. Companion runtime fix in `tools/operator_authorize.py::_whole_tree_clean_check_bypass_active()` requires BOTH env vars, raises `SystemExit(12)` for bare intent, emits a loud `[OPERATOR-AUTHORIZE BYPASS]` banner when active, and leaves Catalog #166 worker-side sentinel hashes running independently. This STRICT preflight gate also verifies the canonical operator-authorize file via AST: helper exists, helper reads both env vars through `os.environ`, helper raises `SystemExit`, `_dispatch_modal` calls the helper, and the `--require-clean-head` literal remains auditable. Same-line waivers: `# OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CLEAN_BYPASS_OK:<reason>` for rare caller-side independent attestation and `# CATALOG_202_BYPASS_LOGIC_OK:<reason>` for rare canonical-helper alternate logic. Sister of Catalog #166 (worker-side source hash), #191 (dispatcher threads sentinels/clean-head), #199 (paired-env noninteractive operator authorization), and #201 (sentinels stay inside Modal mount set). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0. Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_202_operator_authorize_clean_bypass.py` and `src/tac/tests/test_check_202_whole_tree_clean_check_bypass.py` cover live-repo zero, positive .py/.sh detection, paired-env acceptance, comment/docstring non-detection, same-line waiver, out-of-window rejection, canonical helper AST regression cases, strict-mode raise, runtime helper subprocess behavior for no env, bare intent, paired env, falsy attestation, and `_dispatch_modal` command construction with/without the bypass.

203. `check_modal_training_image_includes_hard_runtime_deps` — PR95++ enhanced-curriculum Modal smoke dependency-closure harden 2026-05-14. Refuses any state where `experiments/modal_train_lane.py`'s Modal training image omits hard entropy/runtime dependencies declared in `pyproject.toml` for archive/inflate/replay paths. Sister of Catalog #153/#165/#166 (Modal mount/source correctness) and #167 (smoke-before-full): a smoke that reaches Python import setup must fail locally/preflight before paid GPU if the image omits known archive-runtime deps. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against"; live count at landing: 0. Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_203_modal_training_image_runtime_deps.py` cover live repo clean, missing constriction, missing pyppmd, comment-only false confidence, pyproject without entropy dep, and strict-mode raise; `src/tac/tests/test_modal_train_lane_hardening.py` pins the live Modal image dependency literals.

204. `check_pr95plus_modal_smoke_uses_durable_provider_output` — PR95++ enhanced-curriculum Modal smoke score-custody harden 2026-05-14. Refuses any state where the PR95++ remote smoke writes archive/runtime/auth-eval evidence only under the Modal worker's `/tmp/pact` workspace, where `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py` correctly rejects score-grade JSON as non-durable. Empirical anchor: clean-head smoke `substrate_pr101_lc_v2_clone_enhanced_curriculum_modal_a100_dispatch_20260514T011305Z__smoke__100ep` reached training, produced a valid archive/runtime, and then failed at inline `contest_auth_eval.py` with "evidence path is under temp storage"; a concurrent bypass attempt added `--allow-temp-work-dir`, but that would make the smoke score diagnostic-only and unsuitable for smoke-before-full custody. Companion runtime fix defaults `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_pr101_lc_v2_clone_enhanced_curriculum.sh` to `/modal_results/${DISPATCH_INSTANCE_JOB_ID}/output` when `MODAL_RUNTIME=1`, exports `CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=:4096:8` for deterministic CUDA matmul warnings, removes the temp-output bypass from `experiments/train_substrate_pr101_lc_v2_clone_enhanced_curriculum.py`, and makes `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` return `/modal_results/<label>` artifacts during recovery. Sister of Catalog #167 (smoke-before-full), #166 (Modal source/head parity), and #203 (Modal runtime dependency closure): a smoke that validates contest-CUDA semantics must preserve the score evidence as provider-durable custody, not only as stdout or temp scratch. STRICT-from-byte-one; live count at landing: 0. Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_204_pr95plus_modal_durable_output.py` cover live repo clean, temp-workspace default rejection, `--allow-temp-work-dir` rejection, missing Modal volume harvest rejection, and strict-mode raise. **Cross-driver expansion 2026-05-19** (lane `lane_catalog_204_cross_driver_expansion_20260519`): per the Catalog #244 NVML wave precedent + sister `956ad2e76` stack_of_stacks driver fix, the gate now ALSO scans `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_*.sh` glob for the canonical 3-branch Modal-aware OUTPUT_DIR resolution. Sister bug-class anchors that the cross-driver scope-extension extincts: stack_of_stacks 2026-05-19 + stc_v2 2026-05-14 + the historical PR95++ anchor — all three were the SAME class (driver defaulted OUTPUT_DIR to `$LOG_DIR/output` which resolves to `/tmp/pact` on Modal workers and gets refused by `contest_auth_eval.py`). Pre-expansion: only 3 of 50 substrate drivers were structurally compliant (PR95++, stack_of_stacks, d1); 47 were vulnerable. Post-expansion commit `b43c8f2fd` (Phase 2 driver fixes) drives all 50 drivers to the canonical pattern. Acceptance cascade for cross-driver scan: (a) canonical 3-branch shape detected via regex; (b) driver has no `OUTPUT_DIR=` assignment (scaffold-only out of scope); (c) same-line `# CATALOG_204_CROSS_DRIVER_WAIVED:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected). Sister-extended tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_204_cross_driver_expansion.py` (20 tests covering helper regex unit cases, end-to-end clean/violation aggregation, waiver semantics, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR variant rejection, multi-violation aggregation, live-repo regression guard, and orchestrator-strict-true wire-in guard).

206. `check_subagent_dispatches_use_checkpoint_discipline` — Subagent crash-resume discipline 2026-05-14 (operator directive "why did it die? need to investigate and fix permanently"). Empirical anchor: Wyner-Ziv research subagent (id `a1362a24d986029c3`) crashed mid-session with Anthropic API `Internal server error` after 17 minutes / 58 tool uses / 1704 tokens; all in-flight progress was lost. Sister pattern WAVE-3-HNERV-C-RETRY (DSNeRV + HiNeRV trainers) hit the same failure class but survived because intermediate commits had already landed. Bug class: long-running subagents accumulate non-trivial work in-context (file edits, research synthesis, dispatch plans) that is INVISIBLE to the parent until either (a) a commit lands, or (b) the subagent reports back. When the API crashes mid-session, every uncommitted-and-unreported byte is lost. The 3-layer permanent fix: (Layer 1) canonical checkpoint store `tools/subagent_checkpoint.py` appends JSONL to `.omx/state/subagent_progress.jsonl` under `fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX)` per Catalog #131 bare-write discipline; schema = `{subagent_id, parent_id_or_session, lane_id, step, status (in_progress|blocked|complete), files_touched, next_action, notes, written_at_utc, pid, host}` — `lane_id` added 2026-05-14 codex finding #3 fix to enable predecessor-resume by lane id. (Layer 2) new CLAUDE.md "Mandatory crash-resume protocol" subsection under "Subagent coherence-by-default" mandates that every long-running subagent (a) read predecessor checkpoint at start, (b) checkpoint every ~10 tool uses or major milestone, (c) checkpoint `--step complete` on completion. **Codex finding #3 fix (2026-05-14)** added three predecessor-resume query paths to `tools/subagent_checkpoint.py read`: `--parent-id-or-session <id>` (resolve by predecessor's parent session), `--latest-incomplete` (return the most-recent non-complete record), `--lane-id <lane>` (resolve by structured `lane_id` field OR notes-substring fallback). The original `--subagent-id` exact-match flow is preserved for backward compat. (Layer 3) this STRICT preflight gate scans the last 50 commits, intersects with `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` (so only TRUE subagent commits per Catalog #117 are in scope), and refuses any commit body that does NOT mention the canonical checkpoint surface (`tools/subagent_checkpoint.py` / `subagent_checkpoint.py` / `subagent_progress.jsonl` / `checkpoint discipline honored`) OR carry a same-line `# CHECKPOINT_DISCIPLINE_WAIVED:<reason>` waiver for short subagents (≤5 tool uses estimate). Bare `CHECKPOINT_DISCIPLINE_WAIVED` keyword and placeholder `<reason>` literal are both rejected so the catalog's own example phrasing cannot self-waive. Sister of Catalog #117 (`check_subagent_commit_serializer_uses_lock` — the gate that establishes which commits are subagent-attributed) + Catalog #119 (`check_subagent_commits_have_co_author_trailer` — the gate that requires Claude attribution). Together the three close the subagent-attribution → checkpoint-discipline → co-author chain. **STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-14** per codex finding MEDIUM #2 fix with cutoff `_CHECK_206_DISCIPLINE_CUTOFF_UTC = "2026-05-14T11:00:00Z"`. Strict-flip is atomic per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule": commits whose serializer-log `started_at_utc` is BEFORE the cutoff are exempt (legacy backfill window covering the originally-detected 13 in-flight subagent commits + sister-subagent OSS release prep + catalog-claim helper commits); commits AT OR AFTER the cutoff MUST comply or carry the waiver. Companion fix in `tools/claim_catalog_number.py --commit-via-serializer` now emits a `# CHECKPOINT_DISCIPLINE_WAIVED:` waiver in catalog-claim commit bodies so future claim commits do NOT trip the gate. Live count at strict-flip landing: 0 (post-cutoff). 90+ dedicated tests: 27 in `src/tac/tests/test_subagent_checkpoint.py` (schema validation, atomic append, latest/read API, CLI surface, 8-thread × 5-row concurrent-append safety) + 36 in `src/tac/tests/test_check_206_subagent_crash_resume_discipline.py` (canonical-token recognition, waiver-with-reason acceptance, bare-keyword + placeholder rejection, serializer-log intersection, dirty-commit flag, operator-commit excluded, strict-mode raises, live-repo regression guard, cutoff filter pre/post behavior, missing-`started_at_utc` legacy-treatment, orchestrator-callsite regression guard, post-cutoff strict-mode regression guard) + 27 in `src/tac/tests/test_subagent_checkpoint_resume_lookup.py` (the codex #3 fix: structured `lane_id` field, `read_checkpoints_by_parent`, `read_checkpoints_by_lane` with notes-substring fallback, `latest_incomplete_checkpoint`, CLI `read --parent-id-or-session` / `--latest-incomplete` / `--lane-id`, mutually-exclusive query-mode validation, end-to-end successor-with-different-id crash-resume scenario, CLI subprocess invocation). Memory: `feedback_subagent_crash_resume_discipline_landed_20260514.md` + `feedback_codex_3_findings_fix_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_subagent_crash_resume_discipline_20260514` + `lane_codex_3_findings_fix_20260514`.

207. `check_no_unguarded_rmtree_in_build_tools` — Codex 3-findings fix HIGH (2026-05-14). Refuses `tools/build_*.py` / `tools/promote_*.py` whose enclosing function BOTH (a) references a `--force`-style flag (`args.force` / `arguments.force` / `.force` / `options.force` / `namespace.force`) AND (b) calls `shutil.rmtree(...)` (or bare-imported `rmtree(...)`) WITHOUT a recognized guard token (`_assert_rmtree_safe`, `assert_rmtree_safe`, `rmtree_within_namespace`, `ensure_within_namespace`, `ensure_under_results_namespace`, `validate_force_delete_path`, `guard_force_delete`, `UnsafeRmtreeRefusedError`) in the same function body. Empirical anchor: `tools/build_pr101_nonlocal_sweep_packets.py:712-714` piped a user-controlled `--out-dir` straight into `shutil.rmtree` when `--force` was set; a typo such as `--out-dir . --force` would have recursively deleted the entire repo, and an absolute path outside the repo would have deleted unrelated user state. Companion fix landed in the same commit-batch: `_assert_rmtree_safe(out_dir)` validator + `_write_tool_owned_manifest(out_dir)` helper + `UnsafeRmtreeRefusedError` typed exception in `tools/build_pr101_nonlocal_sweep_packets.py`. The validator refuses (1) repo root, (2) `$HOME`, (3) absolute non-repo paths, (4) parent-directory escapes, (5) the `experiments/results/` namespace root itself, (6) any path NOT under `<repo_root>/experiments/results/`, AND (7) any namespaced path that does NOT contain the tool-owned manifest `build_pr101_nonlocal_sweep_manifest.json` (or legacy `manifest.json`). Same-line waiver `# RMTREE_UNGUARDED_OK:<reason>` honored on the rmtree call line; placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. The narrow scope (only fires when the function references a `--force` flag) avoids false positives on the many legitimate rmtree calls that recursively clear a freshly-created tmp/staging directory whose path is computed entirely inside the function. Sister of Catalog #154 (`check_experiments_results_gc_helper_is_canonical` — extincts the same bug class for the canonical `experiments/results/` GC helper) and Catalog #156 (`check_gc_helper_refuses_delete_on_tracked_paths` — defense-in-depth against tracked-path deletion). Together they extinct the user-controlled-recursive-deletion bug class across the canonical GC surface, the public release prep surface, and the build-tool surface. Canonical helper file `tools/build_pr101_nonlocal_sweep_packets.py` is on the `_CHECK_207_CANONICAL_HELPERS` allowlist (its rmtree IS guarded by the helper it itself defines); future canonical helpers should be added there. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — live count at landing: 0 (1 sister callsite in `tools/build_pr101_runtime_packet.py:511` annotated with `# RMTREE_UNGUARDED_OK:cleanup of parity dir created by run_local_inflate_parity earlier in this try block` since it's a legitimate cleanup of a self-created parity directory on timeout). 21 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_207_no_unguarded_rmtree_in_build_tools.py` covering positive (unguarded rmtree gated by `--force` flag flagged / `promote_*.py` also flagged / multiple violations one file all flagged / bare `rmtree` import detected), negative (rmtree without `--force` out of scope / guard token in body satisfies / namespace guard satisfies / canonical helper file exempt), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, naked `:` rejected, `<reason>` placeholder rejected), strict-mode (raises with PreflightError, silent on clean), AST helper edge cases (file-glob filter / missing dir / syntax error tolerated / bare rmtree import recognized), live-repo regression guard, and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wire-in regression guard. 17 sister tests in `src/tac/tests/test_build_pr101_nonlocal_sweep_packets_force_guard.py` covering the runtime guard itself: positive case (namespaced dir + tool-owned manifest accepted / legacy `manifest.json` accepted), refusal cases (repo root / `--out-dir . --force` / `$HOME` / absolute non-repo path / namespace root / repo-but-outside-namespace / parent-directory escape / namespace dir without tool-owned manifest / namespace dir with unrelated files / unresolvable path), boundary (non-existent path no-op accepted), tool-owned manifest writer round-trip, end-to-end main()-style refusal. Memory: `feedback_codex_3_findings_fix_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_codex_3_findings_fix_20260514`.

205. `check_inflate_py_uses_canonical_select_inflate_device` — A1 PR Council Round 1 finding F1/F11 (CRITICAL, 2026-05-13) inflate-device-fork permanent fix. Refuses `submissions/*/inflate.py` files containing the raw inline `device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"` (or `torch.device(...)` wrapper) pattern OUTSIDE the canonical `select_inflate_device` helper. Empirical anchor: A1 archive sha `87ec7ca5f2f328a8acdfc65f5cce0ab08a3a558eae88f36d4140870f141492b5` produced contest-CPU `0.19285` AND contest-CUDA `0.22635` from the SAME archive bytes — a +0.0335 score gap caused by inline device-fork at `submissions/a1/inflate.py:56` rendering different frames per device (F.interpolate bicubic kernel + clamp/round/uint8 cast are non-bit-identical across CPU/CUDA). Per CLAUDE.md FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS "Forbidden device-selection defaults (the MPS-fallback trap)" + "MPS auth eval is NOISE" non-negotiables: the inflate device must be operator-pinnable via `PACT_INFLATE_DEVICE` env var (auto/cpu/cuda) with `mps` explicitly refused. Companion fix refactored 4 raw-inline files (`submissions/a1/inflate.py`, `submissions/anr_substrate/inflate.py`, `submissions/categorical_substrate/inflate.py`, `submissions/hnerv_lc_ac/inflate.py`) to add a local `select_inflate_device` helper matching the canonical `tac.substrates._shared.inflate_runtime.select_inflate_device` contract, plus renamed `submissions/scpp_substrate/inflate.py::_select_device` → `select_inflate_device` for canonical-name consistency. The 8 `submissions/pr106_*_residual_sidecar/inflate.py` files already followed the canonical local-helper pattern and pass without modification. Acceptance: (a) file uses local helper named exactly `select_inflate_device` AND helper body honors `PACT_INFLATE_DEVICE` env var (text-substring check); (b) inline pattern only appears INSIDE the helper body (its `auto` mode last-branch return — that IS the canonical design); (c) same-line `# INLINE_DEVICE_FORK_OK:<reason>` waiver on the assignment line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the gate's own docstring cannot self-waive). Scope: `submissions/*/inflate.py` ONLY (`submissions/exact_current/` is exempt as the pinned upstream snapshot per CLAUDE.md mutation frontier; `experiments/results/` is DERIVED_OUTPUT per Catalog #113 and out-of-scope). Sister of Catalog #1 `check_no_mps_fallback_default` (which scans `src/tac`/`experiments`/`scripts` for the MPS-fallback ternary; this gate is the `submissions/` complement scoped to inflate runtime files specifically). Together they extinct the silent device-selection-fork bug class across the entire repo. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — live count at landing: 0 (4 raw inline sites + 1 wrong-helper-name site driven to 0 in the same commit batch across 33 scanned `submissions/*/inflate.py` files; `submissions/exact_current/` correctly excluded). 23 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_205_inflate_device_fork.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (bare `torch.device(...)` inline / bare string inline / wrong helper name `_select_device` / canonical helper without `PACT_INFLATE_DEVICE` env var), negative (canonical helper accepted / canonical helper with extra top-level code), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, `<reason>` placeholder rejected, empty waiver rejected), aggregation (multiple violations one file / multiple files), scope (`exact_current` exempt, `experiments/results/` out-of-scope), edge cases (no `submissions/` dir / empty `inflate.py` / malformed Python tolerated / unrelated assignment not flagged / helper-internal fallback not flagged), and strict-mode behavior (raises on violation, silent on clean, returns list when not strict, verbose summary). Memory: `feedback_catalog_205_inflate_device_fork_gate_landed_20260513.md`. Cross-ref `feedback_a1_pr_submission_council_round_1_of_5_20260513.md` (the F1/F11 anchor), `submissions/a1/dual_eval_adjudicated.json` (the empirical +0.0335 gap), commit `27a7950fd` (the floor-v3 routing memo that posted A1 as the verified frontier), and the canonical helper at `src/tac/substrates/_shared/inflate_runtime.py`. <!-- HISTORICAL_SCORE_LITERAL_OK:a1_archive_cpu_cuda_gap_anchor_2026-05-13_catalog_205_landing -->

208. `check_docs_no_local_absolute_paths` — OSS v0.2.0-rc1 release prep audit finding D-2 self-protect (2026-05-14). Refuses `docs/**/*.md` files containing hardcoded local absolute path patterns (`/Users/<word>/`, `/home/<word>/`, `/private/var/`, `/tmp/`, `C:\Users\<word>\`). Per CLAUDE.md "Public Disclosure Hygiene" non-negotiable: "Keep credentials, private infrastructure URLs, **local absolute paths**, raw provider logs, unpublished operator state, and account metadata out of GitHub/docs/site/public supplement surfaces." Anchor: OSS audit commit `c293ba425` found 2 tracked files in `docs/superpowers/**` leaking `/Users/adpena/Projects/pact/...` paths — `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-10-anti-drift-runtime-hardening.md` (3 leaks) and `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-anti-drift-runtime-design.md` (13 leaks in markdown link URLs). Companion fix sanitized both files: plan file's 3 `--repo-root /Users/...` operator-example flags rewritten to `--repo-root <repo-root>`; spec file's 13 absolute markdown link URLs rewritten to `../../../`-anchored repo-relative links. Acceptance: (a) same-line `# DOCS_LOCAL_PATH_OK:<reason>` or `<!-- DOCS_LOCAL_PATH_OK:<reason> -->` waiver on the leak line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the gate's own docstring cannot self-waive); (b) demo-block waiver `<!-- DEMO_LOCAL_PATH_OK -->` HTML comment within 3 preceding lines allows literal local paths inside the NEXT code-fenced block (rare; document-and-justify only); the waiver does NOT persist past the closing fence. Detection is line-anchored regex; the `/tmp/` pattern requires a leading non-path character (so `attempt/tmp_path` and similar embedded substrings are NOT flagged). Self-exempt: `src/tac/preflight.py` (carries the patterns in the regex literals) and `src/tac/tests/test_check_208_docs_no_local_absolute_paths.py` (contains verbatim violation samples in tmp_path fixtures). Sister of Catalog #109 `check_public_pr_intake_clones_pristine` (which guards public-PR intake source provenance from in-place edits) and the FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS rule "Forbidden /tmp paths in any persisted artifact (the transient-evidence trap)" — together they extinct the local-path-leak class across upstream-imported sources, public docs, and persisted evidence ledgers. WARN-ONLY at landing per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule": 14 pre-existing violations across 5 doc files OUTSIDE the D-2 scope (`docs/2026-04-07-comprehensive-handoff.md`, `docs/paper/SUBMISSION_CHECKLIST.md`, `docs/recovery_audit_session_20260505.md`, `docs/remote_gpu_lane.md`, `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-05-smarter-segmentation-main-roi.md`) remain at landing; the 2 D-2 anchor files are clean. Strict-flip pending the bulk backfill-or-waive sweep on the 5 broader-finding files. 24 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_208_docs_no_local_absolute_paths.py` covering (i) positive (macOS home / Linux home / private-var / /tmp/ / Windows home / markdown-link / multi-line / nested-subdir paths), (ii) negative (relative path / placeholder / no-docs-dir / non-markdown file / unrelated path / tmp-substring inside word), (iii) waiver mechanism (HTML comment / hash comment / placeholder rejection / demo-block 3-line marker / demo-block does-not-persist-past-fence), (iv) strict-mode (raises with PreflightError + "Public Disclosure Hygiene" message / silent on clean), and (v) D-2 anchor regression guards (plan file clean / spec file clean / live-repo D-2 files clean). Memory: `feedback_docs_local_path_sanitization_catalog_208_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_docs_local_path_sanitization_208_20260514`.

**DP1 Phase 2 contest-video-leakage caller-routing (2026-05-14, STRICT @ 0):**

209. `check_no_contest_video_leakage_in_distillation_callers` — DP1 Phase 2 self-protection 2026-05-14. Refuses `distill_codebook(...)` callsites under `src/tac/`, `tools/`, `experiments/`, `scripts/` (excluding the canonical `src/tac/substrates/pretrained_driving_prior/distillation.py` implementation file + tests + intake clones + OSS export mirrors + `experiments/results/` build artifacts) that do NOT route through a `Comma2k19FrameIterator(...)` construction (which runs `check_no_contest_video_leakage` internally on the chunk path BEFORE any decode). Acceptance: (a) Comma2k19FrameIterator construction within 30-line lookback OR (b) canonical helper `_build_frame_iterator(...)` / `build_frame_iterator(...)` invocation within 30-line lookback OR (c) manual `check_no_contest_video_leakage([...])` invocation within 30-line lookback OR (d) same-line `# COMMA2K19_LEAKAGE_VERIFIED_OK:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive) OR (e) file-level `# COMMA2K19_LEAKAGE_VERIFIED_OK_FILE:<rationale>` waiver in the first 20 lines (rare; for harnesses that route through internal helpers not detectable statically). Bug class: per CLAUDE.md "HNeRV / leaderboard-implementation parity discipline" L1 the codebook MUST be distilled from OUT-OF-DISTRIBUTION dashcam data, never the contest video; bypassing the `Comma2k19FrameIterator` constructor's leakage refusal makes the protection opt-in instead of structural. The L1 scaffold landing 2026-05-13 deferred this catalog # because no real frame iterator existed; Phase 2 lands `Comma2k19FrameIterator` and so the gate has live coverage. Sister of Catalog #124 (`check_representation_lane_has_archive_grammar_at_design_time` — the L1 scaffold's representation-lane gate that admits DP1 with all 8 fields declared) + Catalog #164 (scorer-preprocess routing — the DP1 trainer's `score_pair_components` callsite is the canonical pattern).
