210. `check_dp1_codebook_provenance_metadata_present` — DP1 Phase 2 hardening v2 self-protection 2026-05-14 (operator directive *"need to ensure it's useful... will be using that over and over"*). Refuses DP1 archive builders that strip the 6 required forensic provenance metadata keys (`license_tags`, `dataset_provenance`, `distillation_version`, `random_seed`, `basis_sha256`, `num_frames_used`). Bug class: DP1 is the canonical pretraining lane reused across A1/PR101/HDM8/YUCR/TT5L/sane_hnerv; without forensic provenance, downstream replay cannot audit dataset origin, license attribution, codebook reproducibility, or tampering. A future hand-rolled federated aggregator or "minimal repack" script that drops provenance silently inherits the bug class. Scope: scans every file in `src/tac/`, `tools/`, `experiments/`, `scripts/` that imports from `tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior` AND uses `pack_archive(`, OR explicitly references DP1's own `DP1_MAGIC` / `DP1_HEADER_FMT` / `DrivingPriorArchive` tokens (false-positive guard prevents flagging sister substrates' own `pack_archive` that mention `pretrained_driving_prior` only in a registry list). For each in-scope `pack_archive(...)` callsite, requires the 60-line preceding window to reference all 6 keys OR carry a same-line `# DP1_PROVENANCE_OK:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected). Self-exempt: `src/tac/substrates/pretrained_driving_prior/{distillation,codebook,composition}.py` + `src/tac/preflight.py`. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (driven from 5 → 0 by tightening scope to require DP1 imports AND fixing 2 legitimate trainer callsites in `experiments/train_substrate_pretrained_driving_prior.py` smoke + full meta dicts). 10 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_210_211_dp1_hardening.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (DP1 archive builder missing provenance), negative (all 6 keys present), same-line waiver, placeholder rejection, false-positive guard (substrate name in registry list NOT flagged), strict-mode raise, tests/ exempt, canonical implementation self-exempt, experiments/results/ exempt. Sister of Catalog #211 (the canonical-helper-routing gate that propagates provenance via `verify_composition` forensic surface). Memory: `feedback_dp1_phase_2_hardening_v2_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_dp1_phase_2_hardening_v2_20260514`.

211. `check_dp1_composition_routes_through_canonical_helper` — DP1 Phase 2 hardening v2 self-protection 2026-05-14 (operator directive *"need to ensure it's useful... will be using that over and over"*). Refuses any caller that hand-rolls DP1 composition (e.g. ad-hoc `open(dp1).read() + open(base).read()` byte concatenation) outside the canonical helper API at `tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior.composition`: `compose_with(dp1_bytes, base_bytes, *, base_substrate)` / `decompose(composed_bytes)` / `verify_composition(composed_bytes, ...)` / `compose_from_files(dp1_path, base_path, out_path, ...)`. Bug class: hand-rolled byte concatenation silently bypasses the byte-stable DPCOMP wrapper (13-byte header + length-prefix + 4-byte base_tag); downstream replay cannot detect codebook tampering, license attribution is lost, the inflate path mis-parses the bytes. Scope: scans every file in `src/tac/`, `tools/`, `experiments/`, `scripts/` that REFERENCES DP1/DPCOMP magic literals (`b"DP1\x00"` or `b"DPC\x00"`) AND does NOT import / use any of the 4 canonical helpers. The DP1 archive module itself (uses `DP1_MAGIC` + `DP1_HEADER_FMT` together) is heuristically accepted — that path is Catalog #210 territory. Same-line `# DP1_COMPOSITION_OK:<rationale>` waiver accepted for the rare legitimate manual byte path; placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. Self-exempt: `src/tac/substrates/pretrained_driving_prior/composition.py` + `src/tac/substrates/pretrained_driving_prior/__init__.py` + `src/tac/preflight.py`. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (no production hand-rolled callers exist; the canonical helper module + tests are the only references to DPCOMP magic). 13 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_210_211_dp1_hardening.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (hand-rolled DPCOMP magic), negative (compose_with / decompose / verify_composition / compose_from_files imported), same-line waiver, placeholder rejection, archive-module-pattern accepted, strict-mode raise, tests/ exempt, intake clones exempt, canonical implementation self-exempt, DP1 magic alone without compose handler flagged. Sister of Catalog #210 (the codebook-provenance gate). Memory: `feedback_dp1_phase_2_hardening_v2_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_dp1_phase_2_hardening_v2_20260514`.

220. `check_substrate_l1_scaffold_no_byte_addition_without_operational_score_improvement_mechanism` — D1 R3 self-protection 2026-05-14 (RECOVERY-1 commit batch resumes crashed predecessor `ac92c985dc4f99357` D1-L2-FIX-AND-PERMANENT-PROTECTION). Refuses any in-scope substrate lane at Level ≥1 in `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` whose `notes` + gate `evidence` strings claim a byte addition >1 KB (canonical regex patterns: `+\d+ KB` / `~\d+ KB` / `overhead.*\d+ KB` / `sidecar.*\d+ KB` / `d1_overhead_bytes=\d+` / `archive_bytes_added=\d+` / `rate +0.0\d+` / `\d+,\d+ bytes`) WITHOUT one of the following acceptance signals: (1) operational mechanism token — `score_improvement_mechanism_status=OPERATIONAL` / `l2_integration_landed` / `l2_overlay_active` / `runtime_overlay_consumed=true` / `operational_overlay=true` / `operational_consumption=true`; (2) `research_only=true` / `research-only=true` per CLAUDE.md HNeRV parity L2 (export-first design opt-out); (3) BOTH `lane_class=substrate_engineering` AND a pre-build signal (`_full_main raises NotImplementedError` / `full_path_council_gated` / `scaffold_only` / `pre_build_substrate_engineering` — substrate work where the trainer's full path is council-gated so no $1+ dispatch can fire); (4) same-line `# SCAFFOLD_DEFERRED_INTEGRATION_OK:<rationale>` waiver with a real rationale (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected). In-scope substrate lanes match by id-substring: `substrate_` / `_substrate_` / `_polytope_` / `_sidecar_` / `_overlay_` / `yucr_` / `d1_segnet` / `d2_` / `d4_` / `lane_a1_` / `_hnerv_` / `_nerv_` / `_lora_` / `wavelet_residual` / `siren_residual` / `coord_mlp_residual`. Bug class: D1 R3 dispatch 2026-05-14 (call_id `fc-01KRKABYAC9C6MA161NKSGH9PY`, $0.15 Modal T4 smoke) produced score ~0.222 vs L1 SCAFFOLD design-bound predicted band [0.181, 0.188] because the L1 substrate landed a 43 KB sidecar (margin_map_int8 brotli'd dominates) that the inflate runtime did NOT operationally consume — the overlay was no-op-by-default per Verdict V3 deferral. The sidecar bytes structurally consumed for the no-op detector (Catalog #105 / #139) but produced no per-pixel polytope-interior noise application, so the rate-axis cost (+0.029) landed without compensating Δseg savings. Net effect: paying rate-term penalty for dead bytes — the "research-substrate trap" anti-pattern from HNeRV parity discipline lesson 2 + the 8th forbidden pattern. The RECOVERY-1 commit batch (a) lands `tac.substrates.d1_segnet_margin_polytope.overlay.apply_l2_overlay_for_video_list` which applies per-pixel polytope-interior noise to frame_1 RGB at camera resolution, (b) makes `build_readiness_manifest` default `runtime_overlay_consumed=True` so post-L2-INTEGRATION lanes self-declare OPERATIONAL, (c) adds the `MARGIN_MAP_SHRUNK_RESOLUTION = (96, 128)` constant + `upsample_margin_map_for_overlay` helper for 16× archive cost reduction (~2.7 KB vs ~43 KB), and (d) updates D1's lane registry impl_complete evidence to carry the operational token + byte-addition signal so this gate fires structurally on the lane and accepts via the operational cascade. Sister of Catalog #105 / #139 (no-op detector + structural consumption proof — those gates verify the BYTES are read at runtime; #220 verifies the BYTES PRODUCE FRAME CHANGES), Catalog #124 (`check_representation_lane_has_archive_grammar_at_design_time` — the design-time discipline gate; #220 is the runtime-effect discipline gate), and Catalog #90 (`check_lane_registry_consistent` — the umbrella registry-schema gate). Together they extinct the "L1 SCAFFOLD ships byte addition with deferred overlay" failure mode. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (D1 declares operational mechanism in the same commit batch; gate accepts via cascade). 65 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_220_scaffold_no_byte_addition_without_operational.py` covering helper unit tests (in-scope classifier / byte-addition KB extraction across 11 token forms / operational signal / research-only signal / pre-build substrate-engineering signal / waiver with rationale + placeholder rejection), end-to-end gate behavior (no registry / lane below L1 / out-of-scope / no byte addition / below threshold / D1-R3-bug-class refused / operational mechanism accepted / research_only accepted / pre-build accepted / waiver accepted / placeholder waiver rejected / substrate_engineering alone insufficient / strict raises with Catalog #220 message / strict silent on clean / aggregates multiple violations / gate-evidence strings scanned / corrupted registry graceful / live-repo regression guard). Memory: `feedback_d1_l2_integration_plus_permanent_gate_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_d1_l2_integration_plus_no_byte_addition_without_score_improvement_gate_20260514`.

219. `check_archive_promotion_blocked_by_mdl_density_above_threshold` — Z1 empirical revision self-protection 2026-05-14 (operator decision #5 from `feedback_z1_mdl_ablation_landed_20260514.md`). Refuses any Level ≥2 lane in `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` whose L2+ promotion gate (`real_archive_empirical` / `contest_cuda` / `contest_cpu`) references an archive sha256 with measured scorer-conditional MDL density > 0.90 (the Z1 empirical class-saturation threshold; A1 measured 99.29%, PR106 r2 97.21%). The gate scans `experiments/results/mdl_ablation*/` for `*_mdl_ablation.json` (canonical schema: `archive_sha256` + `mdl_density_estimate_lo`), builds a sha → density index (higher density wins on collision), then extracts sha-prefix tokens (>=8 hex chars) from the lane's L2+ gates' evidence strings + notes body — ONLY in text bodies that contain a sha-indicative context token (`sha256` / `archive sha` / `...` ellipsis form) to avoid false-positive hex matches on timestamps/counters. Prefix matching against full shas supports the canonical lane-registry abbreviation `87ec7ca5...492b5`. Exemption cascades (HNeRV parity discipline lesson 7): (a) `lane_class` ∈ {substrate_class_shift, substrate_engineering, research_substrate}; (b) `target_modes` containing `research_substrate` / `research_only`; (c) `notes` body containing `research_only=true` / `lane_class=substrate_engineering` / `substrate_engineering_exception`; (d) same-line `# MDL_DENSITY_OVER_THRESHOLD_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the lane-registry notes / evidence body (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected). Bug class: a lane promoted to L2+ with archive bytes whose MDL density is empirically class-saturated is by definition in the within-class trap per Z1 ablation — any further within-class encoder/codec refinement yields diminishing returns; the operator's next dispatch should route to a class-shift architecture (predictive-receiver / cooperative-receiver / foveation / Wyner-Ziv) not another within-class bolt-on. Initial wire-in is `strict=False` (warn-only) per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (4 MDL-evidenced L2+ lanes all declare `lane_class=substrate_engineering` in their notes which exempts them as operator-acknowledged baselines; A1 and PR106 r2 ARE the empirical anchors that produced the threshold). Strict-flip planned after 3 consecutive clean preflight passes confirm 0 violations across the production registry. Sister of Catalog #90 (`check_lane_registry_consistent` — the umbrella registry-schema gate), Catalog #127 (`check_authoritative_tag_requires_custody_metadata` — the per-anchor custody rule), and Catalog #125 (`check_subagent_landing_has_solver_wire_in` — the 6-hook wire-in non-negotiable). Together they extinct the "class-saturated archive silently promoted to L2+" bug class. The cathedral autopilot ranker (`tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py`) was updated in the same commit-batch with the sister `adjust_predicted_delta_for_mdl_density` + `adjust_predicted_delta_for_class_shift` + `apply_z1_empirical_revision_to_candidate_delta` helpers and a new `apply_z1_empirical_revision=True` default kwarg on `rank_candidates` — class-saturated candidates (density > 0.95) have their predicted ΔS floored at -0.005; trending (0.90-0.95) get a 50% penalty; class-shift literature anchors (cooperative-receiver / predictive-coding / Tishby-Zaslavsky / Atick-Redlich / Rao-Ballard / Wyner-Ziv / MDL-IBPS / Slepian-Wolf / time_traveler / foveation / Information Bottleneck) receive additive -0.01 to -0.03 ΔS rewards. The re-ranked autopilot queue v2 lives at `.omx/state/autopilot_candidate_queue_v2_post_z1_revision_20260514.jsonl` and promotes time_traveler / DARTS-SuperNet / C6 MDL-IBPS class-shift candidates to top-3 ahead of all within-A1-class sidecar bolt-ons. 44 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_219_mdl_density_promotion_gate.py` covering helper unit-tests (sha context detection / MDL result discovery / sha token extraction with abbreviated forms / sha prefix matching / exemption cascades / waiver mechanism) and end-to-end gate behavior (passes when no registry / passes when no MDL evidence / flags lane with density 0.99 / skips L1 lanes / skips exempt lanes via lane_class / skips waived lanes / threshold boundary 0.90 strict > / multiple lanes flagged together / abbreviated sha form matched / strict raises with Catalog #219 message / strict silent on clean repo / live-repo regression guard). Plus 32 dedicated ranker tests in `src/tac/tests/test_cathedral_autopilot_z1_revision.py` covering the within-class trap penalty, the class-shift reward stacking, the JSONL backward-compat loader, and A1-baseline / PR106-baseline / C6-MDL-IBPS / time-traveler-L5 regression cases. Memory: `feedback_mdl_density_gate_and_autopilot_ranker_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_mdl_density_gate_and_autopilot_ranker_20260514`.

221. `check_auth_eval_result_artifacts_fail_closed_for_score_claims` — D1/Z1/FES/PacketIR evidence-surface hardening 2026-05-14. Refuses generated `experiments/results/**/auth_eval_roundtrip_results.json` artifacts whose top-level or row-level `score_claim` is anything other than `false`, whose rows are rank/kill eligible, or whose rows lack the fail-closed `result_review_blockers` (`roundtrip_matrix_is_command_planner_not_claim_surface`, `requires_separate_auth_eval_result_review_before_score_claim`). Also refuses PacketIR `closure.json` artifacts with a `contest_cpu` axis row that lacks explicit CPU-diagnostic blockers (`not_contest_cuda_axis`, `cpu_axis_not_rank_or_kill_authority`, `requires_cuda_cpu_policy_review`) in both `promotion_blockers` and `rank_or_kill_blockers`. Bug class: command-planner/recovery artifacts can preserve useful `[contest-CUDA]` and `[contest-CPU]` signal while still being non-authoritative; if nested rows leak `score_claim=true` or CPU rows carry empty blockers, downstream autopilot, rank/kill, or operator review can promote a planning artifact or CPU diagnostic as if it were reviewed CUDA frontier evidence. Companion fixes landed in `src/tac/auth_eval_roundtrip_matrix.py` (row-level `score_claim=false`, `contest_axis_anchor`, `score_claim_possible_after_result_review`, review blockers), `src/tac/analysis/scorer_conditional_mdl.py` (missing eval archive identity is weak custody, not `custody_match=true`), and `src/tac/packetir_exact_closure.py` (CPU-axis diagnostic blockers). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against"; live count at landing: 0 after regenerating the FES roundtrip results and PR106/R2 PacketIR closure. 7 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_preflight_auth_eval_result_artifact_guards.py` cover row-level score-claim leakage, missing review blockers, CPU-axis blocker omissions, strict-mode raise, clean-artifact pass, live-repo regression, and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wiring. Ledger: `.omx/research/d1_z1_score_lowering_hardening_20260514_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_d1_z1_fes_packetir_hardening_20260514`.

222. `check_scorer_loader_assignment_order` — C6/score-aware trainer self-protection 2026-05-14. Refuses any source file under `src/tac/`, `experiments/`, or `tools/` that assigns canonical scorer loader outputs in reversed order, e.g. `seg_scorer, pose_scorer = load_differentiable_scorers(...)`, `segnet, posenet = load_default_scorers(...)`, or the same pattern around `load_scorers(...)`. The canonical API returns `(posenet, segnet)`; reversing it feeds SegNet into the PoseNet slot and usually fails only inside `scorer_loss_terms_btchw` when a tensor is indexed as `fp_out["pose"]`. Anchor: C6 MDL-IBPS Modal smoke `substrate_c6_e4_mdl_ibps_modal_t4_dispatch_20260514T142426Z__smoke__100ep` failed after scorer load with `TypeError: new(): invalid data type 'str'` at `src/tac/losses/core.py:740`, caused by `experiments/train_substrate_c6_e4_mdl_ibps.py` assigning `seg_scorer, pose_scorer = load_differentiable_scorers(...)`. Companion fix changed the trainer and its real-scorer test to `pose_scorer, seg_scorer = ...`. Same-line waiver `# SCORER_LOADER_ORDER_OK:<reason>` is accepted only for deliberate nonstandard wrappers; placeholder `<reason>` is rejected. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against"; live count at landing: 0 after patching C6. 7 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_preflight_scorer_loader_assignment_order.py` cover reversed differentiable/default loader assignments, clean order, waiver semantics, strict raise, live-repo regression, and `preflight_all` strict wiring. Ledger: `.omx/research/d1_z1_score_lowering_hardening_20260514_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_c6_scorer_loader_order_hardening_20260514`.

223. `check_substrate_auth_eval_invocations_use_current_cli` — C6/auth-eval CLI self-protection 2026-05-14. Refuses any `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` trainer that invokes `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py` with stale flags such as `--archive-zip` / `--output-json`, or with a direct invocation missing any of the current required score-custody flags `--archive`, `--inflate-sh`, and `--json-out`, unless it routes through the canonical `tac.substrates._shared.smoke_auth_eval_gate.gate_auth_eval_call(...)`. Bug class: C6 reached post-train archive/runtime emission but its inline auth-eval command used stale argparse flags and no emitted runtime `inflate.sh`; the trainer caught the failure, left `auth_eval_score=None`, wrote stats, printed DONE, and returned rc=0, letting the remote driver potentially emit a false `[contest-CUDA]` completion marker from trainer rc alone. Companion fix routes C6 through `gate_auth_eval_call(...)`, writes fail-closed stats authority fields (`auth_eval_score_axis`, `auth_eval_score_claim_valid`, `promotion_eligible=false`, `rank_or_kill_eligible=false`, `result_review_blockers`), makes the remote C6 driver validate `auth_eval_score_claim_valid=true` + `contest_cuda` before writing `LANE_C6_MDL_IBPS_DONE [contest-CUDA]`, and hardens `detect_hardware_substrate(axis="cpu")` so local macOS CPU artifacts are labeled `macos_arm64` rather than `linux_x86_64_modal_cpu`. Same-line waiver `# AUTH_EVAL_CLI_OK:<reason>` is reserved for reviewed non-runtime fixtures only; production substrate trainers should use the canonical gate. STRICT-from-byte-one; live count at landing: 0 after patching C6. Dedicated tests: `src/tac/tests/test_preflight_substrate_auth_eval_cli_contract.py` plus CPU-substrate regression tests in `src/tac/tests/test_trainer_skeleton.py`. Ledger: `.omx/research/d1_z1_score_lowering_hardening_20260514_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_c6_auth_eval_cli_fail_closed_hardening_20260514`.

224. `check_modal_training_image_includes_hard_runtime_deps` extension — Modal training DALI/NVML runtime-env self-protection 2026-05-14. Tightens Catalog #203 so the canonical Modal training dispatcher must carry not only hard archive entropy dependencies, but also `DALI_DISABLE_NVML` and `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True` through the Modal image, generated `env.sh`, and subprocess environment. Empirical anchors: D1 R4 T4 smoke `fc-01KRKBF28G2M3N73FS7PDCB6AZ` and C6 patched smoke `fc-01KRKG566Z2F48CVCGF8JFA0S1` both inflated successfully and then failed inside upstream `evaluate.py` / DALI with `nvml error (999): A nvml internal driver error occurred`. Modal auth-eval and lane-specific wrappers already set `DALI_DISABLE_NVML=1`; the generic `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` image/env path lagged and could burn full inflate time before a provider NVML crash. Companion fix imports shared constants from `tac.deploy.modal.runtime`, threads them into `image.env(...)`, `env.sh`, and the lane subprocess `env`, and keeps scores fail-closed if the provider still crashes. Dedicated tests extend `src/tac/tests/test_check_203_modal_training_image_runtime_deps.py` and `src/tac/tests/test_modal_train_lane_hardening.py`. Ledger: `.omx/research/d1_z1_score_lowering_hardening_20260514_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_modal_training_dali_nvml_env_hardening_20260514`.

225. `tools/claim_lane_dispatch.py` terminal-status vocabulary hardening — dispatch ledger churn prevention 2026-05-14. Adds `timed_out` to the shared terminal-prefix vocabulary in both the CLI helper and `tac.deploy.claims`. Empirical anchor: D4 rows with statuses `timed_out_3600s_a10g_smoke` and `timed_out_3600s_t4_smoke` were operationally terminal, but `tools/claim_lane_dispatch.py summary` counted them as active because the terminal vocabulary only recognized `failed_...` timeout closures. That inflated active claims from 5 to 7 and risked false same-lane blocking or duplicate manual cleanup. Companion fix keeps existing `failed_modal_training_timeout` rows valid and additionally treats `timed_out_*` as terminal. Dedicated tests extend `src/tac/tests/test_claim_lane_dispatch.py` and `src/tac/tests/test_deploy_claims_active_row.py`. Ledger: `.omx/research/d1_z1_score_lowering_hardening_20260514_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_dispatch_claim_terminal_timeout_vocabulary_hardening_20260514`.

227. `check_substrate_class_promotion_requires_tier_c_evidence` — Tier C empirical revision self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_mdl_ablation_tier_c_ibps1_landed_20260514.md` + `project_c6_substrate_class_shift_first_empirical_confirmation_tier_c_20260514.md` operator decision #4 RECOMMENDED-LAND-NEXT). Sister of Catalog #219 (Tier A density gate). Where #219 refuses L2+ promotion when Tier A density > 0.90, #227 refuses substrate-class-shift PROMOTION (lanes claiming `lane_class` ∈ class-shift tokens OR `notes` referencing class-shift literature anchors) at L2+ WITHOUT Tier C empirical evidence. Bug class: Tier A is brotli-saturated at the byte layer (any fp16-weight + brotli archive sits at Tier A density ~0.99) and structurally CANNOT discriminate substrate class. A lane can claim ACROSS-CLASS lineage via its lane_class / notes / literature_anchor field WITHOUT empirically demonstrating the class-shift signature via Tier C post-decode perturbation. Per the C6 5ep empirical anchor, the dispositive test IS Tier C — the latent Δscore at σ=1.0 must be sub-0.05 AND state_dict perturbation must show a knee+plateau structural signature. Acceptance cascades: (a) lane evidence/notes contain a Tier C token (`tier_c` / `Tier C` / `mdl_tier_c` / etc.) — the operator has explicitly acknowledged Tier C either ran OR is in flight; (b) lane's archive sha256 is in the Tier C ablation index (an MDL ablation JSON with non-empty `tier_c` rows exists for the archive); (c) lane carries a same-line `# TIER_C_EVIDENCE_PENDING_OK:<rationale>` waiver — reserved for pre-empirical lanes (still in design / 5ep / pre-Tier-C-feasibility); placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected; (d) lane is exempt via `lane_class` ∈ {`substrate_engineering`, `research_substrate`} OR `target_modes` ∈ {`research_substrate`, `research_only`} OR notes containing `research_only=true` / `substrate_engineering_exception`. NARROWER than Catalog #219's exemption — #227 does NOT exempt `substrate_class_shift` lanes (those ARE the gated lanes). Class-shift tokens scanned: `substrate_class_shift` / `predictive_receiver` / `cooperative_receiver` / `foveation` (lane_class) + `MDL-IBPS` / `Wyner-Ziv` / `Tishby-Zaslavsky` / `Atick-Redlich` / `Rao-Ballard` / `cooperative-receiver` / `predictive-coding` / `Hafner` / `DreamerV3` / `Ha-Schmidhuber` / `world_model` / `time_traveler` / etc. (notes literature). Companion landings in same commit-batch: (1) `tools/mdl_scorer_conditional_ablation.py::aggregate_mdl_estimate` now computes `mdl_tier_c_density_estimate` + `mdl_tier_c_substrate_class_verdict` + `mdl_tier_c_curve_knee_signal` + `mdl_tier_c_latent_sigma1_delta` from any non-empty `tier_c` list (composite density = mean of two logistic-squashed signals: latent_sigma1 = |Δscore_latents@σ=1.0| / (latent_sigma1 + 0.05); curve_knee = |Δscore_sd@σ=0.1| / |Δscore_sd@σ=0.01| / (curve_knee + 3.0); thresholds density ≥ 0.70 → within-class, density ≤ 0.30 → across-class, else indeterminate); (2) `tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py` extends CandidateRow with `mdl_tier_c_density` + `composition_alpha` fields + adds `adjust_predicted_delta_for_mdl_tier_c_density` (within-class density ≥ 0.70 floors at -0.005 like Tier A; trending 0.50-0.70 halves; across-class ≤ 0.30 applies -0.01 bonus matching the Tier-C-empirical-anchor across-class reward) + `adjust_predicted_delta_for_composition_alpha` (α > 0.7 ADDITIVE / 0.3-0.7 SUB-ADDITIVE halve / ≤ 0.3 SATURATING floor at -0.005) + `load_substrate_composition_alpha_index` reader of canonical `.omx/state/substrate_composition_matrix.json` posterior surface + `apply_substrate_composition_matrix_to_candidates` populator + extends `_CLASS_SHIFT_LITERATURE_TOKENS` with `Ha-Schmidhuber` / `Hafner` / `DreamerV3` / `Dreamer V3` / `Dreamer-V3` per C1 council RETAIN decision (`project_c1_world_model_revision_SUPERSEDED_by_council_unfair_probe_finding_20260514.md`); (3) `apply_z1_empirical_revision_to_candidate_delta` composition order Tier A → Tier C → class-shift → composition_alpha. Initial wire-in is warn-only per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0 (673 lanes scanned, 0 class-shift L2+ lanes without Tier C evidence). Strict-flip planned once 0 violations are confirmed AND converged 100ep+ Tier C anchor lands on at least one class-shift substrate (C6 IBPS1 100ep at Modal A100 per HARVEST-AND-Z1 op-routable #1). Sister of Catalog #219 (Tier A density gate; brotli-saturated layer) + Catalog #125 (subagent landing 6-hook wire-in non-negotiable — this gate IS hook #4 for the Tier C result loop). Together they extinct the "class-shift claim without empirical Tier C evidence" bug class. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL without research exhaustion": the gate REFUSES promotion of an unproven class-shift CLAIM but does NOT kill the lane; the operator can either run Tier C OR add the waiver for pre-empirical scope. 48 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_227_substrate_class_promotion_requires_tier_c.py` covering helper unit-tests (class-shift detection via lane_class / notes / literature; tier_c evidence acceptance via token / sha-index; waiver semantics with placeholder rejection; sha-context discovery; exempt-cascade narrower than #219; tier_c discovery skips empty rows) + end-to-end gate behavior (passes when no registry / no class-shift lanes / L1 lanes / gate not satisfied; flags L2 class-shift without Tier C; passes with token/sha/waiver; skips research_only lanes; multi-lane aggregation; strict-mode raises with Catalog #227 + Tier C disambiguator message; verbose summary) + live-repo regression guard + orchestrator-callsite wire-in regression guard. Plus 42 ranker tests in `src/tac/tests/test_cathedral_autopilot_tier_c_and_composition.py` covering Tier C density penalty/reward bands (within-class floor / trending halve / across-class bonus / indeterminate / None / non-numeric / threshold boundaries) + composition_alpha bands (additive / sub-additive / saturating / None / non-numeric / T1-F Z3×C6 realistic value) + composition matrix loader (missing / canonical / most-recent / refuses score_claim / malformed JSON / live T1-F artifact) + composition populator (populates / sorted key lookup / skips single-substrate / handles empty) + C1 council literature token retention (Hafner / DreamerV3 / Ha-Schmidhuber) + full ranker stack composition (Tier A + Tier C + class-shift + composition correct ordering) + JSONL loader backward-compat (new fields read / invalid fields fall back / legacy rows unchanged) + rank_candidates integration (within-class loses to across-class / disable-z1-revision preserves legacy order) + live regression guards (v2 queue still loads / constants pinned). Plus 11 Tier C aggregator tests in `src/tac/tests/test_mdl_scorer_conditional_ablation.py` covering empty/canonical/within-class signatures + JSON round-trip + real C6 5ep archive regression. Memory: `feedback_autopilot_tier_c_integration_catalog_227_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_autopilot_tier_c_integration_catalog_227_20260514`.

233. `check_l1_to_l2_promotion_canonical_4_gate` — L1→L2 substrate-class promotion canonical 4-gate enforcement self-protection 2026-05-14 (per Grand Council omnibus commit `7872c9f4b` Decision 8 binding verdict, PROCEED Option D, 11/11 unanimous; lines 336-388 of `.omx/research/grand_council_omnibus_design_decisions_20260514.md`). Operationalizes the council's L1→L2 substrate-class promotion canonical: (1) **Smoke green** (rc=0 + auth-eval JSON parseable); (2) **Tier C MDL density measured** (`tools/mdl_scorer_conditional_ablation.py` on the smoke artifact); (3) **100ep auth-eval anchor** with byte-deterministic archive (sha256 stable across re-runs); (4) **Custody validated per Catalog #127** (`evidence_grade` matches axis + hardware). The conditional 5th gate (probe-disambiguator built if 2+ defensible interpretations exist) is enforced separately via Catalog #125 hook #6. Bug class anchor (per omnibus ledger §"Math + rationale" lines 375-381): each gate is structurally non-redundant; together they prevent THREE failure modes: "substrate trained but never validated" (smoke gate), "substrate validated but within-class saturated" (Tier C gate), "score claim without custody" (100ep auth-eval + Catalog #127 gates). The gate scans `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` for in-scope substrate L2+ lanes (via `_CHECK_233_IN_SCOPE_ID_SUBSTRINGS` mirroring Catalog #220's set: `substrate_` / `_substrate_` / `_polytope_` / `_sidecar_` / `_overlay_` / `yucr_` / `d1_segnet` / `d2_` / `d4_` / `lane_a1_` / `_hnerv_` / `_nerv_` / `_lora_` / `wavelet_residual` / `siren_residual` / `coord_mlp_residual`); for each in-scope L2+ lane it concatenates `notes` + every gate's `evidence` string and refuses the lane unless ALL FOUR gate-token sets are present (`_CHECK_233_SMOKE_GREEN_TOKENS` / `_CHECK_233_TIER_C_TOKENS` / `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_TOKENS` / `_CHECK_233_CUSTODY_VALIDATOR_TOKENS`). Acceptance cascades: (a) lane is exempt via `lane_class=substrate_engineering` / `lane_class=research_substrate` / `target_modes` containing `research_substrate` / `research_only` / notes containing `research_only=true` / `substrate_engineering_exception` (these lanes are explicitly NOT contest-promotion eligible per HNeRV parity discipline lessons 2 + 7); (b) lane carries a same-line `# L1_L2_PROMOTION_CANONICAL_OK:<rationale>` waiver in registry notes / evidence (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected); (c) all four gate tokens appear in concatenated lane text. Sister of Catalog #127 (`check_authoritative_tag_requires_custody_metadata` — the per-call-site custody validator routing gate; #233 is its lane-registry-level enforcement complement) + Catalog #220 (`check_substrate_l1_scaffold_no_byte_addition_without_operational_score_improvement_mechanism` — refuses L1+ scaffolds adding bytes without operational mechanism; #233 refuses L2+ promotion claims lacking the canonical 4-gate evidence) + Catalog #227 (`check_substrate_class_promotion_requires_tier_c_evidence` — refuses L2+ class-shift lanes lacking Tier C; #233 extends to ALL L2+ substrate lanes and enforces 4 gates instead of single Tier C). Together the four close the substrate L2+ promotion bug-class meta-surface: per-call-site custody routing (#127) + L1 scaffold byte-addition (#220) + L2+ class-shift Tier C (#227) + L2+ all-substrate 4-gate canonical (#233). Initial wire-in is warn-only per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" + the council's operator-routable checkpoint #2 ("any L2-claimed lane WITHOUT all 4 gates is forensically reviewed and downgraded if necessary"). Live count at landing: 6 violations across 17 in-scope substrate L2+ lanes (11 exempt via substrate_engineering / research_only opt-out, 6 unexempt: `lane_12_nerv_mask_codec`, `lane_a1_inflate_time_bias_correction_sweep`, `lane_a1_segnet_boundary_smoothing_inflate`, `lane_pr106_latent_sidecar_r2_pr101_grammar_contest_cpu`, `lane_b1_nerv_enc_dec_x_magic_codec_a1_20260512`, `lane_macos_cpu_substrate_canvas_sweep_20260513`). Strict-flip pending the operator-routed audit-and-downgrade sweep per the council's checkpoint #2 — each flagged lane is either backfilled with the missing canonical-gate evidence into its registry notes, downgraded to L1 if the 4-gate canonical cannot yet be satisfied, re-classified as `research_only=true` / `lane_class=substrate_engineering` if explicitly NOT contest-promotion eligible, OR carries a council-reviewed `# L1_L2_PROMOTION_CANONICAL_OK:<rationale>` waiver. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL without research exhaustion": the gate REFUSES L2+ promotion of unproven canonical claims but does NOT kill the lane. 60 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_233_l1_to_l2_promotion_canonical.py` covering helper unit-tests (in-scope classifier with canonical id-substring set + empty-id rejection + non-substrate id rejection; exempt classifier across all 4 exemption surfaces — lane_class / target_modes / research_only-in-notes / substrate_engineering_exception; concatenate-lane-text helper notes-plus-gates aggregation; waiver semantics with rationale accepted + `<reason>` + `<rationale>` placeholder rejection + inline-form acceptance + no-marker case; case-insensitive token-substring contains-helper preserving `_` vs space; 4-gate evaluator returning per-gate booleans across 5 single-token-only scenarios + 1 three-of-four scenario) + end-to-end gate behavior (passes when no lane registry / no L2+ substrate lanes / lane with all 4 gates in notes / lane with all 4 gates split across gate.evidence strings; flags lane missing each individual gate with the EXACT `(N) <gate-name>` message; flags lane missing all 4 gates with "missing 4 of 4" aggregation; L1 lane skipped per L1→L2 scope; L3 lane also audited per L2+ scope; research_only / substrate_engineering / research_substrate target-mode lanes exempt; waiver with rationale passes lane / placeholder rejected; multi-lane aggregation; corrupt registry handled gracefully; registry without lanes list handled; non-dict lane entries skipped; string repo_root accepted; verbose output lists per-lane state) + strict-mode (raises with PreflightError mentioning Catalog #233 + council Decision 8 + 4-gate canonical / silent on clean) + token-constants pinned (smoke / tier_c / auth_eval / custody / exempt-lane-class / exempt-target-mode token sets all importable + canonical phrasings present) + orchestrator-callsite strict=False wire-in regression guard + live-repo regression guard (upper bound 20 violations until operator-routed audit sweep brings count to 0) + Catalog #185 sister regression (gate function callable via globals) + signature regression (kwargs-only) + anti-regression (every canonical in-scope substring classifies as in-scope) + violation-message specificity (lane missing exactly 2 of 4 gates names exactly the missing 2, NOT the satisfied 2) + docstring anchors (4 gates verbatim from council verdict + omnibus commit `7872c9f4b` + "Option D" cited). Lane: `lane_d8_l1_l2_promotion_canonical_strict_preflight_20260514`. Memory: `feedback_d8_l1_l2_promotion_canonical_strict_preflight_landed_20260514.md`. Cross-ref `.omx/research/grand_council_omnibus_design_decisions_20260514.md` Decision 8 (lines 336-388, the council ledger anchor) + commit `7872c9f4b` (the omnibus binding verdict).

228. `check_substrate_trainer_consumes_f3_cache_when_flag_declared` — F3 GTScorerCache trainer-side consumption self-protection 2026-05-14 (F3-BACKPORT-WAVE-V2 op-routable #5 from `feedback_f3_backport_wave_v2_eleven_trainers_landed_20260514.md`). Refuses substrate trainers under `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` that declare the RESERVED `--enable-gt-scorer-cache` Tier-1 optimization flag (Catalog #151 manifest entry) but do NOT consume the GTScorerCache primitive (`gt_cache.lookup(` + `gt_pose_batch=` threading) in their hot loop. Bug class: a future trainer refactor could silently remove the wire-in (replace canonical `_build_optimized_training_context` with manual `loss_fn(...)`); regression would not surface until a Modal/Lightning smoke timeout on contest-resolution training. The gate delegates verdict classification to the canonical helper at `tools/check_f3_trainer_actionable.py` (`_classify` function) so the gate + operator-facing reproducer stay in lock-step. Only `NEEDS_F3_BACKPORT` and `NEEDS_F3_BACKPORT_PLUS_TIER1_FLAGS` are refused; every other canonical verdict (`ALREADY_WIRED` / `D1_SEGNET_SIDECAR_NO_HOT_LOOP` / `FULL_MAIN_STUB_NO_SCORER_HOT_LOOP` / `Z3_V1_RATE_ONLY_SCORERS_IDENTITY` / `SMOKE_SYNTHETIC_SCORERS_FULL_STUB` / `NEEDS_SUBSTRATE_F3_WIRE_IN` / `RESERVED_FLAG_BUT_NO_SCORER_HOT_LOOP` / `RESERVED_FLAG_NOT_CONSUMED` / `UNKNOWN_STATE_INVESTIGATE` / `NO_TRAINER_FILE`) is a structurally legitimate non-actionable state. Same-line waiver `# F3_CACHE_CONSUMPTION_WAIVED:<reason>` on the argparse flag-declaration line; placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. Initial wire-in is warn-only per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 2 (`s2sbs_byte_stuffing` + `vq_vae`, both `NEEDS_F3_BACKPORT_PLUS_TIER1_FLAGS`); 17 ALREADY_WIRED + 12 non-actionable verdicts classify cleanly. **Live count after vq_vae wire-in commit `0916332eb`: 1 (`s2sbs_byte_stuffing` remaining)** per QUANTIZR-2 R1 finding closure (`feedback_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_landed_20260514.md`). Strict-flip pending sister-subagent backfill of the 1 remaining trainer per F3-V2 op-routable #4. Sister of Catalog #172 (autocast_fp16) + #178 (TF32) + #179 (torch.compile) + #180 (no_grad) — same pattern of "substrate-trainer engineering hygiene gate" applied to a different Tier-1 primitive. 18 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_228_f3_cache_consumption.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (unconsumed-flag-declared trainer flagged via stub classifier), negative (wired trainer not flagged / no-flag trainer not flagged), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, placeholder rejected), strict-mode (raises with Catalog #228 / silent on clean), edge cases (no experiments dir / no canonical helper / classifier exception surfaces as violation), and orchestrator-callsite strict=False wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_f3_backport_wave_v2_eleven_trainers_landed_20260514.md` + `feedback_prompt_premise_verification_before_edit_pattern_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_catalog_227_to_231_strict_preflight_wave_20260514`.

229. `check_subagent_landing_includes_premise_verification_evidence` — premise-verification-before-edit pattern self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_prompt_premise_verification_before_edit_pattern_20260514.md`). Refuses post-2026-05-14 subagent landing memos under `~/.claude/projects/-Users-adpena-Projects-pact/memory/feedback_*_landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` (date suffix >= `20260514`) that claim >=3 bulk edits (regex captures `\d+ (trainers|substrates|files|recipes|wrappers|checks|gates) (edited|wired|rewritten|backported|patched|landed|added|refactored|extracted|created)`) WITHOUT an empirical verdict table OR reproducer-script path. Bug class: F3-BACKPORT-WAVE empirically falsified the parent prompt's "7 trainers need F3 backport" claim (0/7 actionable; 11+ OTHER trainers as actual target) via reproducer BEFORE any edit. Without this gate, future bulk-edit subagents will apply edits based on parent assertions without verification. Acceptance: (a) verdict-table token in body (`| trainer_id |` / `| verdict |` / `verdict_table` / `actionable` / `empirical verdict` / `Empirical verdict`) OR (b) reproducer-path token (`.omx/tmp/` / `_reproducer.py` / `tools/check_`). Same-line waiver `# PREMISE_VERIFICATION_WAIVED:<reason>`; file-level waiver `# PREMISE_VERIFICATION_WAIVED_FILE:<reason>` for infrastructure landings; placeholder `<reason>` rejected. Initial wire-in is warn-only per "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 1 (`feedback_d1_l2_integration_plus_permanent_gate_landed_20260514.md` claims 220 edits without table or reproducer); strict-flip pending operator-routed backfill sweep. Sister of Catalog #125 (`check_subagent_landing_has_solver_wire_in`) + Catalog #126 (`check_lane_pre_registered_before_work_starts`) + Catalog #206 (`check_subagent_dispatches_use_checkpoint_discipline`) — together the four form the discipline gate quartet for subagent landings: solver wire-in + lane registry + checkpoint discipline + premise verification. 21 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_229_premise_verification.py` covering date-suffix parser, cutoff filter (pre/post), verdict-table acceptance, reproducer-path acceptance, waiver semantics (per-line, file-level, placeholder rejection), bulk-edit threshold (<3 not flagged, regex captures multiple phrases), strict-mode raise, edge cases (no memo dir, string repo_root), and orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_prompt_premise_verification_before_edit_pattern_20260514.md` + `feedback_f3_backport_wave_v2_eleven_trainers_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_catalog_227_to_231_strict_preflight_wave_20260514`.

230. `check_bulk_rewrite_respects_sister_subagent_ownership_map` — editor-vs-editor collision self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_editor_vs_editor_collision_patterns_in_parallel_waves_20260514.md`). Refuses subagent commits (last 50, intersected with the canonical `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` so only TRUE subagent commits are scoped — operator-direct commits exempt by definition) whose body mentions a bulk-op token (`spdx header sweep` / `spdx sweep` / `bulk rewrite` / `mass refactor` / `bulk import` / `scope sweep` / `bulk-edit` / `wide-edit` / `bulk-rewrite`) WITHOUT referencing the sister-subagent ownership map / disjoint-scope marker. Bug class: F3-GTSCORERCACHE-WIRE-IN landing observed transient working-tree wipes when OSS-RELEASE subagent's SPDX header sweep captured F3's in-flight substrate files via `find` + `bulk-rewrite` operations that didn't honor the recursive R2 ownership map. Without this gate, future parallel waves with multiple editor subagents will silently revert sister-subagent edits at the filesystem level (BEFORE either subagent reaches the commit serializer, so Catalog #117/#157/#216 commit-time protections don't apply). Acceptance: ownership-map token in body (case-insensitive: `ownership map` / `disjoint scope` / `sister-subagent` / `OWNS:` / `scope isolated` / `path-prefix exclusion` / `recursive R2`). Same-line waiver `# BULK_REWRITE_OWNERSHIP_WAIVED:<reason>`; placeholder rejected. Initial wire-in is warn-only per "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; live count at landing: 0 (no subagent bulk-op commits in current serializer log); strict-flip planned after operator-routed backfill clears any legacy bulk-op commits. Sister of Catalog #117 (`check_subagent_commit_serializer_uses_lock`) + Catalog #157 (`check_commit_serializer_pre_lock_hash_against_head`) + Catalog #216 (`check_commit_serializer_post_stage_hash`). Together they extinct the commit-vs-edit collision class at four surfaces: commit-time-pre-pre-lock (#157) + commit-time-staged (#216) + commit-time-lock-arbitration (#117) + edit-time-bulk-op (#230). 22 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_230_bulk_rewrite_ownership.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (SPDX sweep / mass refactor / bulk-rewrite without ownership), acceptance (ownership map reference / OWNS: marker / path-prefix exclusion), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, placeholder rejected), out-of-scope (operator-direct commits exempt), strict-mode raise, edge cases (no serializer log, malformed JSON in log, non-git directory), constants verified, multi-violation aggregation, verbose output, string repo_root accepted, and orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_editor_vs_editor_collision_patterns_in_parallel_waves_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_catalog_227_to_231_strict_preflight_wave_20260514`.

234. `check_subagent_commit_bodies_not_empty` — META-3 R1 self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_landed_20260514.md`). Refuses subagent commits in the last 50 commits (intersected with `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` so only TRUE subagent commits are scoped) whose body is EMPTY (subject-only) OR lacks ALL canonical body markers (`Co-Authored-By` trailer / `subagent_checkpoint.py` / `subagent_progress.jsonl` / `checkpoint discipline` / `CHECKPOINT_DISCIPLINE_WAIVED` waiver / `6-hook wire-in` / `Lane:` / `lane_` / `Memory:` / `feedback_` / `.omx/research/` / `EMPTY_BODY_OK` waiver). Bug class anchor: 2026-05-14 commit `0916332eb` "Wire C1 Z5 routing and F3 cache surfaces" landed with EMPTY body — no Co-Authored-By, no checkpoint trace, no journal-grade ledger reference, no premise verifier path, no 6-hook wire-in declaration. The serializer log showed two concurrent subagent commits BOTH ending in `commit_failed:nothing-to-commit` at `head_after=0916332eb` — diagnostic signature of the commit-swap class (auto-commit hook pre-staged everything before the canonical serializer ran; well-formed subagent message bodies were never attached to the actual commit). The R1 fix-wave landing backfills `0916332eb` via `git notes` + canonical journal-grade ledger at `.omx/research/c1_z5_f3_wire_in_landing_20260514.md` + lands this gate as the structural self-protection. Same-line `# EMPTY_BODY_OK:<reason>` waiver for the rare deliberate subject-only commit (e.g. trivial state-file mutations); placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. Cutoff filter (`_CHECK_234_DISCIPLINE_CUTOFF_UTC = "2026-05-14T22:00:00Z"`) exempts pre-fix legacy commits per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; commits AT OR AFTER the cutoff MUST comply. Initial wire-in is warn-only at landing; live count at landing: 0 (no post-cutoff subagent commits yet — all 0916332eb-class violators are pre-cutoff). Strict-flip planned after operator-routed sweep confirms post-cutoff live count = 0 across at least 5 sister-subagent commits. Sister of Catalog #117 (`check_subagent_commit_serializer_uses_lock` — last-50-commit serializer usage gate), Catalog #119 (`check_subagent_commits_have_co_author_trailer` — Co-Authored-By trailer gate), and Catalog #206 (`check_subagent_dispatches_use_checkpoint_discipline` — checkpoint discipline gate). Together the four close the subagent-attribution → checkpoint-discipline → co-author → body-content chain at every surface. Memory: `feedback_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_council_proceed_20260514`.

235. `check_no_sha_prefix_length_mismatch_comparisons` — META-meta META-4 R1 self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_landed_20260514.md`). Refuses any source-code comparison pattern in `src/tac/preflight.py` of the form `<short_sha>[:7] in <set_or_iterable_of_9char_prefixes>` — the canonical bug pattern that returns False for ALL inputs. Bug class anchor: META-4 R1 finding cited Catalog #117 as having a hash prefix matching bug — `short_sha` (7 chars) compared against `seen_hashes` (which contained 9-char prefixes from serializer-log `head_after` field) via `in` membership. Empirical receipts: `seen = {'951858245'}; '9518582' in seen` returns False; ALL serializer-log rows were structurally false-positive Catalog #117 violations. The actual fix sister-landed via codex commit `4d483ac39` "preflight: fix catalog 117 serializer prefix matching" added `_check_117_serializer_sha_matches_commit(full_sha, serializer_sha)` helper that uses `full.startswith(token)` instead of `in` membership. Live count after the codex fix: 14 (legitimate operator-direct commits, NOT false positives). This META-meta gate sister-protects against future re-introduction of the bug-class via literal-pattern detection of `[:7] in seen_hashes` / `[:7] in seen_shas` / `[:7] in serializer_shas`. Same-line waiver `# SHA_PREFIX_LENGTH_MISMATCH_OK:<rationale>` for the rare deliberate-pattern-literal case (e.g. the gate's own internal pattern-tuple definition); placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" — live count at landing: 0 (3 self-references in the gate's pattern tuple definition carry the canonical waiver). Sister of Catalog #117 (the original bug-class anchor), Catalog #185 (`check_strict_flipped_catalog_entries_have_live_count_zero` — drift detection between CLAUDE.md catalog text and gate empirical result; #235 prevents the drift from arising in the first place by extincting the comparison-mismatch bug class structurally), and Catalog #118 (`check_claude_md_catalog_no_duplicate_numbers` — together the three keep the CLAUDE.md catalog table the canonical strictness ledger). Memory: `feedback_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_fix_wave_r1_wave_a_council_proceed_20260514`.

231. `check_review_gate_hook_uses_retry_helper` — DuckDB-lock-fix self-protection 2026-05-14 (per `feedback_duckdb_lock_fix_review_gate_hook_landed_20260514.md` operator decision #1, RECOMMENDED YES). Refuses any future revision of `tools/review_gate_hook.py` that replaces the canonical retry helper `review_tracker._connect_duckdb` with a bare `duckdb.connect()` call. Empirical anchor: pre-fix, F3-GTSCORERCACHE-WIRE-IN observed 5+ min / 10-retry failures forcing `REVIEW_GATE_ENABLED=0` workaround; CATALOG-226-REFACTOR observed 84.9s commit stall. The canonical fix routes through `review_tracker._connect_duckdb` (retry_seconds=1.5 budget) + `load_entities_from_json_snapshot` (JSON fallback) + `_is_duckdb_lock_error` (predicate). Detection: AST-walk `tools/review_gate_hook.py` and check (a) at least ONE canonical retry-helper token (`_connect_duckdb` / `connect_duckdb_with_retry` / `load_entities_from_json_snapshot` / `_is_duckdb_lock_error`) is imported or referenced AND (b) NO bare `duckdb.connect()` Call node appears OUTSIDE docstrings/comments. AST-aware Call collection distinguishes docstring mentions (string literals) from real Call expressions; Call nodes INSIDE a canonical helper function definition (function name contains one of the canonical tokens) are exempt because the helper legitimately wraps `duckdb.connect()`. Same-line waiver `# REVIEW_GATE_BARE_DUCKDB_OK:<reason>` on the bare-connect line; placeholder rejected. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable — live count at landing: 0 (the canonical fix is already present in `tools/review_gate_hook.py`). Sister of Catalog #131 (`check_no_bare_writes_to_shared_state`) + Catalog #138 (`check_state_writers_strict_load_for_mutating_path`) + Catalog #128 (`check_continual_learning_writes_use_lock`). Together they extinct the bare-state-IO bug class across `.omx/state` writes (#131), `.omx/state` strict loads (#138), continual-learning writes (#128), and review-tracker reads (#231). 23 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_231_review_gate_retry_helper.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (bare duckdb.connect outside helper / bare connect with helper token still flagged), negative (canonical helper used no bare connect / canonical helper internal connect exempt), docstring exclusion, waiver semantics (rationale accepted, placeholder rejected), AST helper unit-tests (collects bare call / exempts canonical function / handles syntax error / ignores unrelated calls / multiple bare calls), strict-mode (raises with Catalog #231 / silent on clean), edge cases (no hook file / string repo_root / canonical tokens constant / waiver regex / verbose output / missing canonical token / unreadable hook file), and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_duckdb_lock_fix_review_gate_hook_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_catalog_227_to_231_strict_preflight_wave_20260514`.

226. `check_trainer_auth_eval_uses_canonical_helper` — trainer hand-rolled auth_eval subprocess self-protection 2026-05-14 (RECOVERY-2 D-4 + AUTH-EVAL-CANONICAL-HELPER-GATE-AND-D4-TIMEOUT-INVESTIGATION-SUBAGENT). Refuses ANY substrate trainer under `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` that calls `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py` via a hand-built `subprocess.run` / `Popen` / `call` / `check_call` / `check_output` invocation (typically `cmd = [sys.executable, str(CONTEST_AUTH_EVAL_SCRIPT), ...]; subprocess.run(cmd, ...)`) WITHOUT routing through the canonical helper `tac.substrates._shared.smoke_auth_eval_gate.gate_auth_eval_call`. Bug class anchor: C6 5ep Modal T4 smoke `fc-01KRKG566Z2F48CVCGF8JFA0S1` (2026-05-14) returned `auth_eval rc=2` with a `usage` error in stderr because the C6 trainer hand-wrote `--archive-zip` / `--output-json` flags that do NOT exist on `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py` (the canonical CLI surface uses `--archive` / `--json-out`). The RECOVERY-2 fix landed at commit `3e4571c3a` and routed C6 through the canonical helper. This META gate is the STRUCTURAL improvement over the sister Catalog #223 (`check_substrate_auth_eval_invocations_use_current_cli`): #223 catches drift (stale flags present + required flags missing); #226 catches the PATTERN that enables drift (any hand-rolled invocation, period). #223 currently reports 0 because all 18 hand-rolled trainers use correct flags TODAY; the next CLI refactor of `contest_auth_eval.py` would silently break all 18 if #226 did not refuse the pattern at landing. AST-aware: walks each function in `experiments/train_substrate_*.py`, collects subprocess.X(...) calls whose args reference `CONTEST_AUTH_EVAL_SCRIPT` / `contest_auth_eval.py` / `contest_auth_eval` either inline OR via a previously-assigned cmd variable that itself mentions one of the tokens. Acceptance: (a) the enclosing function calls the canonical helper (`gate_auth_eval_call` / `smoke_auth_eval_gate` token in a Call node — both direct-name and aliased-import forms accepted); (b) same-line `# AUTH_EVAL_DIRECT_SUBPROCESS_OK:<reason>` waiver on the subprocess.run line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the gate's own docstring cannot self-waive). Scope: only `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` files — non-substrate trainers (`train_renderer*.py`, `train_distill.py`, etc.) are out-of-scope by design. Initial wire-in was WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 18 (all 18 substrate trainers hand-rolled the pattern with correct flags: a1_plus_lapose, a1_plus_wavelet_residual, balle_renderer, block_nerv, cool_chic, ds_nerv, ff_nerv, grayscale_lut, hi_nerv, hybrid_renderer_residual, pretrained_driving_prior, sabor_boundary_only_renderer, sane_hnerv, self_compress_nn, siren, tc_nerv, vq_vae, wavelet). **STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-14** (CATALOG-226-REFACTOR-SUBAGENT) — all 18 trainers refactored through the canonical `gate_auth_eval_call` helper in commits `7b2fece4a` (batch 1: balle_renderer / sane_hnerv / block_nerv / cool_chic / ds_nerv / ff_nerv) + `27db6f6bd` (batch 2: grayscale_lut / hi_nerv / hybrid_renderer_residual / sabor_boundary_only_renderer / self_compress_nn / siren) + `9839a3a73` (batch 3: tc_nerv / vq_vae / wavelet / a1_plus_lapose / a1_plus_wavelet_residual / pretrained_driving_prior). Live count at strict-flip: 0. The `a1_plus_lapose` + `a1_plus_wavelet_residual` refactors replaced their module-level `_run_contest_auth_eval_cuda` helper functions with inline `_canon_gate_auth_eval_call` invocations at the call sites in `_full_main` and removed the now-unused helper functions; `pretrained_driving_prior` added the canonical helper import inside the trainer's lazy-import block at the top of `_full_main`; `siren` preserves its extended `auth_eval_*` field surface (`auth_eval_score` / `auth_eval_score_axis` / `auth_eval_lane_tag` / `auth_eval_score_claim_valid` / `auth_eval_exact_cuda_complete`) populated from the canonical helper's return dict. Sister of Catalog #164 (`check_substrate_score_aware_loss_calls_preprocess_input_before_scorer` — canonical scorer-preprocess routing) + Catalog #205 (`check_inflate_py_uses_canonical_select_inflate_device` — canonical inflate device-fork) + Catalog #218 (`check_substrate_reconstruct_methods_support_mini_batch` — canonical mini-batch reconstruct) + Catalog #223 (`check_substrate_auth_eval_invocations_use_current_cli` — sister flag-drift gate). Together they extinct the substrate-trainer canonical-path-bypass bug class across all known surfaces (scorer-preprocess / inflate device / mini-batch reconstruct / auth-eval CLI flag drift / auth-eval subprocess pattern). 23 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_226_trainer_auth_eval_canonical_helper.py` covering positive (hand-rolled cmd-var subprocess.run / inline-args subprocess.run / Popen / check_call / check_output / call), negative (canonical-helper routing / aliased canonical helper / no contest_auth_eval invocation / docstring mention only / const definition only), waiver semantics (same-line with reason / placeholder rejection / wrong-line not honored), aggregation (multi-violation one file / multi-file aggregation), scope (non-substrate trainer out-of-scope), strict-mode (raises on violation with Catalog #226 + "C6 auth_eval rc=2 self-protect" message / silent on clean), edge cases (missing experiments dir / SyntaxError tolerated / unrelated subprocess unrelated), and live-repo regression guard (current count bounded 0-20). 218. `check_substrate_reconstruct_methods_support_mini_batch` — D4 OOM fix self-protection 2026-05-14 (anchor: fc-01KRK9RKD3QV4C276Y5KXFMF65 — D4 Modal T4 smoke at 2026-05-14T13:10:25Z returned rc=1 elapsed 121s with CUDA OOM at `synthesize_frame_0` F.interpolate residual upsample with 600-pair batch). Refuses substrate reconstruct methods under `src/tac/substrates/*/architecture.py` and `src/tac/substrates/*/frame*_synthesis.py` and `src/tac/substrates/*/synthesis.py` whose name matches a canonical per-pair-batch reconstruct pattern (`reconstruct_pair`, `reconstruct_pairs`, `reconstruct_batch`, `archive_build_pass`) WITHOUT one of the following: (a) a mini-batch kwarg in the signature (`pair_indices`, `chunk_size`, `indices`, `batch_indices`, `minibatch_indices`); (b) a `torch.no_grad` / `torch.inference_mode` body context OR `@torch.no_grad` / `@torch.inference_mode` decorator; (c) a same-line `# RECONSTRUCT_BATCH_OOM_OK:<reason>` waiver (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the docstring cannot self-waive). Bug class: full-N-pair forward through warp + F.interpolate residual upsample (48x64 → 384x512) requires ~13 GB activation memory which exceeds T4's 14.56 GB capacity. The fix landed `pair_indices` kwarg on `WynerZivFrame0Substrate.reconstruct_pair` (allows `torch.index_select` over per-pair motion params + residual rows; gradients flow into selected rows only via autograd scatter-add) and updated `_full_main` + `_run_val_loop` to mini-batch inside the inner loop instead of reconstructing all 600 pairs upfront. The `retain_graph=True` chain that compounded activation memory is also gone — each batch now builds its own forward graph. Sister of Catalog #154 (`check_experiments_results_gc_helper_is_canonical`) + Catalog #207 (`check_no_unguarded_rmtree_in_build_tools`) — same pattern of "force callers into the canonical bounded-memory path" applied to a different bug class. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (D4 substrate gained the `pair_indices` kwarg in the same commit batch; 29 substrate files scanned clean). 26 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_218_substrate_reconstruct_mini_batch.py` covering positive (bare reconstruct_pair / reconstruct_pairs / archive_build_pass / frame-synthesis-helper / multiple violations), negative (pair_indices / chunk_size / indices kwarg / no_grad body / inference_mode body / no_grad decorator / unrelated method), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, `<reason>` placeholder rejected, bare waiver without colon rejected), strict-mode (raises with PreflightError mentioning Catalog #218 / silent on clean), AST helpers (function name match / signature kwarg detection / missing dir / syntax error tolerated), verbose output (clean / dirty), string repo_root accepted, live-repo regression guard, and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wire-in regression guard. Plus 16 mini-batch tests in `src/tac/substrates/d4_wyner_ziv_frame_0/tests/test_d4_substrate.py::TestMiniBatchReconstructPair` covering correctness of the canonical fix (gradients flow into selected rows only, frame-1 byte-identical invariant preserved, residual rows selected by index not by 0:B slice, mini-batch loss.backward without retain_graph). Memory: `feedback_d4_oom_fix_minibatch_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_d4_oom_fix_minibatch_reconstruct_20260514`.

213. `check_comma2k19_downloads_route_through_canonical_cache` — DP1 Comma2k19 auto-load self-protection 2026-05-14 (operator directive *"you can download the dataset locally and configure the chunks dir to be fed dynamically cached with a log incremental generator"*). Refuses any caller under `src/tac/`, `tools/`, `experiments/`, `scripts/` that fetches Comma2k19 chunk bytes (URL fragments: `raw.githubusercontent.com/commaai/comma2k19`, `github.com/commaai/comma2k19/raw`, `github.com/commaai/comma2k19/blob`, `github.com/commaai/comma2k19/archive`, `academictorrents.com/details/65a2fbc964078aff62076ff4e103f18b951c5ddb`, `academictorrents.com/download/65a2fbc964078aff62076ff4e103f18b951c5ddb`, `comma2k19.tar`, `comma2k19.zip`) via download-call tokens (`urlopen`, `urlretrieve`, `requests.get`/`post`/`head`, `httpx.get`/`post`, `Session().get`, shell `wget `/`curl `/`aria2c `, `transmission-cli`, `transmission-remote`) OUTSIDE the canonical `tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior.local_chunk_cache.Comma2k19LocalCache` helper. Bug class: ad-hoc downloads silently bypass (1) URL pinning (the canonical `DEFAULT_CHUNK_MANIFEST` is the single source-of-truth — a tampered mirror or stale URL can leak into a non-canonical fetch), (2) SHA-256 integrity verification (the cache hashes every downloaded chunk against the pinned expected sha; bare downloads cannot detect tampering / bit-rot / MitM), (3) license-tag propagation (`license="MIT"` + `source_url` stamped into cache meta and forwarded to codebook provenance per Catalog #210), (4) disk-budget LRU eviction (`max_disk_gb` cap + oldest-LRU eviction; bare fetches can fill the Modal/Vast.ai worker disk and crash the dispatch), (5) `/tmp` avoidance (partial downloads land in `<cache_dir>/.downloads/` and are `os.replace`-d atomically per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden /tmp paths"). Scope: scans `.py` + `.sh` files in the 4 dirs; excludes tests / vendored intake clones / OSS export mirrors / `experiments/results/` / `reports/raw/` / `build/lib/`. Per-line detection: a line with a Comma2k19 URL fragment AND a download-call token within ±2 lines (handles multi-line argument lists). Acceptance: (a) ±15-line window contains one of the canonical-cache tokens (`Comma2k19LocalCache`, `local_chunk_cache`, `from tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior.local_chunk_cache`, `from tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior import`, `.fetch_chunk(`, `.fetch_chunks(`); (b) same-line `# COMMA2K19_DOWNLOAD_OK:<rationale>` waiver with placeholder `<reason>` rejected (so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive); (c) file-level `# COMMA2K19_DOWNLOAD_OK_FILE:<rationale>` waiver in the first 10 lines for legitimate one-off operator-reviewed download scripts (rare). Self-exempt: `src/tac/substrates/pretrained_driving_prior/local_chunk_cache.py` (the canonical helper), `log_incremental_feeder.py` (uses the helper), `__init__.py` (re-exports), `src/tac/preflight.py` (carries URL/token literals in regex/docstring). Sister of Catalog #209 (`check_no_contest_video_leakage_in_distillation_callers` — refuses non-canonical FRAME-ITERATOR construction at the distill side; #213 refuses non-canonical FETCH at the DOWNLOAD side) + Catalog #210 (DP1 codebook provenance metadata — the cache is where `license_tags` originates). Together the three gates extinct the bug class bidirectionally at every surface: download → iterator → archive provenance. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (the canonical helper is the only file with URL literals + download tokens; the helper itself is on the self-exempt list). 36 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_213_comma2k19_canonical_download.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (bare urlopen with raw.githubusercontent URL / requests.get / shell wget+curl+aria2c; multiple violations one file; multiple files), negative (canonical-cache token in lookback / `.fetch_chunk(` invocation accepted / URL mention without download call accepted as commented context), waiver semantics (rationale accepted, `<reason>` placeholder rejected, file-level waiver accepted, file-level placeholder rejected, ±2-line download-call window detection), exempt-marker behavior (experiments/results/ + intake clones + OSS export + tests/ + reports/raw/ + build/lib/ all skipped), self-exempt (canonical implementation files), strict-mode behavior (raises with PreflightError mentioning Catalog #213 + Comma2k19LocalCache on violation; silent on clean), AST-style false-positive guards (URL mention in docstring not flagged; commented URL line not flagged unless download call adjacent), and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_dp1_comma2k19_autoload_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_dp1_comma2k19_autoload_log_incremental_20260514`.

215. `check_modal_smoke_recipe_min_gpu_class_consistent` — FIX-HARDEN-OPT 2026-05-14 P0 (SIREN smoke timeout anchor 2026-05-13). Refuses substrate operator-authorize recipes (`.omx/operator_authorize_recipes/substrate_*_modal_*_dispatch.yaml`) whose full-run gpu field resolves to one of `{A100, H100, L40S}` WITHOUT a sibling top-level `min_smoke_gpu: <class>` field naming the canonical smoke compute class (one of `{T4, L4, A10G, L40S, A100, H100}`). Bug class: the smoke-before-full wrapper `tools/run_modal_smoke_before_full.py` historically defaulted to T4 to keep the smoke cheap, but coordinate-MLP / NeRV-family / Ballé hyperprior substrates can't complete a 100-epoch smoke on T4 within the 1-hour timeout — the smoke times out (rc=124) and the operator never gets the cheap integration validation. Anchor: SIREN smoke `substrate_siren_modal_a100_dispatch_..._smoke` 2026-05-13T14:04:10Z returned rc=124 at 3601s wall-clock. Companion runtime fix lives in `tools/run_modal_smoke_before_full.py`: recipes can declare `min_smoke_gpu: "<class>"` and the wrapper honors it over a CLI `--smoke-gpu` downgrade. Same-line waiver `# MIN_SMOKE_GPU_OK:<reason>` for recipes that explicitly opt to run smoke on T4 (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the docstring cannot self-waive). STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-14 by RECIPE-CANON-BACKFILL-SUBAGENT (lane_recipe_canon_backfill_sweep_20260514): the warn-only-at-26 landing was promoted to STRICT after the 26 legacy A100 recipes were backfilled with `min_smoke_gpu: "A100"` in 3 commits (NeRV-family 11 / substrate-engineering 8 / composition-stacking 7). Live count at strict-flip: 0 (27 substrate recipes scanned clean; the new SIREN-class anchor pattern is now enforced before any new A100/H100/L40S dispatch can fire). Sister of Catalog #167 (`check_substrate_dispatch_uses_smoke_before_full_pattern` — the gate that mandates the wrapper exist) + Catalog #170 (`check_substrate_recipes_declare_min_vram_gb_floor` — the sister recipe-schema gate that captures VRAM floor). Together the three close the smoke-cost/quality bug class: #167 forces operators to USE the wrapper, #170 ensures the recipe declares the resource floor, #215 ensures the smoke compute class matches the substrate-design intent. Memory: `feedback_fix_harden_opt_sweep_landed_20260514.md` + `feedback_recipe_canon_backfill_sweep_landed_20260514.md`. Lane: `lane_fix_harden_opt_sweep_20260514` (origin) + `lane_recipe_canon_backfill_sweep_20260514` (strict-flip).

**R2 critical fix wave TAO-1 + BOYD-1 (2026-05-15, STRICT @ 0):**

236. `check_catalog_233_gate_3_prose_negation_guard_present` — TAO-1 R2 prose-negation guard self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_recursive_review_r2_wave_a_fresh_eyes_council_proceed_20260514.md` Tao + MacKay CRITICAL). Refuses any state of `src/tac/preflight.py` that drops the canonical surfaces required by the prose-negation guard for Catalog #233 gate 3 (`auth_eval_100ep`): the helper function `_check_233_text_has_prose_negation_for_auth_eval`, the canonical token-set `_CHECK_233_PROSE_NEGATION_TOKENS`, the structured pattern set `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_STRUCTURED_PATTERNS`, AND the loose pattern set `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_LOOSE_PATTERNS`. Bug class anchor: the R2 ledger TAO-1 finding documented that Catalog #233 gate 3 accepted PROSE co-occurrences of "100ep" + "auth-eval" within an 80-char window even when the surrounding context was clearly NOT affirmative evidence — retired-config descriptions ("previously 100ep auth-eval was attempted; superseded by 200ep"), design discussions ("discussion of 100ep auth-eval choice from earlier"), tradeoff comparisons ("considered 100ep vs 50ep auth-eval"). Empirical premise verifier at `.omx/tmp/r2_critical_premise_verifier.txt` confirmed: pre-fix, retired-config + pure-prose mentions both returned `auth_eval_100ep=True` at gate 3. Companion runtime fix split `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_PATTERNS` into TWO sets — STRUCTURED (key=value pairs like `auth_eval_score_axis=contest_cuda` + lane tags like `[contest-CUDA]` + completion markers like `auth_eval_complete` — accepted unconditionally) + LOOSE (the original "100ep within 80 chars of auth-eval" co-occurrence — gated by the prose-negation guard). The guard scans for 32 negation tokens (`previously`, `superseded`, `deprecated`, `retired`, `discussion of`, `tradeoff(s)`, `vs `, `instead of`, `originally`, `abandoned`, `hypothetical`, `counterfactual`, `planned`, `pending`, `not yet`, etc.) within a 160-char window around each `100ep` mention. Backwards-compat preserved via `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_PATTERNS` alias union. There is no waiver — these surfaces ARE the contract. Sister of Catalog #136 (`check_custody_gate_accept_tokens_concrete_only` — same META class for the custody validator token set; #136 + #236 together extinct the bag-of-tokens validator class across BOTH the custody validator surface AND the 4-gate canonical's gate 3 surface) + Catalog #185 (`check_strict_flipped_catalog_entries_have_live_count_zero` — META-meta-meta gate that refuses CLAUDE.md drift) + Catalog #233 (the parent 4-gate canonical gate). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (all 4 required surfaces present in the live preflight.py; negation token set carries 32 tokens vs floor of 10). 26 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_236_tao1_prose_negation_guard.py` covering R2 anchor cases (retired-config / discussion-of-tradeoffs / pure-prose / planning-pending all REJECTED at gate 3), affirmative structured evidence (auth_eval_score_axis / [contest-CUDA] tag / auth_eval_complete / converged auth-eval ALL ACCEPT unconditionally + structured-overrides-prose-in-same-text), loose co-occurrence (clean ACCEPTED + `vs`/`originally`/`hypothetical`/`abandoned` markers REJECTED), prose-negation guard helper unit tests (no-100ep + empty-text + previously/superseded/discussion-of-detected + window-bounded + 100_ep + 100-epochs forms), live-repo regression guard, and token-set integrity (frozenset / lowercase / structured-loose-union-equals-legacy-alias). Plus 21 META-gate tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_236_237_meta_gates.py`. Memory: `feedback_r2_critical_fix_wave_tao1_boyd1_landed_20260515.md`. 237. `check_d9_fallback_semantic_disambiguator_present` — BOYD-1 R2 fallback-semantic disambiguator self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_recursive_review_r2_wave_a_fresh_eyes_council_proceed_20260514.md` Boyd + Tao CRITICAL). Refuses any state of `src/tac/cost_band_calibration.py` that drops the canonical surfaces required by the BOYD-1 fix: `_CHEAPER_ALTERNATIVE_FALLBACKS_PER_CLASS` dict, `_CAPACITY_OVERFLOW_FALLBACKS_PER_CLASS` dict, `class FallbackReason` enum with both `CHEAPER_ALTERNATIVE` and `CAPACITY_OVERFLOW` members, `capacity_overflow: bool` parameter on `select_provider_for_class`, AND `_fallback_reason_for` classifier helper. ALSO enforces the disjointness invariant: a (provider, gpu) tuple must NOT appear in BOTH dicts for the same dispatch class — same semantic in two places defeats the purpose of the disambiguator. Bug class anchor: the R2 ledger BOYD-1 finding documented that D9 `FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS['long_burn']=[('vastai','H100')]` was semantically OVERLOADED — Time-Traveler amendment fires when fallback is >25% CHEAPER than canonical, but `vastai/H100` ($1.50-1.99/hr) is MORE EXPENSIVE than canonical `lightning/A100` ($0/hr subscription). The Time-Traveler amendment will NEVER fire for `long_burn` because the cost-shift inequality fails. The capacity-overflow semantic (race-mode urgency when Lightning A100 saturated) had no code path. Empirical premise verifier at `.omx/tmp/r2_critical_premise_verifier.txt` confirmed: pre-fix, calling `select_provider_for_class('long_burn', consult_posterior=False)` returned canonical lightning/A100 with no fallback considered — the H100 entry was effectively dead code. Companion runtime fix per R2 ledger Option B: split `FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS` into TWO semantically-distinct dicts (`_CHEAPER_ALTERNATIVE_FALLBACKS_PER_CLASS['long_burn']=[]` + `_CAPACITY_OVERFLOW_FALLBACKS_PER_CLASS['long_burn']=[('vastai','H100')]`); added `FallbackReason` enum (`CHEAPER_ALTERNATIVE | CAPACITY_OVERFLOW`); added `_fallback_reason_for()` classifier; added `capacity_overflow=False` parameter to `select_provider_for_class` + `select_provider_for_recipe` — capacity-overflow fallbacks are ONLY considered when explicitly opt-in, preserving the auto-route safety. `ProviderRoutingDecision` exposes `fallback_reason` field + `as_dict()` serialization (string or null). Backwards-compat preserved via `FALLBACK_PROVIDERS_PER_CLASS` legacy alias (read-only union of both dicts). There is no waiver — these surfaces ARE the contract. Sister of Catalog #131 (`check_no_bare_writes_to_shared_state` — META gate for state-write discipline) + Catalog #136 (`check_custody_gate_accept_tokens_concrete_only` — META gate for token-set discipline) + Catalog #175/#177 (cost-band outcome discipline). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (all 7 required surfaces present + dict-disjointness invariant satisfied across all 5 dispatch classes). 21 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_237_boyd1_fallback_semantic_disambiguator.py` covering R2 anchor cases (long_burn-cheaper-empty / long_burn-overflow-h100 / no-overflow-optin-no-fallback / overflow-optin-considers-h100), semantic invariants (two-dicts-disjoint-per-class / legacy-alias-union / every-class-has-entries / long_burn-legacy-includes-overflow-h100), FallbackReason enum (4 tests covering both members + classifier helper across cheaper-alt / overflow / unknown / unknown-class), `select_provider_for_class` behavior (4 tests covering decision-includes-fallback-reason-field / as_dict-serializes / capacity_overflow-default-false / select_provider_for_recipe-propagates), Time-Traveler posterior-driven routing (3 tests with synthetic posterior anchors covering cheaper-alternative-fires + overflow-no-optin-no-fallback + overflow-optin-can-re-route), and autopilot wire-in (decision-serializable-for-autopilot). Plus 9 META-gate tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_236_237_meta_gates.py`. Memory: `feedback_r2_critical_fix_wave_tao1_boyd1_landed_20260515.md`.
