239. `check_classify_dispatch_no_raw_ge_boundaries` — BOYD-2 R2 classify_dispatch open-boundary self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_recursive_review_r2_wave_a_fresh_eyes_council_proceed_20260514.md` Boyd LOW; voice: convex optimization-feasibility lens). Refuses any state of `src/tac/cost_band_calibration.py::classify_dispatch` that uses raw `>=` (CLOSED boundary) against UPGRADE-class ceilings (`PER_CLASS_SOFT_WALLCLOCK_CEILING_HR[upgrade_class]` or `PER_CLASS_SOFT_COST_CEILING_USD[upgrade_class]` where `upgrade_class` ∈ {`full`, `long_burn`, `eval`}). Bug class anchor: the R2 ledger BOYD-2 finding documented that the `classify_dispatch` numeric inference branches used CLOSED `>=` boundaries for the `long_burn` upgrade — at exactly 12.0h wallclock or 100 USD cost, the routing returned `long_burn` (Lightning A100 subscription, $50+ class) instead of `full` (Vast.ai 4090, $2-15 class). This is a 5-10x cost-class jump for a 1-second wallclock difference at the boundary. Per Boyd's convex-feasibility lens: "convex feasibility regions should have OPEN boundaries (strict `<` / `>`) when crossing them changes the cost class non-trivially. The existing CLOSED boundary creates a discontinuity in cost expectation at the boundary." Companion runtime fix changed lines 1208 (`>=` → `>`) for wallclock and 1220 (`>=` → `>`) for cost on the long_burn upgrade boundary. Smoke downgrade boundaries kept CLOSED `<=` because they route DOWNWARD to the cheaper class — at the smoke ceiling exactly the safer routing is `smoke` (no cost penalty for over-routing). Acceptance: same-line waiver `# CLASSIFY_DISPATCH_GE_BOUNDARY_OK:<rationale>` for the rare future case where a CLOSED upgrade boundary is genuinely required (e.g., a new dispatch class whose cost is identical to its successor); placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected. Detection scope: only inside the `classify_dispatch` function body (other functions in the same module are out of scope); only `>=` comparisons (smoke `<=` boundaries are OUT of scope by design). Sister of Catalog #237 (`check_d9_fallback_semantic_disambiguator_present` — same lane D9, different bug class) + Catalog #136 (`check_custody_gate_accept_tokens_concrete_only` — same META pattern: refuse a class of subtle data-discipline regressions at the source level). MACKAY-2 (R2 LOW finding 2026-05-14, voice: MacKay style + immutability) was AUTO-CLOSED by sister commit `3882468ef` ("preflight: harden l1 promotion evidence matching") which converted the 4 cited Catalog #233 token sets (`_CHECK_233_SMOKE_GREEN_TOKENS`, `_CHECK_233_TIER_C_TOKENS`, `_CHECK_233_AUTH_EVAL_100EP_TOKENS`, `_CHECK_233_CUSTODY_VALIDATOR_TOKENS`) from `tuple[str, ...]` to `frozenset[str]` for O(1) membership + immutability hygiene; no new self-protection gate needed because frozenset hygiene is mathematical/idiomatic discipline rather than a recurring bug class. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (the BOYD-2 fix lands in the same commit batch as Catalog #239 + the wire-in). 27 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_239_boyd2_classify_dispatch_open_boundary.py` covering boundary semantics (wallclock 12h-1s/exact/+1s, smoke ceiling exact/-1s, cost long_burn ceiling exact/+1s, smoke cost ceiling exact, eval upgrade class), gate detection (synthetic ge_full / ge_long_burn_cost / smoke_le_skipped / outside_classify_dispatch_skipped), waiver semantics (same-line accepted / placeholder rejected), strict mode (raises with Catalog #239 message / silent on clean), edge cases (missing target file / string repo_root / unrelated dict / smoke_cost_le_skipped / multiple violations aggregated / orchestrator strict=True / source-level regression guard preserves `>` and `<=`). Plus 37/37 sister `test_d9_per_class_provider_routing.py` regression tests + 63/63 sister Catalog #233 tests + 66/66 sister Catalog #236+#237 tests pass. 240. `check_substrate_contest_cuda_chain_complete_or_research_only_tagged` — CANONICALIZE-SUBSTRATE-CONTEST-CHAIN self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_canonicalize_substrate_contest_cuda_chain_landed_20260515.md` + audit `.omx/research/substrate_contest_cuda_chain_audit_20260515.md` + canonical pattern `.omx/research/canonical_substrate_contest_cuda_chain_pattern_20260515.md`). Refuses any substrate operator-authorize recipe at `.omx/operator_authorize_recipes/substrate_*.yaml` that implicitly claims contest-CUDA dispatch capability (no top-level `smoke_only: true` / `research_only: true` / `dispatch_enabled: false` flag) while the matching `experiments/train_substrate_<id>.py` trainer's `_full_main` raises `NotImplementedError` (Phase 2 council-gated). Bug class anchors: (a) Z3 v2 smoke `fc-01KRNHWD6AEMWGDJXBEY8GM0P3` (2026-05-15) returned SMOKE GREEN with manifest layout `append_only_z3hp1_diagnostic` because the recipe was `smoke_only: true / research_only: true` — the v2 latent-replacement code path NEVER ACTIVATES even though env→CLI wiring is intact (substrate looks "built" but produces no contest-CUDA score on dispatch); (b) Z4 + Z5 (pre-fix 2026-05-15) had contest recipes (no research-only flag) while their trainers' `_full_main` raised `NotImplementedError` — Modal dispatch would have crashed pre-auth-eval at $2-15 cost per smoke (Z3 v2 / Z4 / Z5 bug class). Per CLAUDE.md "Substrate scaffolds MUST be COMPLETE or RESEARCH-ONLY" non-negotiable + "HNeRV / leaderboard-implementation parity discipline" lesson 2 (export-first design) + Catalog #220 (sister gate at the L1+ substrate-byte-addition surface). Acceptance: (a) recipe top-level YAML declares one of `smoke_only: true` / `research_only: true` / `dispatch_enabled: false` (transparent non-dispatchable per CLAUDE.md HNeRV parity L2); (b) trainer's `_full_main` is implemented (no `raise NotImplementedError` in the function body within first ~3000 chars after def); (c) recipe is an orphan (no matching trainer; out of scope — operator_authorize.py refuses missing trainers earlier); (d) same-line `# CONTEST_CUDA_CHAIN_PARTIAL_OK:<rationale>` waiver in the recipe YAML for the rare deliberate operator-approved partial state (placeholder `<reason>` / `<rationale>` literals rejected so the docstring example cannot self-waive). Detection scope: every `substrate_*.yaml` recipe under `.omx/operator_authorize_recipes/`. Companion runtime fix: same commit batch added `research_only: true` + `dispatch_blockers: [phase_2_council_approval_required_to_lift_full_main_NotImplementedError]` to `substrate_z4_cooperative_receiver_loss_modal_t4_dispatch.yaml` and `substrate_z5_predictive_coding_world_model_modal_t4_dispatch.yaml` with traceable comments referencing this catalog row. Sister of Catalog #220 (`check_substrate_l1_scaffold_no_byte_addition_without_operational_score_improvement_mechanism` — same META class at the L2-promotion-of-substrate-with-byte-addition surface; #220 catches the runtime-effect symptom AFTER promotion, #240 catches the recipe-vs-trainer-state divergence BEFORE dispatch) + Catalog #226 (`check_trainer_auth_eval_uses_canonical_helper` — sister canonical-path-enforcement gate for auth_eval) + Catalog #167 (`check_substrate_dispatch_uses_smoke_before_full_pattern` — smoke-before-full wrapper) + Catalog #176 (`check_operator_authorize_canonical_use` — operator-authorize canonical entry point). Together they extinct the "built-but-not-firing" bug class structurally at FOUR surfaces: design-time (#240) + runtime-effect (#220) + auth-eval-routing (#226) + dispatch-flow (#167/#176). Companion runtime sister at the 30-second pre-deploy harness: `tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py` extended with 7th check `recipe_status_consistent_with_trainer_state` that mirrors this gate at the operator-authorize 30s harness BEFORE any Modal dispatch fires. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — Z4 + Z5 fix lands in same commit batch driving live count to 0 (14 DISPATCHABLE_CONTEST_CUDA + 17 RESEARCH_ONLY_TRANSPARENT, 0 violations across 40 substrate recipes; 9 orphan recipes excluded as out-of-scope). 31 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_240_substrate_contest_cuda_chain.py` covering helper unit-tests (recipe id extraction across modal_t4 / modal_a100 / smoke_dispatch / local_apple_silicon suffixes; recipe research-only detection via 3 flag types + negative cases; waiver semantics with placeholder rejection; trainer `_full_main` NotImplementedError detection), end-to-end gate behavior (no recipes dir / complete substrate / research-only / smoke-only / dispatch-disabled / orphan recipe / Z3-v2-bug-class-flagged / strict-raises-with-Catalog-#240-message / strict-silent-on-clean / aggregates-multiple-violations / waiver-accepted / placeholder-waiver-rejected), live-repo regression guard, and orchestrator-callsite strict=True wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_canonicalize_substrate_contest_cuda_chain_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_canonicalize_substrate_contest_chain_20260515`. Audit JSON (machine-readable consumer surface for cathedral autopilot): `.omx/state/substrate_contest_cuda_chain_audit.json`.

**Codex round 8 MEDIUM in-place harden of #131 (2026-05-09, STRICT @ 0):**

131-v2. `check_no_bare_writes_to_shared_state` (in-place harden, no new catalog #) — Two in-place enhancements per codex round 8 MEDIUM (2026-05-09): (a) **AST-based path binding** (`_bare_write_collect_shared_vars_ast`) adds lowercase variables (e.g. `state_path = Path('.omx/state/foo.json')`), attribute paths (e.g. `self.foo_path = ...`), Path-joined RHS (`base / '.omx/state/foo.json'`), `AnnAssign` annotated bindings, and f-string bindings — every shape that the original all-caps regex collector silently missed; (b) **transactional-pattern requirement** — the previous lookback waiver accepted any lock token in the 20-line preceding window; the harden now requires EITHER a canonical helper invocation in the lookback (e.g. `update_active_jobs_locked(`, `register_instance(`, `posterior_update_locked(`) OR an explicit transactional pattern (write to `<path>.tmp` + `os.replace`) visible in the wider 30-line window. Lock-token alone is insufficient because partial writes are visible to readers even under the same lock and a process crash mid-write leaves a corrupt file. Canonical-helper detection skips `import ` / `from ` lines so an import statement bringing a helper name into the module does NOT falsely waive an unrelated bare write below. Same-line `# BARE_WRITE_OK:<reason>` waiver respected. Sister test files updated to reflect the new (correct) behavior: `test_131_locked_path_in_file_accepts` and `test_131_window_lock_token_accepts` (both in `src/tac/tests/test_preflight_proactive_custody_concurrency_audit.py`) now use the canonical transactional pattern; `test_check131_local_lock_context_accepted` (in `src/tac/tests/test_preflight_custody_validator_and_locked_writes.py`) updated similarly. New negative tests pin the hardened behavior: `test_131_lock_alone_no_atomic_replace_now_violation` + `test_check131_lock_alone_no_atomic_replace_is_violation_round8`. STRICT @ 0 (already strict in `preflight_all()`; no wire-in change). Live count after harden: 0. 15 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_codex_round78_check_131_harden_lowercase_and_atomic_replace.py`. Memory: `feedback_codex_round78_findings_fix_with_self_protection_landed_20260509.md`. The harden is a META-meta-meta layer over #131 → #128 (proactive sweep → continual-learning helper). Cross-ref Catalog #128 (`check_continual_learning_writes_use_lock`) and #131 (the original META gate).

**WIRE-AND-INTEGRATE-ALL local pre-deploy harness wire-in (2026-05-15, WARN-ONLY initially):**

241. `check_substrate_uses_register_decorator_or_explicitly_legacy_tagged` — META-LAYER-SUBSTRATE-CONTRACT-AUTO-WIRE-SUBAGENT 2026-05-15 (per `.omx/research/substrate_meta_layer_design_20260515.md` + `feedback_meta_layer_substrate_contract_landed_20260515.md`). Refuses `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` files that neither use `@register_substrate(SubstrateContract(...))` NOR carry a same-line `# LEGACY_SUBSTRATE_PRE_META_LAYER:<rationale>` waiver in the file's first ~25 lines. The META layer extincts the Z3 v2 silent-drift bug class (substrate code claimed bytes saved / recipe claimed smoke_only / Modal smoke routed V1 path — three independent surfaces drifted) by making the contract the single source of truth. Bug class anchor 2026-05-15: Z3 v2 unit test asserted -4842 B latent-replacement, recipe was honest (`smoke_only:true / research_only:true`), but Modal smoke verdict landed V1 path (additive +838 B) — three surfaces with no shared truth. Same-line waiver `# LEGACY_SUBSTRATE_PRE_META_LAYER:<rationale>` accepted with non-placeholder rationale; placeholder `<rationale>` literal rejected. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL" the 31 legacy substrates at landing are TAGGED-PENDING-MIGRATION via the waiver, not killed; sequential migration wave is the follow-up subagent. Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 31 (every legacy substrate trainer flagged as "not yet migrated"). Strict-flip planned alongside the migration's final commit. Sister of Catalog #124 (`check_representation_lane_has_archive_grammar_at_design_time` — design-time companion gate; #241 is the runtime-bound companion that forces the contract into a single executable artifact) + Catalog #125 (`check_subagent_landing_has_solver_wire_in` — the 6-hook wire-in non-negotiable; the META layer's contract IS the structural enforcement of all 6 hooks) + Catalog #220 (`check_substrate_l1_scaffold_no_byte_addition_without_operational_score_improvement_mechanism` — the operational-mechanism gate; the contract's `score_improvement_mechanism_status` field encodes it at decoration time). 11 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_241_242_meta_layer_gates.py` covering decorator-passes / legacy-waiver-with-real-rationale-passes / no-decorator-no-waiver-flagged / placeholder-rejected / empty-rationale-rejected / strict-raises / strict-silent-on-clean / aggregates-multiple / string-repo-root / live-repo-warn-only-regression-guard / no-experiments-dir-empty. Memory: `feedback_meta_layer_substrate_contract_landed_20260515.md`. Cross-ref `.omx/research/substrate_meta_layer_design_20260515.md` (full design memo: 36-field schema + auto-wire one-way data flow + migration plan).

242. `check_register_substrate_contract_fields_canonical` — META-LAYER-SUBSTRATE-CONTRACT-AUTO-WIRE-SUBAGENT 2026-05-15. Companion to Catalog #241. Every file that uses `@register_substrate(...)` MUST register a contract that validates against the canonical 36-field `SubstrateContract` schema in `src/tac/substrate_registry/contract.py`. The check imports `tac.substrate_registry.example_template` (forces decorator execution) and re-runs `validate_all_registered()` over the in-memory `_REGISTERED_SUBSTRATES` dict. Defense-in-depth: the import-time validation is the primary surface (substrate fails to import on contract drift); this gate catches a registered-but-corrupted contract that may have escaped initial validation through an out-of-band mutation (e.g. `_REGISTERED_SUBSTRATES[id] = corrupted_object` test-only back door). The validator wraps both `SubstrateContractError` (field-level invariants) AND `TypeError` (missing-field / wrong-type construction errors) so any registry-mutation surface produces a string-formatted error. Validators enforce: id pattern `/^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/`; lane_id pattern `/^lane_[a-z0-9_]+_\d{8}$/`; target_modes non-empty + legal enum members; deployment_target legal; export_format legal; score_aware_loss legal; loc budgets > 0; runtime_dep_closure non-empty; OPERATIONAL ↔ runtime_overlay_consumed=True invariant (Catalog #220 mirror); SCAFFOLD with >1 KB byte addition refused (the research-substrate trap); recipe_smoke_only=True with cost_band_epochs > 100 refused (consistency invariant); recipe_canary_status=post_canary_dependent ↔ recipe_canary_dependency required; min_vram_gb >= 1; cost_band_epochs >= 1; hook_*=not_applicable_with_rationale REQUIRES non-empty rationale entry in hook_not_applicable_rationale; hook_probe_disambiguator=None requires rationale entry; hook_rationale dict keys restricted to legal hook field names. **STRICT-from-byte-one** per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (only `tac.substrate_registry.example_template` uses the decorator and its contract is canonically clean). 5 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_241_242_meta_layer_gates.py` covering clean-registry-empty / strict-silent-on-clean / live-repo-zero-violations-regression-guard / strict-raises-when-corrupted / orchestrator-wires-strict-true. Sister of Catalog #241 (decorator-or-legacy-tagged adoption gate; #242 is the per-contract field-validation gate) + Catalog #176 (`check_strict_preflight_callsites_have_claude_md_catalog_row` — the META-meta gate that this entry satisfies for #242's strict-callsite). Memory: `feedback_meta_layer_substrate_contract_landed_20260515.md`. Cross-ref `.omx/research/substrate_meta_layer_design_20260515.md` § 5.3 (the contract validation invariants enumeration).

244. `check_remote_lane_scripts_carry_canonical_nvml_block` — META-CONSOLIDATION-CRITICAL-PLUS-POC self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_*.sh` files that lack the canonical Modal/CUDA env hygiene block — 3 required exports: `DALI_DISABLE_NVML=1` + `CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=:4096:8` + `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True`. Bug class anchor: 2026-05-15 D1 dispatch (`substrate_d1_segnet_margin_polytope_modal_t4_dispatch_..._smoke__50ep`) crashed at `nvml error (999): A nvml internal driver error occurred` inside DALI `fn.experimental.inputs.video` pipeline because the lane script did not export `DALI_DISABLE_NVML=1` before DALI imported NVML. Sister lanes D4/Z3/Z4/Z5 had the 3 exports; D1 was the missing wire-in (commit `611495f26` patched D1 directly). 6th occurrence of the same NVML 999 bug class on Modal workers in the 24h preceding the gate landing. Per the standing operator directive *"all possible should be pulled into the decorator or similar reusable and shareable tools and helpers"*: companion runtime fix in `tac.substrate_registry.driver_generator.generate_driver_shell` AUTO-EMITS the 3 exports immediately after `set -euo pipefail`, sourced from canonical constants `CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG_VALUE` + `DALI_DISABLE_NVML_VALUE` + `PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF_VALUE` in `tac.deploy.modal.runtime`. New substrate drivers regenerated through the META layer cannot ship without the block. Acceptance per substrate driver: (a) all 3 required tokens appear in the file body, OR (b) same-file `# CANONICAL_NVML_BLOCK_OK:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<reason>` / `<rationale>` literals rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Scope: only `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_*.sh` (other `remote_lane_*` prefixes out of scope). **STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-15** by CATALOG-244-BACKFILL-31-DRIVERS subagent (per `feedback_catalog_244_backfill_31_drivers_landed_20260515.md`) after all 31 legacy substrate drivers were backfilled with the canonical 3-export block in the same commit batch per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule". **Live count at strict-flip: 0** (all 36 substrate drivers carry the block — 5 sister-owned canonical references D1/D4/Z3/Z4/Z5 + 31 backfilled). Each backfilled driver received the 3-export block IMMEDIATELY after `set -euo pipefail` with citation header referencing commit `611495f26` + canonical constants in `tac.deploy.modal.runtime` + the standing directive from `feedback_consolidate_everything_into_meta_layer_or_canonical_helpers_standing_directive_20260515.md`. All 31 backfilled drivers passed `bash -n` syntax verification. Sister of Catalog #163 (`check_remote_lane_script_uses_sentinel_when_sourcing_bootstrap` — bootstrap source-time sentinel) + Catalog #224 (`check_modal_training_image_includes_hard_runtime_deps` — Modal image env block). Together the three close the Modal NVML 999 bug class at THREE surfaces: source-time bootstrap sentinel (#163) + Modal image runtime env (#224) + remote lane driver script env (#244). 19 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_244_remote_lane_canonical_nvml_block.py` covering positive (script missing all 3 exports / missing nvml only / missing cublas only / missing alloc only / multi-file aggregation), negative (canonical block accepted / late-position exports accepted), waiver (rationale accepted / `<reason>` placeholder rejected / `<rationale>` placeholder rejected / bare-no-rationale rejected), scope (only substrate glob / non-substrate excluded), edge cases (no scripts dir / empty scripts dir / Path repo_root accepted), strict-mode (raises with PreflightError citing Catalog #244 + commit 611495f26 + DALI_DISABLE_NVML / silent on clean), live-repo regression guard (count <= total substrate scripts), and orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard. Plus 4 sister tests in `src/tac/tests/test_driver_generator.py` covering the auto-emit producer side (block present / cited canonical anchor / appears BEFORE bootstrap source / uses canonical constants from tac.deploy.modal.runtime). Memory: `feedback_meta_consolidation_critical_plus_poc_landed_20260515.md`. Cross-ref the standing directive `feedback_consolidate_everything_into_meta_layer_or_canonical_helpers_standing_directive_20260515.md` op-routable #1 (driver_generator NVML wire-in) + `feedback_d1_modal_dispatch_council_omnibus_pri_2_landed_20260515.md` (the D1 incident anchor) + commit `611495f26` (the per-lane fix that this gate makes structural). Lane: `lane_meta_layer_substrate_contract_auto_wire_20260515` (extends the META layer's `lane_id` per Catalog #126 pre-registration discipline).

243. `check_dispatch_wrappers_invoke_local_pre_deploy_check_first` — WIRE-AND-INTEGRATE-ALL self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_wire_and_integrate_all_cross_stack_landed_20260515.md`). Refuses any dispatch wrapper (a `.py` or `.sh` file under `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, or `src/tac/` containing one of `_CHECK_152_DISPATCH_TOKENS`: `modal run`, `modal.run`, `modal_train_lane`, `launch_lane_on_vastai`, `launch_lane_lightning`, `vastai create instance`, `lightning run`, `lightning.ai run`, `kaggle kernels push`) that BYPASSES the canonical `tools/operator_authorize.py` (which now wires `_run_local_pre_deploy_check` per WIRE-AND-INTEGRATE-ALL) AND does NOT invoke `tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py` directly. Bug class anchor: 2026-05-15 Z3 v2 smoke `fc-01KRNHEGC9ZE48Y68GGJHP7FXN` ($2 wasted) + Z4 smoke `fc-01KRNHE942JSV7VRGXGR1FJGHQ` ($2 wasted) both crashed on bug classes the local 30s harness would have caught (Z3 v2: archive ZIP member name `'x'` vs canonical `'0.bin'`; Z4: trainer didn't reach auth_eval stage). Operator directive 2026-05-15: *"shouldn't you run python compile locally before deploy to find those bugs"*. Companion runtime fix landed `_run_local_pre_deploy_check` in `tools/operator_authorize.py` between Catalog #152's `_validate_required_input_files` and `_native_dispatch_preflight` — every operator-authorize dispatch now runs the 30s harness BEFORE the GPU meter starts. Bypass via paired-env discipline: `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_SKIP_LOCAL_PRE_DEPLOY_CHECK=1` REQUIRES paired `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_LOCAL_PRE_DEPLOY_BYPASS_REASON=<text>` per CLAUDE.md "Comment-only contracts are FORBIDDEN" pattern + Catalog #199 paired-env discipline; bare-skip raises SystemExit. Acceptance: (a) wrapper invokes `tools/operator_authorize.py` (canonical routing); (b) wrapper invokes `tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py` directly (manual --strict gate); (c) same-line `# LOCAL_PRE_DEPLOY_CHECK_BYPASS_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the dispatch-token line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected). Self-exempt: canonical helper + canonical wrapper + preflight.py self-references. Test files excluded; `experiments/results/` + `_intake_` + `.omx/oss_export/` excluded. Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — 56 historical wrappers exist (many are documentation/diagnostic tools that document dispatch one-liners but do not actually dispatch); strict-flip pending audit + waiver backfill. Sister of Catalog #152 (`check_operator_wrapper_validates_required_input_files_pre_dispatch` — same canonical insertion point, complementary bug class) + Catalog #167 (`check_substrate_dispatch_uses_smoke_before_full_pattern` — same dispatch-wrapper canonical-routing META) + Catalog #199 (`check_operator_authorize_bypass_requires_session_budget` — paired-env discipline sister). 24 dedicated tests across `src/tac/tests/test_check_243_dispatch_wrappers_local_pre_deploy.py` (15 tests covering clean repo / dispatch-token-without-routing flagged / canonical-routing accepted / direct-harness-call accepted / same-line waiver / placeholder rejection / canonical-helper self-exempt / canonical-wrapper self-exempt / test files excluded / intake clones excluded / strict raises with Catalog #243 message / strict silent on clean / unrelated files not flagged / live-repo regression guard bound at 200 / orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard) and `src/tac/tests/test_operator_authorize_local_pre_deploy_wire_in.py` (9 tests covering helper exists / invoked in main / invoked after Catalog #152 / invoked before _native_dispatch_preflight / passes --strict flag / paired-env bypass discipline / SystemExit on bare skip without reason / silent on paired bypass / missing harness warns does not crash / live Z3 trainer passes harness). Memory: `feedback_wire_and_integrate_all_cross_stack_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_wire_and_integrate_all_cross_stack_20260515`.

246. `check_paired_dispatch_uses_anchor_skip_flag` - PAIRED-DISPATCH-SKIP-IF-ANCHOR-EXISTS-ENHANCEMENT self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses callers of `tools/dispatch_modal_paired_auth_eval.py` under `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, or `src/tac/` that omit `--skip-axis-if-promotable-anchor-exists` and lack `# PAIRED_DISPATCH_FORCE_REFIRE_OK:<rationale>`. Empirical anchor: a 2026-05-15 Z3 v2 FULL paired dispatch re-fired CUDA on Modal T4 even though a contest-CUDA anchor already existed for the same archive SHA, wasting spend and creating duplicate custody. Companion helper: `tac.deploy.modal.anchor_lookup.find_promotable_anchor_for_axis_and_sha(axis, archive_sha256, repo_root)` checks the Modal call-id ledger first, then canonical filesystem anchor JSONs, and requires axis-matching evidence grade, explicit score claim, exact archive SHA, and finite numeric score. Fail-safe semantics: unclear custody returns `None` and dispatches fresh. CLI plan schema is `modal_paired_auth_eval_dispatch_plan_v2` with `skip_axis_if_promotable_anchor_exists`, `axes_skipped_due_to_existing_anchor`, and `existing_anchors_reused`; execute mode writes `anchor_repointer_<axis>.json` for reused axes and exits cleanly when both axes already have anchors. Warn-only at landing; live count at landing is 0. Tests: 26 lookup/CLI tests in `src/tac/tests/test_paired_dispatch_anchor_lookup.py` and 5 preflight-gate tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_246_paired_dispatch_anchor_skip_flag.py`. Sister of Catalog #167, #226, and #245. Memory: `feedback_paired_dispatch_skip_if_anchor_exists_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_paired_dispatch_skip_if_anchor_exists_enhancement_20260515`.

248. `check_no_stash_pop_conflict_markers_in_canonical_files` — GRAND-COUNCIL-MULTI-SISTER-MERGE-RESOLUTION self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_grand_council_multi_sister_merge_resolution_landed_20260515.md` + operator NON-NEGOTIABLE directive *"need grand council subagent with full research authority to deeply research and resolve all by hand / merge all into main"*). Refuses any state of the repo that contains residual git conflict markers (`<<<<<<< Updated upstream` / `<<<<<<< HEAD` / `<<<<<<< ours` / `>>>>>>> Stashed changes` / `>>>>>>> theirs` / generic `>>>>>>> <branch>` / `<<<<<<< <branch>`) in canonical source files under `src/`, `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, `submissions/`, `tests/`, `configs/`, `docs/`, `reports/`, `.omx/`, `.ralph/`, `.agents/`, `runtime-rs/`, `cuda/`, `jax/`, `mojo/` with extensions `.py`, `.sh`, `.json`, `.jsonl`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.md`, `.txt`, `.cfg`, `.toml`, `.rs`, `.cu`, `.cuh`. Excluded path markers: `experiments/results/`, `_intake_`, `.omx/oss_export/`, `vendored/`, `build/lib/`, `reports/raw/`, `.venv/`, `__pycache__/`, `node_modules/`, `.git/`, `upstream/` (per CLAUDE.md mutation frontier non-negotiable). Self-exempt: `src/tac/preflight.py` (carries the marker literals in its own gate definition) and `src/tac/tests/test_check_248_no_stash_pop_conflict_markers.py` (fixtures contain verbatim violation samples). Bug class anchor: 2026-05-15 multi-sister stash-pop race produced 79 UU files (65 trivially-clean, 14 with residual conflict markers) across `src/tac/`, `experiments/`, `submissions/`, `tools/`, `reports/` because 3+ parallel sister subagents (PAIRED-DISPATCH-SKIP / ORPHAN-SIGNAL-AUDIT / 8-AXIS-SWEEP) all stashed-and-popped concurrently. Conflict markers are syntactically invalid in EVERY supported file type (Python ImportError, shell parse-fail, JSON/YAML schema-fail, Markdown rendering-fail) and slipping even one into main is a hard rebuild-required incident. Acceptance: same-line `# CONFLICT_MARKER_INTENTIONAL_OK:<rationale>` waiver for the rare case where a file legitimately documents conflict-marker text (e.g. this file's own catalog block, a tutorial, a test fixture); placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive. Sister of Catalog #117 (`check_subagent_commit_serializer_uses_lock` — last-50-commit serializer-usage gate), Catalog #157 (`check_commit_serializer_pre_lock_hash_against_head` — commit-swap pre-pre-lock race detector), Catalog #174 (`check_subagent_commit_serializer_always_uses_expected_content_sha256` — mandates `--expected-content-sha256` so sibling-edit detection is symmetric), and Catalog #230 (`check_bulk_rewrite_respects_sister_subagent_ownership_map` — extincts the editor-vs-editor collision class at the bulk-rewrite surface; #248 extincts the residual artifact at the file-content surface). Together the four close the multi-sister stash-pop race bug-class meta-surface: edit-time (#230) + commit-time-pre-pre-lock (#157) + commit-time-lock-arbitration (#117 + #174) + post-resolution-residual-marker (#248). 245. `check_modal_dispatches_register_call_id` — MODAL-CALL-ID-LEDGER-CANONICAL-HELPER self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_modal_call_id_ledger_canonical_landed_20260515.md` + operator directive *"perhaps canonicalize and record the call_id in a log or archive or in review_tracker duckdb or similar"*). Refuses any source-code site under `experiments/`, `tools/`, `scripts/`, `src/tac/` that invokes `fn.spawn(...)` (or recognized aliases `function.spawn` / `run_lane_training_<gpu>.spawn` / `modal_fn.spawn` / `modal_func.spawn`) on a Modal `Function` object WITHOUT one of: (a) a reference to `register_dispatched_call_id` (or sister tokens `from tac.deploy.modal.call_id_ledger import` / `tac.deploy.modal.call_id_ledger` / `MODAL_CALL_ID_LEDGER_PATH`) in the ±100-line window around the spawn invocation; (b) a same-line `# CALL_ID_LEDGER_REGISTRATION_OK:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Bug class anchor: pre-Catalog #245, Modal call_ids were captured at SCATTERED surfaces — `experiments/results/lane_*_modal/modal_call_id.txt` (per-dispatch text) + `experiments/results/lane_*_modal/modal_metadata.json` (per-dispatch JSON) + `.omx/state/active_lane_dispatch_claims.md` (markdown ledger w/ embedded ids) + `tools/harvest_modal_calls.py` (crawler that re-discovers them). There was NO single source-of-truth for "show me every call_id we have ever dispatched in chronological order, including outcome". Per CLAUDE.md "Modal `.spawn()` HARVEST OR LOSE" non-negotiable: every dispatch via `modal_train_lane.py` MUST be followed by a scheduled harvest within 24h; the harvester relied on per-dispatch metadata files whose discovery cost is O(N_dispatches) `glob` calls — a fragile primary index for an audit-grade ledger. Companion runtime fix landed `src/tac/deploy/modal/call_id_ledger.py` (~600 LOC mirroring the canonical `tac.deploy.lightning.active_jobs_state.py` pattern: fcntl-locked JSONL append-only at `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger.jsonl`; transactional `.tmp.<uuid12>` + `os.replace`; strict-load helper raises `CallIdLedgerCorruptError`; quarantines `.corrupt.<utc>` on JSON parse failure; APPEND-ONLY per CLAUDE.md HISTORICAL_PROVENANCE Catalog #110/#113/#132 — outcomes are NEW rows referencing the same `call_id`). Public API: `register_dispatched_call_id(call_id, lane_id, label, ...)` / `update_call_id_outcome(call_id, status, ...)` / `query_by_call_id` / `query_by_lane` / `query_unharvested(older_than_seconds=N)` / `query_all_post_utc(utc_str)` / `latest_status_by_call_id` / `load_call_ids_strict`. Schema: 5 event_types (`dispatched` / `harvested` / `failed` / `stale` / `manually_terminated`), 25-field row including `call_id` / `lane_id` / `label` / `platform` / `gpu` / `expected_cost_usd` / `expected_axis` / `recipe` / `dispatched_at_utc` / `harvested_at_utc` / `status` / `rc` / `elapsed_seconds` / `cost_actual_usd` / `score` / `score_axis` / `archive_sha256` / `archive_bytes` / `evidence_grade` / `max_seconds` / `mounted_code_git_head` / `agent` / `subagent_id` / `session_id` / `written_at_utc` / `written_pid` / `written_host`. Producer wire-ins (same commit batch): `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` (registers immediately after fn.spawn() returns + sentinel files write) + `experiments/modal_component_sensitivity_shards.py` (registers per-shard call_id). Consumer wire-in: `tools/backfill_modal_call_id_ledger.py` (one-time historical backfill: emitted 114 dispatched + 107 outcome rows from the historical `experiments/results/lane_*_modal/modal_metadata.json` + `harvest_summary.json` corpus). `MODAL_CALL_ID_LEDGER_PATH` registered in Catalog #131 (`check_no_bare_writes_to_shared_state`) `_SHARED_STATE_PATH_MARKERS` so direct writes outside the canonical helper are refused; `_ledger_lock` registered in `_BARE_WRITE_LOCK_TOKENS`; `register_dispatched_call_id` / `update_call_id_outcome` / `load_call_ids_strict` / `_append_event_locked` registered in `_BARE_WRITE_CANONICAL_HELPER_CALL_TOKENS`; the canonical helper file itself registered in `_BARE_WRITE_CANONICAL_HELPERS` exclusion list. Self-exempt: `src/tac/deploy/modal/call_id_ledger.py` (canonical helper), `src/tac/deploy/modal/auth_eval.py` (provides `function_call_id` helper), `tools/backfill_modal_call_id_ledger.py` (historical backfill helper), `src/tac/preflight.py` (gate's own pattern literals); test files + `experiments/results/` + `_intake_` + `.omx/oss_export/` + `build/lib/` excluded. **STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-15** at landing per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (the only two existing producers landed the canonical wire-in in the same commit batch). 34 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_modal_call_id_ledger.py` covering schema validation (4 tests: schema_version pinned + valid_event_types canonical set + valid_statuses == event_types + terminal_statuses subset), register_dispatched_call_id happy path + validation (8 tests: writes row + persists to disk + rejects empty call_id/lane_id/label + rejects newline in id + extra kwargs attached + extra cannot overwrite reserved), update_call_id_outcome (4 tests: appends new row + rejects invalid status + event_type overrides status + does NOT mutate dispatched row per CLAUDE.md HISTORICAL_PROVENANCE), load_call_ids lenient (3 tests: empty when missing + skips malformed lines + skips non-dict rows), load_call_ids_strict per Catalog #138 (4 tests: empty when missing + raises on malformed JSON + raises on non-dict root + clean file returns rows), quarantine on corrupt (1 test: quarantines and refuses), atomic write (1 test: no .tmp leakage), query helpers (5 tests: by_call_id chronological + by_lane returns all + post_utc filters + unharvested only dispatched + age filter), latest_status_by_call_id (1 test), 4-process spawn pool concurrency stress (1 test: 20 rows survive across 4 procs × 5 rows each), full call_id lifecycle (1 test: dispatch → harvest+failure+stale across 3 distinct call_ids), JSONL byte-stable format with sort_keys=True (1 test). Backfill executed in same commit batch: `tools/backfill_modal_call_id_ledger.py --execute` emitted 114 dispatched + 107 outcome rows from the historical `experiments/results/lane_*_modal/modal_metadata.json` corpus to `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger.jsonl` (221 total events). Sister of Catalog #131 (`check_no_bare_writes_to_shared_state` — META gate that this gate's path is registered into) + Catalog #138 (`check_state_writers_strict_load_for_mutating_path` — strict-load discipline; #245 mirrors via `load_call_ids_strict` + `CallIdLedgerCorruptError`) + Catalog #128 (`check_continual_learning_writes_use_lock` — sister fcntl-locked JSONL writer) + Catalog #143 (`check_paid_job_register_before_submit` — sister "register before paid network call" pattern). Together they extinct the "Modal call_id orphaned at the per-dispatch sentinel layer" bug class structurally. Memory: `feedback_modal_call_id_ledger_canonical_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_modal_call_id_ledger_canonical_helper_20260515`.

249. `check_no_misleading_device_named_output_directories` — phantom-score directory class permanent fix 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_misleading_directory_name_phantom_score_class_permanent_fix_landed_20260515.md`). Refuses any caller under `src/tac/`, `tools/`, `experiments/`, `scripts/` that constructs an auth-eval output JSON path or work directory whose filename device-token (`_cuda` / `_cpu` / `_mps`) is HARDCODED instead of derived from the actual auth-eval device. Empirical anchor: Z3 v2 FULL Modal A100 dispatch 2026-05-15T11:41:15Z. The trainer hardcoded `result_json_path = out_dir / "contest_auth_eval_cuda.json"` regardless of `auth_eval_device`. The Modal dispatcher injected `AUTH_EVAL_DEVICE=cpu` + `MODAL_AUTH_EVAL_ADVISORY_ONLY=1`. Result: `contest_auth_eval_cuda.json` containing `device=cpu` + `score_axis=diagnostic_cpu`, plus `contest_auth_eval_cuda_work/` directory containing CPU-evaluated artifacts. Parent agent quoted "0.19869 [contest-CUDA T4]" from the FILENAME despite the metadata saying CPU. Paired re-eval revealed true CUDA = 0.2317 — the originally-claimed CUDA score did not exist. This is the "phantom-score directory" anti-pattern: an artifact whose FILENAME makes a claim its CONTENT cannot honor. Per CLAUDE.md "Apples-to-apples evidence discipline" + "Forbidden component-aliasing for baselines" non-negotiables, the filename MUST match the device that produced the contents OR be device-agnostic. Bug-class spread (META-meta 6-7x per CLAUDE.md): 23 substrate trainers + 4 deployment files + canonical helper all hardcoded `_cuda.json`. The canonical helper `gate_auth_eval_call` derives `work_dir` from `output_json.stem`, so a single misleading filename poisoned the work-dir name too. Companion runtime fix in `src/tac/substrates/_shared/smoke_auth_eval_gate.py` adds `_redirect_output_json_to_match_device` which auto-rewrites mismatched filenames at runtime + emits a loud `[phantom-score-fix]` warning so the upstream caller can be migrated. The runtime auto-redirect extincts the actual lie surface immediately; this STRICT preflight gate is the structural sister that refuses NEW hardcoded device tokens in source code so future trainers don't regrow the bug class at a different surface. Detection is AST-aware (string-literal targets only with auth-eval context tokens like `contest_auth_eval` / `auth_eval_result` / `auth_eval_json` / `auth_eval_path` AND `_<device>.json` / `_<device>_work` / `_<device>` suffix). Same-line waiver `# DEVICE_NAMED_DIR_OK:<rationale>` accepts the rare deliberate case (e.g., a CUDA-only benchmark fixture); placeholder `<rationale>` literal rejected. Self-exempt: canonical helper file + this gate's defining file. Path markers excluded: `experiments/results/` (DERIVED_OUTPUT per Catalog #113) + `_intake_` (Catalog #109) + `.omx/oss_export/` + `vendored` + `/tests/` + `test_*` files + `build/lib/` + `reports/raw/`. Sister of Catalog #127 (`check_authoritative_tag_requires_custody_metadata` — per-call-site custody routing) + Catalog #221 (`check_auth_eval_result_artifacts_fail_closed_for_score_claims` — result-artifact fail-closed) + Catalog #226 (`check_trainer_auth_eval_uses_canonical_helper` — caller-must-route-through-canonical-helper). Together they extinct the phantom-score class at FOUR surfaces: per-call-site custody routing (#127) + result-artifact fail-closed (#221) + caller-must-route-through-canonical-helper (#226) + filename-must-match-device (#249). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" because 23 substrate trainers + 4 deploy files currently hardcode `contest_auth_eval_cuda.json` — strict-flip planned once a sister wave drives caller-side hardcoded filenames to 0. Live count at landing: 32 hardcoded device-named auth-eval paths across ~27 files. Companion FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS entry "Forbidden misleading-directory-name (the phantom-score directory trap)" lands in same commit batch. 22 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_249_misleading_device_named_output_dirs.py` covering runtime auto-redirect (preserve agnostic / preserve matched cuda+cpu / rewrite cuda→cpu Z3 anchor / rewrite cpu→cuda / handle mps token / handle unrelated filenames) + STRICT preflight gate (clean repo / flag hardcoded cuda literal in substrate / accept same-line waiver with rationale / reject placeholder waiver / exclude test files / exclude results artifacts / exclude intake clones / strict-mode raises with Catalog #249 + phantom-score message / strict-mode silent on clean / aggregate multi-file violations / recognize cpu reverse lie / recognize work_dir suffix / self-exempt canonical helper / live-repo bounded baseline / orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard). Memory: `feedback_misleading_directory_name_phantom_score_class_permanent_fix_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_misleading_directory_name_phantom_score_class_permanent_fix_20260515`. <!-- HISTORICAL_SCORE_LITERAL_OK:z3_v2_phantom_filename_score_class_anchor_2026-05-15_catalog_249_landing -->

250. `check_slim_ranker_consumes_canonical_taylor_proxies` — RUDIN-DAUBECHIES Phase 1 self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_rudin_daubechies_autopilot_full_implementation_landed_20260515.md`). 251. `check_falling_rule_list_canonical_use` — RUDIN-DAUBECHIES Phase 2 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any source file mentioning a non-falling rule-list pattern token (`ascending_rule_list`, `rising_rule_list`, `rules.append.*sort.*reverse=False`) UNLESS it carries a same-line `# NON_FALLING_RULE_LIST_OK:<rationale>` waiver. Bug class: ascending or rising rule lists defeat Rudin & Wang's "Falling Rule Lists" canonical discipline where higher-priority rules MUST come first (first-match-wins semantics break otherwise). 252. `check_rashomon_ensemble_continual_update_locked` — RUDIN-DAUBECHIES Phase 3 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any source file under `src/tac/`, `tools/`, or `experiments/` (excluding the canonical helper + tests) that constructs `RashomonEnsembleRanker(...).update_all(..., store_path=None)` UNLESS it carries a same-line `# RASHOMON_NON_PERSISTED_OK:<rationale>` waiver. Bug class: dense-anchor landscape reconstruction defeats Daubechies' canonical compressive-sensing discipline (Candes-Romberg-Tao 2006 + Daubechies-DeVore-Fornasier-Gunturk 2010) which recovers a sparse signal from FEW measurements via L1 reconstruction. The fine-rule-overrides-coarse-gate antipattern specifically inverts the Daubechies "coarsest dominates on disagreement" rule. Bug class anchor: codex review bkrbqet3p HIGH F1 caught that the G1 trainer's full-main path built the Z3HV2 archive with empty hyperprior + class-index slots (sigma table + class indices written to `g1_diagnostic.pt` OUTSIDE the archive) AND then ran `_canon_gate_auth_eval_call` against the archive, producing authoritative-looking `[contest-CUDA]` artifacts attributed to a substrate the runtime never consumed — auth eval was actually measuring a direct-residual Z3HV2 control packet. 268. `check_theoretical_floor_rate_term_unit_calibrated` — Codex review bkrbqet3p F3 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any state of `src/tac/symposium_impls/blahut_arimoto_theoretical_floor.py` that produces a `contest_score_floor` from `25.0 * r_combined` (or equivalent `25.0 * <bits-per-unit>` patterns) WITHOUT either: (1) the canonical bits-to-contest-rate converter `bits_per_unit_to_contest_rate_term(...)` AND `CONTEST_RATE_DENOM_BYTES` constant (= 37,545,489), OR (2) the explicit advisory tag `theoretical_floor_units_calibrated=false` in the same module so downstream consumers know the `contest_score_floor` field is dimensionally invalid until calibration lands. Bug class anchor: codex review bkrbqet3p HIGH F3 caught that `compute_contest_theoretical_floor` adds `25.0 * r_combined` directly to the contest score where `r_combined` is bits-per-unit while the contest rate term is normalized archive bytes (`25 * archive_bytes / 37,545,489`); the returned `contest_score_floor` is dimensionally invalid; for realistic distortions it can be orders of magnitude wrong while presenting as a Pareto lower-bound anchor consumed by autopilot ranking. File-level `# THEORETICAL_FLOOR_UNITS_OK:<rationale>` waiver in first 30 lines accepts the rare deliberate placeholder case. Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY because `src/tac/symposium_impls/` is sister-owned (subagent `a338c146` mid-flight at landing); the Phase 2 rebase will land the calibration. Strict-flip pending sister subagent completion + this gate's live count = 0. 6 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_266_to_269_codex_bkrbqet3p_fix_wave.py` covering uncalibrated-rate-term flagged, canonical calibration accepted, advisory tag accepted, target absent silent skip, strict raise, file-level waiver. Sister of Catalog #269 (paired F4 scorer-conditional-entropy gate).

269. `check_scorer_conditional_entropy_actually_uses_scorer_state` — Codex review bkrbqet3p F4 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any state of `src/tac/symposium_impls/mackay_conditional_entropy_a1_archive.py` whose `scorer_prior_context` / `scorer_conditional_entropy` helpers partition bytes WITHOUT either: (1) reading actual scorer state — at least one of `load_segnet_state(`, `load_posenet_state(`, `load_differentiable_scorers(`, `load_default_scorers(`, `load_scorers(`, `segnet_class_features(`, `posenet_pose_features(`, `from tac.differentiable_eval_roundtrip import`, or `torch.load(` must appear as a CALL signature in the module body, OR (2) the explicit self-disclosure `true_scorer_conditional_entropy_claim=false` in the module so downstream Pareto / autopilot consumers know the value is a position-partition proxy NOT a true `H(A1_archive | scorer_state_dict)` conditional entropy. Bug class anchor: codex review bkrbqet3p MEDIUM F4 caught that `_scorer_prior_buckets` partitions payload bytes by hashing byte position only — never reads scorer weights, tensor shapes, classes, or any scorer-derived feature — while presenting the result as `H(A1_archive | scorer_state_dict)` and writing a reusable lower-bound estimate consumed by autopilot ranking. Token-set is intentionally call-signature only (e.g. `load_default_scorers(` not `load_default_scorers`) so docstring mentions of `scorer_state_dict` do NOT count as feature binding — the live MacKay file claims `H(X | scorer_state_dict)` in the module docstring while never reading scorer state. File-level `# SCORER_CONDITIONAL_ENTROPY_OK:<rationale>` waiver in first 30 lines accepts the rare deliberate stub case. Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY because `src/tac/symposium_impls/` is sister-owned (subagent `a338c146` mid-flight at landing); the Phase 2 rebase will land disclosure tag or feature binding. Strict-flip pending sister subagent completion + this gate's live count = 0. 7 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_266_to_269_codex_bkrbqet3p_fix_wave.py` covering position-partition-without-disclosure flagged, real-scorer-feature-binding accepted, self-disclosure accepted, target absent silent skip, strict raise, file-level waiver, docstring-mention-alone-does-NOT-satisfy. Sister of Catalog #268 (paired F3 theoretical-floor-units gate).

271. `check_dispatch_runs_codex_adversarial_review_for_paid_dispatch` — PRE-DISPATCH-CODEX-REVIEW-AUTOMATION self-protection 2026-05-15 (operator-approved 2026-05-15: wire codex adversarial-review automation into `tools/operator_authorize.py` BEFORE every paid Modal/Lightning/Vast.ai dispatch >$1 estimated cost). Bug class anchor: the codex review of Z3-G1 caught F1+F2 BEFORE FULL CUDA dispatched but AFTER $0.59 smoke spend — this gate moves the review BEFORE smoke. Refuses any dispatch wrapper (a `.py` or `.sh` file under `tools/`, `scripts/`, `experiments/`, or `src/tac/` containing a `_CHECK_152_DISPATCH_TOKENS` token: `modal run` / `modal_train_lane` / `launch_lane_on_vastai` / `launch_lane_lightning` / `vastai create instance` / `lightning run` / `lightning.ai run` / `kaggle kernels push`) that does NOT route through the canonical `tools/operator_authorize.py` (which now wires `_run_codex_pre_dispatch_review` per Catalog #271) AND does NOT invoke `tools/run_codex_review_for_dispatch.py` directly. Acceptance: (a) wrapper invokes `tools/operator_authorize.py` (canonical routing); (b) wrapper invokes `tools/run_codex_review_for_dispatch.py` directly (e.g. as a manual gate before its dispatch); (c) same-line `# CODEX_PRE_DISPATCH_REVIEW_BYPASS_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the dispatch-token line (placeholder `<reason>` literal rejected so the docstring's example cannot self-waive). Companion runtime fix landed `tools/run_codex_review_for_dispatch.py` (~600 LOC canonical helper: cache schema = composite SHA-256 of `(git_HEAD_sha, recipe_sha, trainer_sha)` keyed JSONL append-only at `.omx/state/codex_pre_dispatch_review_cache.jsonl` with 1h TTL + fcntl-locked per Catalog #128/#131/#245 sister discipline; verdict taxonomy = `approve` / `advisory` / `needs-attention` / `no-ship` parsed from codex companion output via explicit `VERDICT:` markers + severity-token fallback `CRITICAL` → no-ship / `HIGH` → needs-attention / `LOW`/`MEDIUM` → advisory; cost gate = only invokes codex when `estimated_cost_usd > $1` to avoid burning codex tokens on cheap smokes; paired-env bypass `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CODEX_REVIEW_BYPASS_VERDICT=1` REQUIRES paired `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CODEX_REVIEW_BYPASS_RATIONALE=<text>` per Catalog #199 sister rule, bare intent raises `SystemExit(13)`). Producer wire-in: `tools/operator_authorize.py::_run_codex_pre_dispatch_review` invoked between `_run_local_pre_deploy_check` (Catalog #243) and `_native_dispatch_preflight`. Sister of Catalog #243 (local pre-deploy harness — same canonical insertion point) + Catalog #167 (smoke-before-full pattern — same dispatch-wrapper canonical-routing META) + Catalog #199 (paired-env operator bypass discipline — same paired-env pattern) + Catalog #245 (Modal call-id ledger — same fcntl-locked JSONL append-only store pattern). Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 88 (legacy dispatch wrappers may bypass operator_authorize.py for historical reasons; mirrors Catalog #243's warn-only baseline). Strict-flip pending audit + waiver backfill across the 88 surfaces. 49 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_271_pre_dispatch_codex_review.py` covering verdict parsing (15 tests: explicit markers / implicit severity / underscore form / 50-finding cap / 200-char per-finding cap / HIGHLIGHT-not-HIGH / explicit-overrides-implicit), cost gate (3 tests), cache (5 tests: miss / append+lookup roundtrip / TTL expiry / latest-row-wins / malformed-line tolerance), 4-process spawn-pool concurrent-append safety, paired-env bypass discipline (4 tests covering inactive / active / bare intent / blank rationale), codex companion availability (2 tests), end-to-end run_codex_review_for_dispatch (3 tests), STRICT preflight gate (10 tests: live-repo regression guard / dispatch-token-without-routing flagged / canonical routing accepted / direct helper invocation accepted / same-line waiver / placeholder rejection / strict-mode raises / strict silent on clean / test files exempt / canonical helper self-exempt), CLI subprocess (2 tests), Z3-G1 regression guard (2 tests confirming a needs-attention cached verdict blocks dispatch + paired-env bypass allows). Memory: `feedback_pre_dispatch_codex_review_automation_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_pre_dispatch_codex_review_automation_20260515`.

272. `check_substrate_distinguishing_feature_integration_contract` — DISTINGUISHING-FEATURE INTEGRATION CONTRACT 2026-05-15 (per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE *"the thing that makes them special and smart is not actually engineered or wired or integrated correctly"*). Bug class anchor: Z3-G1 trained a 1KB SegNet-class CDF (the "smart" distinguishing thing) but the archive's `hyperprior_weights_int8` slot is `b""` — the smart thing was engineered, never wired. Smoke score == Z3 v2 baseline to 5 decimals (empirical proof at fc-01KRPKCXARWP7NBGJCXB2P9QEP). This is the per-substrate-distinguishing-feature variant of Catalog #220 (operational mechanism) and #139 (no-op detector). Where #220 catches "lane added bytes but no operational mechanism declared" and #139 catches "some byte mutation changes output (generic)", #272 catches "the bytes claimed as DISTINGUISHING are actually consumed by inflate (specific)". Refuses substrate L2+ lanes in `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` that do NOT declare all 4 contract fields (`distinguishing_feature_name` / `distinguishing_bytes_path` / `inflate_consumer_function` / `byte_mutation_smoke_passes`). Acceptance cascade: (a) all 4 fields present + `byte_mutation_smoke_passes` evidence == True; (b) `research_only=true` (top-level field OR notes/evidence token); (c) `lane_class=substrate_engineering` (top-level field OR notes); (d) same-line waiver `# DISTINGUISHING_FEATURE_CONTRACT_OK:<rationale>` in lane notes / evidence (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literal rejected). Companion runtime helper `tools/verify_distinguishing_feature_byte_mutation.py` (~360 LOC) is the canonical proof generator: mutates one byte at every declared distinguishing offset, re-runs `inflate.sh` source-vs-mutated, compares output frames byte-for-byte, emits `experiments/results/<lane>/distinguishing_feature_byte_mutation_proof.json` with per-section verdict (PASSED / FAILED / INFRASTRUCTURE_ERROR; rc 0 / 1 / 2). Empty-section anchor: zero-byte distinguishing slots automatically FAIL the smoke (the canonical Z3-G1 anchor pattern: `hyperprior_weights_int8 = b""` cannot be mutated meaningfully, so the verifier marks the section FAIL). The 4 contract fields are mutable via canonical `tools/lane_maturity.py set-field --field distinguishing_feature_name --value '<name>'` (extends the existing fcntl-locked allowlist per Catalog #131 sister discipline). Wire-in: `preflight_all()` calls `check_substrate_distinguishing_feature_integration_contract(strict=False, verbose=verbose)` between Catalog #249 (phantom-score directory) and Catalog #266 (codex bkrbqet3p F1). Sister of Catalog #220 (L1+ scaffold operational mechanism) + Catalog #139 (packet compiler no-op detector) + Catalog #105 (no-op provenance) + Catalog #226 (auth_eval canonical helper) + Catalog #240 (recipe-vs-trainer chain) + Catalog #249 (phantom-score directory). Together they extinct the "research-substrate trap" 8th forbidden pattern across 7 surfaces: design-time archive grammar (#124) + L1+ operational mechanism (#220) + per-archive byte-mutation (#139) + no-op provenance (#105) + auth_eval routing (#226) + recipe-vs-trainer-chain (#240) + phantom-score-filename (#249) + per-substrate-distinguishing-feature (#272). Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 6 (`lane_a1_inflate_time_bias_correction_sweep`, `lane_a1_segnet_boundary_smoothing_inflate`, `lane_pr106_latent_sidecar_r2_pr101_grammar_contest_cpu`, `lane_b1_nerv_enc_dec_x_magic_codec_a1_20260512`, `lane_macos_cpu_substrate_canvas_sweep_20260513`, `lane_pr101_frame_exploit_selector_fec3_compact_exact_k8_cpu_overlay_20260514`). The 32-substrate-trainer audit at landing reveals these 6 unexempt L2+ substrate-tagged lanes; the other 12 L2+ substrate lanes are exempt via `lane_class=substrate_engineering` (4 lanes) or `research_only=true` (1 lane: Z3-G1). Strict-flip pending the operator-routed backfill sweep that closes every L2+ substrate lane via either contract declaration OR explicit research_only / substrate_engineering opt-out. 71 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_272_distinguishing_feature_contract.py` covering in-scope classifier (11 tests across substrate / nerv / pr106 / wavelet / yucr / non-substrate id-substrings), required-fields evaluator (10 tests covering full / missing-all-4 / partial / bool-True acceptance / dict-evidence acceptance / str-evidence-path / empty-list rejection / empty-string / None / bool-on-str-field), research-only / substrate-engineering classifiers (10 tests covering top-level bool / top-level str / notes-token / dash-form / negative cases), waiver semantics (5 tests covering rationale accepted / `<rationale>` placeholder rejected / `<reason>` placeholder rejected / empty / no-marker), end-to-end gate (24 tests covering no-registry / corrupt-registry / clean / L1-skipped / L0-skipped / non-substrate-skipped / flagged-without-contract / accepts-research-only / accepts-substrate-engineering / accepts-waiver / rejects-placeholder / multi-violation / strict-mode-raises / strict-silent-on-clean / message-includes-lane-id / message-includes-remediation / cites-Z3-G1-anchor / string-repo-root), substrate fixtures (4 tests for Z3-G1 research-only acceptance / Z3-G1 post-wire-in full contract / D1 substrate-engineering / pr106 violation), live-repo regression guard (≤ 14 ceiling) + orchestrator wire-in regression guard + lane_maturity allowlist regression guard + in-scope-substring parity-with-Catalog-#220 regression guard, acceptance-cascade-ordering (3 tests proving research-only / substrate-engineering / waiver short-circuit missing fields), and edge cases (5 tests for non-dict-lane / no-id / level-as-string / L3 also audited / verbose output). Plus 23 sister tests for the canonical helper at `src/tac/tests/test_verify_distinguishing_feature_byte_mutation.py` covering helper unit (sha256 / sections / mutations / hash-directory) + end-to-end PASSED / FAILED / Z3-G1-empty-section / multi-section mixed verdict + INFRA error (missing archive / missing inflate.sh / missing section) + JSON schema + CLI rc 0/1/2. Memory: `feedback_distinguishing_feature_integration_contract_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_distinguishing_feature_integration_contract_20260515`.

270. `check_dispatch_optimization_protocol_complete` — Canonical dispatch optimization protocol UMBRELLA gate 2026-05-15 (per CLAUDE.md "Production-hardened dispatch optimization protocol — NON-NEGOTIABLE, HIGHEST EMPHASIS" section + operator directive *"remember the multiple deployments that have failed over and over because of missing optimizations? we should investigate and develop a protocol for those too and enforce best practices and production hardened optimization, extreme optimization and correctness and performance and scalability"*). Routes through canonical helper `tools/canonical_dispatch_optimization_protocol.py::verify_dispatch_protocol_complete(trainer, recipe)` which computes `protocol_complete = AND(tier1_engineering, tier2_hardware, tier3_substrate)` and refuses any state with non-empty per-tier blockers. Empirical anchors driving the umbrella formula (the audit): D1 NVML 999 6× in 24h (Tier 2 missing canonical 3-export Modal/CUDA env block; closed by sister Catalog #244); Z3 v2 + Z4 paired $2 each waste from recipe-vs-trainer-state divergence (Tier 3; Catalog #240); Z3 v2 phantom CUDA score from hardcoded `_cuda` filename suffix (Tier 3; Catalog #249); C6 auth_eval rc=2 from hand-rolled CLI flags (Tier 3; Catalog #226); T1 Balle 23h timeout for missing autocast fp16 + TF32 + torch.compile (Tier 1; Catalog #172/#178/#179/#180); D4 T4 OOM from full-batch reconstruct_pair (Tier 1+3; Catalog #218 + #170). Sister gates: Catalog #170/#171/#172/#178/#179/#180/#181/#182/#215 (Tier 1/2 declared canonical) + Catalog #240 (recipe-vs-trainer-state) + Catalog #244 (NVML env block) + Catalog #226/#249 (auth-eval canonical helper). The umbrella conjunction means a single consult-call any future dispatcher / autopilot loop / operator can fire to decide whether the trainer + recipe + lane driver state is production-hardened enough to fire a paid dispatch. Wire-in: `tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py` 8th check `dispatch_optimization_protocol`; the operator-authorize 30s harness consults the protocol BEFORE paid dispatch via the existing `_run_local_pre_deploy_check` route. Same-line `# DISPATCH_OPTIMIZATION_PROTOCOL_OK:<rationale>` waiver in the trainer file's first 30 lines (placeholder rationales `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literal rejected so the docstring example cannot self-waive). Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 26 (of 32 scanned substrate trainers; typical gap is Tier 1 canonical scorer-loss helper routing OR Tier 2 recipe `min_smoke_gpu` undeclared). Strict-flip planned alongside the operator-routed Tier 1 backfill sweep that drives violations to 0 (substrate trainers route through canonical scorer-loss helper + declare engineering primitives in argparse + recipes declare full Tier 2 schema). 38 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_270_dispatch_optimization_protocol_complete.py` covering helper-unit (waiver detection / placeholder rejection / recipe resolution / scope rules), end-to-end protocol behavior (clean trainer accepts / missing trainer flagged / no recipe advisory / missing lane driver advisory / per-Tier-1 signal flagged for autocast/tf32/torch_compile/no_grad/canonical_scorer_loss / Tier 2 recipe missing min_vram_gb / Tier 2 driver missing NVML / Tier 3 recipe-vs-trainer-state divergence + research_only acceptance / Tier 3 reversed scorer-loader pattern / Tier 3 phantom-score filename / overall blocker aggregation / verdict serializable), preflight gate end-to-end (clean synth no violations / dirty has violations / strict raises / strict silent on clean / waiver with rationale skips / placeholder waiver rejected / no trainers dir empty / string repo_root / multi-trainer aggregated), live-repo regression guard (≤ 50 ceiling), orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard, and CLI smoke (JSON emit / strict rc=1). Memory: `feedback_canonical_dispatch_optimization_protocol_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_canonical_dispatch_optimization_protocol_20260515`.

**Codex review bklem3v5j WAVE 2 fix-with-self-protection (2026-05-15, STRICT @ 0):**

279. `check_local_pre_deploy_helper_import_failure_fails_closed` — Codex review bklem3v5j HIGH F1 self-protection 2026-05-15 (per `feedback_codex_fix_wave_2_dispatch_optimization_protocol_fail_open_landed_20260515.md`). Refuses any state of `tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py` where the 8th harness check `check_dispatch_optimization_protocol` returns `PASS-VACUOUS` / `True` on `ImportError` of the canonical Catalog #270 helper `tools/canonical_dispatch_optimization_protocol.py`. Bug class anchor: codex review bklem3v5j HIGH F1 caught that the prior `except ImportError as exc: return True, f"PASS-VACUOUS: protocol helper missing ({exc})"` branch silently bypassed the 8th gate when the canonical helper was missing or an untracked working-tree-only file. Because `tools/operator_authorize.py::_run_local_pre_deploy_check` uses the harness's exit code as its dispatch-gate, a vacuous-pass on missing helper would let paid Modal/Lightning/Vast.ai dispatch proceed with NO Tier 1/2/3 umbrella protection. Companion runtime fix changed the handler to `return False, ("FAIL: Catalog #270 protocol helper unavailable ...; refused per fail-closed-on-import discipline (Catalog #279)...")` so the harness exit code propagates to operator-authorize. The strict-mode CLI regression test (`test_strict_harness_exits_nonzero_when_helper_missing`) verifies — per codex's explicit recommendation — that `python tools/local_pre_deploy_check.py --strict` exits rc=1 when the helper is removed. File-level `# DISPATCH_PROTOCOL_IMPORT_FAILURE_ALLOW_VACUOUS_OK:<rationale>` waiver in the file's first 60 lines accepts the rare deliberate case (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literal rejected so the docstring example cannot self-waive). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — live count at landing: 0 (the F1 fix lands in the same commit batch). 12 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_279_local_pre_deploy_fail_closed.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (vacuous-pass regression flagged / missing required tokens flagged / missing function flagged), negative (canonical fail-closed pattern accepted), waiver semantics (rationale accepted / placeholder rejected), strict-mode raises with Catalog #279, edge cases (missing target file silent), orchestrator strict=True wire-in regression guard, AND the CLI subprocess regression test that verifies strict-harness-exits-nonzero per codex's explicit recommendation. Sister of Catalog #138 (`check_state_writers_strict_load_for_mutating_path` — fail-closed-on-corrupt-state pattern) + Catalog #245 (`check_modal_dispatches_register_call_id`) + Catalog #248 (`check_no_stash_pop_conflict_markers_in_canonical_files`) + Catalog #270 (the umbrella protocol gate this gate self-protects).

**Rudin-Daubechies preflight composite self-protection (2026-05-15, STRICT @ 0):**

Refuses any source file mentioning a non-falling rule-list pattern token (`preflight_ascending_rule_list`, `preflight_rising_rule_list`, `preflight_fine_rule_overrides_coarse_gate`) UNLESS it carries a same-line `# PREFLIGHT_NON_FALLING_RULE_OK:<rationale>` waiver. Bug class anchor: pre-fix lines 539-548 called `parse_verdict_from_codex_output(output)` FIRST then surfaced non-zero rc as a non-blocking advisory finding without changing the verdict. Companion runtime fix: (a) introduced `VERDICT_INVOCATION_ERROR = "invocation-error"` constant and added it to `VERDICTS_ALL`; (b) flipped the verdict-determination logic to check `if rc != 0:` BEFORE parsing text — nonzero rc promotes verdict to `invocation-error` and CLI returns exit code 2; (c) operator_authorize.py now translates rc=2 from the helper into its own `SystemExit` with explicit "diagnose codex companion failure" guidance, mirroring the existing rc=13 paired-env-bypass handler. Paired-env bypass per Catalog #199: `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CODEX_REVIEW_BYPASS_VERDICT=1` + `OPERATOR_AUTHORIZE_CODEX_REVIEW_BYPASS_RATIONALE=<text>` still applies. File-level waiver `# CODEX_REVIEW_RC_FIRST_VERDICT_WAIVED:<rationale>` (placeholder rejected). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0. 13 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_281_282_283_codex_pre_dispatch_fail_open.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (post-rc-classify-by-content forbidden token flagged / missing required tokens), waiver semantics (rationale accepted / placeholder rejected), strict raises with Catalog #281, runtime regression tests (companion timeout produces nonzero CLI exit / rc=2 produces nonzero CLI exit per codex's explicit recommendations). Sister of Catalog #279 (sister fail-closed META gate for the local pre-deploy harness — same fail-open class, different surface) + Catalog #270 (umbrella) + Catalog #271 (PRE-DISPATCH-CODEX-REVIEW-AUTOMATION self-protect — the helper this gate guards).

282. `check_codex_pre_dispatch_review_cache_key_includes_dirty_tree_fingerprint` — Codex review bfa2p1uex HIGH F2 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any state of `tools/run_codex_review_for_dispatch.py` where the cache-key tuple is missing `dirty_tree_fingerprint` or `untracked_relevant_fingerprint` fields, OR where the helper omits the `--no-cache-for-paid-dispatch` CLI flag, OR where the canonical helpers `_git_dirty_tree_fingerprint` / `_git_untracked_relevant_fingerprint` / `_UNTRACKED_RELEVANT_GLOBS` are absent. Bug class anchor: pre-fix the cache key was only `(git_HEAD_sha, recipe_sha, trainer_sha)` but the codex companion is invoked with `--scope working-tree` (`tools/run_codex_review_for_dispatch.py:416-417`) so dirty changes in shared helpers (`src/tac/preflight.py`, `tools/*.py`, etc.) and untracked dispatch-critical files materially change what the review evaluates — yet were INVISIBLE to the cache key. A cached approve from minutes ago could authorize a materially different working tree without rerunning review. Companion runtime fix: (a) extended `_compute_cache_key` to accept `dirty_tree_fingerprint` + `untracked_relevant_fingerprint` kwargs; (b) added `_git_dirty_tree_fingerprint(repo_root)` (sha256 over `git diff --binary --cached HEAD` + `git diff --binary`) and `_git_untracked_relevant_fingerprint(repo_root)` (sha256 over sorted untracked files matching `_UNTRACKED_RELEVANT_GLOBS = ("tools/*.py", "src/tac/*.py", "src/tac/preflight.py", "src/tac/substrates/**/*.py", "CLAUDE.md", "scripts/remote_lane_*.sh", "experiments/train_substrate_*.py")` with path + size + content); (c) added `--no-cache-for-paid-dispatch` CLI flag wired into `run_codex_review_for_dispatch(no_cache_for_paid_dispatch=...)`; (d) operator_authorize.py now passes `--no-cache-for-paid-dispatch` on every paid-dispatch invocation so a stale cached approve cannot authorize a now-different working tree. File-level waiver `# CODEX_REVIEW_CACHE_KEY_DIRTY_TREE_WAIVED:<rationale>` (placeholder rejected). STRICT-from-byte-one — live count at landing: 0. 11 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_281_282_283_codex_pre_dispatch_fail_open.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (missing dirty_tree_fingerprint flagged), waiver semantics (rationale accepted / placeholder rejected), strict raises with Catalog #282, runtime regression tests (dirty shared helper invalidates cached approve / untracked relevant file change invalidates cached approve / no_cache_for_paid_dispatch kwarg present per codex's explicit recommendations). Sister of Catalog #281 (sister F1 self-protect; same codex review bfa2p1uex; same canonical helper) + Catalog #271.
