289. `check_serializer_log_no_dropped_expected_content_sha_retry_pattern` — OMNIBUS-BUG-CLASS-AUDIT GAP-5 commit-serializer drop-flag-and-retry detection 2026-05-15 (per WAVE-D `feedback_commit_swap_incident_2c957c31e_forensic_analysis_landed_20260515.md` recommendation + OMNIBUS op-routable #3). META-meta-meta sister of Catalog #117 (last-50-commit serializer usage), #157 (pre-pre-lock hash), #174 (--expected-content-sha256 mandatory), #216 (post-stage hash). Bug class anchor: even with #117 + #157 + #174, the AGENT-RESPONSE failure mode remained — when the serializer refuses with rc=4 (mismatch), the agent could DROP the `--expected-content-sha256` flag on retry and silently absorb the sister's edits under the wrong commit body. The 2c957c31e WAVE-D incident proved this anti-pattern in the wild. Scans `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` JSONL for the signature: same `message_head` has ≥1 `outcome=expected_content_sha_mismatch` event followed by an `outcome=committed` event WHERE the new GAP-5 log field `expected_content_sha256_present=False` AND `nfiles >= 3` within ~10-min window. Companion runtime hardening in `tools/subagent_commit_serializer.py`: (a) every event now logs `expected_content_sha256_present` boolean + `expected_content_sha256_file_count` integer so the gate has structured evidence; (b) high-risk files (`src/tac/preflight.py`, `CLAUDE.md`) flip opt-in → opt-out — committing them WITHOUT `--expected-content-sha256` returns rc=5 + writes `outcome=high_risk_file_missing_expected_content_sha` log row. Cutoff filter `_CHECK_289_DISCIPLINE_CUTOFF_UTC = "2026-05-15T22:00:00Z"` exempts pre-cutoff legacy events per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; legacy schema events without `expected_content_sha256_present` field also exempt (pre-GAP-5 logging). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables. Live count at landing: 0 (post-cutoff PHASE-2 commits use the canonical pattern; the WAVE-D 2c957c31e violation was pre-cutoff). 13 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_285_to_289_omnibus_gap_gates.py`. Memory: `feedback_phase_2_land_5_gap_gates_omnibus_followon_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_phase_2_land_5_gap_gates_20260515`.

290. `check_substrate_design_memo_has_canonical_vs_unique_decision_section` — KNOWLEDGE-PRESERVATION-WAVE 2026-05-15 self-protection per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE *"Save related memory and knowledge and instructions to prevent and make us smarter than ever and more capable and rigorous"* + retrospective *"this has been a huge problem since the beginning of the competition and prevented us from actually building original implementations because you didn't understand me and didn't understand the domain and problem space well enough at the time but we learned from PR 95 and just learned the same lesson again but across the entire contest and submission"*. Anchor memos: `feedback_canonical_share_when_serves_unique_when_suppresses_standing_directive_20260515.md` + `feedback_pr95_lesson_now_at_meta_level_unique_and_complete_per_method_default_20260515.md` + `feedback_assumptions_challenge_audit_break_out_local_minima_landed_20260515.md`. Refuses repo-local `.omx/research/*_design_<YYYYMMDD>.md` substrate design memos by default, and external Claude-memory substrate scaffold memos only when `memory_dir=...` is passed explicitly, whose date suffix is >= 2026-05-15 AND whose body does NOT contain the literal section header `## Canonical-vs-unique decision per layer` (case-insensitive). External memory is opt-in so clean clones and CI do not depend on `/Users/adpena/.claude/...` machine-local state. Bug class: pre-rule, NEW substrate scaffolds inherited canonical helpers + META layer + shared engineering scaffold by default; the variance between substrates was the variance of the 10% NOT shared. The 0.196-0.199 cluster was what the SHARED 90% produced — a flat plateau encoded by my reflex toward canonical-helper-share. Per the new "UNIQUE-AND-COMPLETE-PER-METHOD operating mode" CLAUDE.md non-negotiable, every substrate scaffold design memo MUST document explicit per-layer canonical-vs-unique decisions so a reviewer can audit whether the substrate's optimal score path was suppressed by reflex canonicalization. The literal section header is the single structural requirement; the section's body content is the operator-facing audit surface. Acceptance: pre-cutoff memos exempt by date filter; post-cutoff memos with the literal section header pass; post-cutoff in-scope memos without the header are flagged. Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; strict-flip planned after the in-flight + just-completed scaffolds backfill the section. Sister of Catalog #220 (substrate L1 scaffold operational mechanism declaration — same META class at the runtime-effect surface; #290 catches the design-memo-discipline surface) + Catalog #229 (premise-verification-before-edit pattern — same per-memo discipline pattern at the verification surface) + Catalog #241 (substrate META layer contract — encodes the canonical fields; #290 forces the design memo to document why each canonical was adopted vs forked). Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_290_substrate_design_memo_canonical_vs_unique_section.py` cover repo-local research memos and explicit external-memory scans. Memory: `feedback_knowledge_preservation_pr95_meta_level_lesson_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_knowledge_preservation_pr95_meta_level_lesson_20260515`.

291. `check_session_has_recent_meta_assumption_review` — L5-STAIRCASE-V2-AND-ADVERSARIAL-APPARATUS-STRUCTURAL-FIXES 2026-05-15 self-protection per operator retrospective verbatim *"I'm also concerned that be no adversarial review or agent working on the codebase or grand council or skunkworks council ever noticed this issue it's a huge rigor and signal loss issue"*. Anchor memos: `feedback_adversarial_review_apparatus_blind_to_shared_assumption_failure_meta_meta_meta_meta_20260515.md` + `feedback_assumptions_challenge_audit_break_out_local_minima_landed_20260515.md` + `feedback_pr95_lesson_now_at_meta_level_unique_and_complete_per_method_default_20260515.md` + `feedback_canonical_share_when_serves_unique_when_suppresses_standing_directive_20260515.md` + `feedback_l5_staircase_v2_and_adversarial_apparatus_structural_fixes_landed_20260515.md`. Refuses any session whose most-recent META-ASSUMPTION ADVERSARIAL REVIEW memo is older than 7 days OR has more than 50 subagent landings since (whichever first). Scans `~/.claude/projects/-Users-adpena-Projects-pact/memory/` for the most-recent file matching `feedback_*meta_assumption*review*.md` OR `feedback_assumptions_challenge_audit_*.md` AND containing at least one canonical body token (`shared assumption`/`shared assumptions`/`assumption-violation`/`assumption violation`/`if violated`/`META-ASSUMPTION`/`meta-assumption`/`ASSUMPTIONS-CHALLENGE-AUDIT`) — placeholder files with matching name but no real content do NOT satisfy the gate. Landings are counted via `outcome=committed` events in `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` strictly AFTER the review memo's mtime so the review's own session-batch is not counted against itself. Bug class: NOT A SINGLE existing review apparatus (10+ codex reviews / multiple grand councils / skunkworks councils / 3-clean-pass / 270+ STRICT preflight gates / META-meta gates #118/#159/#176/#185 / Contrarian veto power) ever caught the canonicalization-by-default reflex suppressing substrate-optimal engineering across the entire contest. Operator catalysis was required. Structural cause: every existing review type operates WITHIN shared assumptions; none has explicit mandate to interrogate the BACKDROP. The 0.196-0.199 cluster across all substrates IS the empirical cost. The recurring cadence (every 7 days OR every 50 landings, whichever first) extincts the structural blindness so future sessions cannot drift far from the last META-ASSUMPTION review without fresh interrogation of the assumption backdrop. Per CLAUDE.md "META-ASSUMPTION ADVERSARIAL REVIEW" non-negotiable + the new sextet-pact council seat for the Assumption-Adversary + the new mandatory item 8 of the "Recursive adversarial review protocol" (the assumption-challenge axis). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (today's `feedback_assumptions_challenge_audit_break_out_local_minima_landed_20260515.md` is the canonical first instance + most-recent + has the canonical body tokens). Strict-flip atomic with the first cycle completion (i.e. when a future session actually triggers the recurring protocol). Sister of Catalog #229 (premise-verification-before-edit pattern — same per-discipline pattern at the verification surface; #291 is at the META-ASSUMPTION periodic surface) + Catalog #185 (LIVE_COUNT drift between CLAUDE.md text and gate empirical state — same drift-prevention pattern) + Catalog #290 (substrate scaffold canonical-vs-unique decision per layer — #290 enforces per-design-memo while #291 enforces per-session). 31 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_291_session_has_recent_meta_assumption_review.py` covering helper unit-tests (date-suffix parser; strictly-after semantics; missing-log fail-OPEN; canonical body tokens), end-to-end gate behavior (no memory dir / empty memory dir / recent assumptions-audit passes / recent meta-assumption-review passes / 8-day-old flags time / 7-day boundary passes / 51-landing flags landings / 50-landing boundary passes / landings before mtime not counted / non-committed outcomes excluded / placeholder body rejected / picks most-recent / non-matching filename ignored / invalid date suffix ignored / both canaries can fire), waiver semantics (none — this gate has no waiver; the structural protection is the cadence itself; if a session genuinely needs more time, the operator extends by running a META-ASSUMPTION review which auto-resets both canaries), strict mode (raises with Catalog #291 message / silent on clean), corruption resilience (malformed JSONL log handled gracefully), live-repo regression guard, orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard, Catalog #185 sister regression (gate function callable via globals), and constants pinned. Memory: `feedback_l5_staircase_v2_and_adversarial_apparatus_structural_fixes_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_l5_staircase_v2_and_adversarial_apparatus_structural_fixes_20260515`.

Catalog #291 OSS-hermetic implementation note (2026-05-15 follow-up): the runtime gate now scans repo-local `.omx/research/` by default and accepts `*_assumptions_challenge_audit*_<YYYYMMDD>.json` / `.md` artifacts with canonical body tokens; external Claude memory scans are explicit opt-in via `memory_dir=...` only. This supersedes the machine-local default described in the original landing text above.

327. `check_master_gradient_contest_axis_requires_authoritative_custody` -- MASTER-GRADIENT CONTEST-AXIS CUSTODY self-protection 2026-05-18 (per Cicero xhigh adversarial review of `.omx/research/council_t3_grand_council_synthesis_all_research_eureka_engineering_meta_20260518.md` + operator clarification that per-pair master gradient is first-class at training, compress time, and guarded inflate-time planning). Refuses effective append-only rows in `.omx/state/master_gradient_anchors.jsonl` whose `measurement_axis` claims `[contest-CPU]` or `[contest-CUDA]` while hardware/method/pair-count/scored-archive/runtime custody says advisory, local/proxy, MPS, macOS/Darwin, subset, missing scored archive SHA/bytes, missing call/runtime custody, or CPU/GPU axis mismatch. Bug class anchor: archive `f174192aeadf...` carried `[contest-CPU]` with `darwin_arm64_m5_max_macos_cpu_advisory` + `autograd_per_parameter_projected_8pair_subset`; the Fields-Medal Slot 1 orthogonality signal is useful diagnostic evidence, but downstream consumers must fail closed if they treat it as authoritative contest-CPU. Canonical helper in `tac.master_gradient` now exposes `contest_axis_authority_violation_reason`, `is_authoritative_axis_anchor`, `latest_rejected_contest_axis_anchor_for_archive`, and `unresolved_contest_axis_authority_violations`; append-only correction rows can re-label stale contest rows to diagnostic/advisory without mutating historical provenance, and corrections only suppress the same gradient artifact rather than any later row on the same archive. Wire-in: `tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py` is the canonical rank-time consumer and now reports rejected/diagnostic/different-authoritative-axis anchors instead of silently consuming them; `tac.master_gradient_consumers` preserves per-pair and aggregate advisory gradients for training/compress/inflate planning while filtering false contest authority; per-X planning and canonical DuckDB sensitivity backfill share the same helper, with DuckDB rows retaining source axis/hardware/method/evidence provenance. STRICT-from-byte-one after appending the live `[macOS-CPU advisory]` correction drove unresolved effective false-authority count to 0. Dedicated tests cover helper filtering, append-only correction semantics, Cathedral CPU/GPU diagnostics, per-pair consumer preservation, aggregate/per-X/DuckDB filtering, and preflight strict failure on synthetic stale state. 6-hook wire-in declaration per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = ACTIVE (diagnostic gradients remain consumable with correct axis); hook #2 Pareto constraint = ACTIVE via per-X plans preserving non-promotable provenance; hook #3 bit-allocator = ACTIVE via per-byte sensitivity tables and planner masks; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = ACTIVE (canonical consumer); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = ACTIVE through append-only correction discipline; hook #6 probe-disambiguator = ACTIVE via explicit rejected-vs-diagnostic-vs-authoritative classification. Memo: `.omx/research/codex_findings_t3_grand_council_synthesis_20260518T145900Z_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_codex_catalog_327_master_gradient_axis_custody_20260518` L1 (impl_complete + strict_preflight + consumer_wire_in).

328. `check_submission_inflate_py_under_loc_budget` — SUBMISSION INFLATE.PY LOC BUDGET AUDIT 2026-05-18 (per `.omx/research/grand_council_symposium_inflate_py_extreme_compression_20260518.md` and the operator clarification that optimization must target score-relevant archive/runtime surfaces, not cargo-cult source minification). Flags direct `submissions/*/inflate.py` files over 200 physical source lines unless they carry `# INFLATE_PY_LOC_BUDGET_OK:<rationale>` in the first 40 lines. This gate is deliberately a review/runtime-closure guard, NOT score evidence: upstream `evaluate.py` charges `archive.zip` bytes, not `inflate.py` source bytes, so compressing Python source has zero direct score impact. The operator-facing audit is `tools/audit_submission_inflate_py_loc_budget.py`, backed by reusable helper `tac.submission_inflate_loc_budget`. Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" because live tracked submissions still include known >200 LOC runtimes (`pr106_latent_sidecar_r2_pr101_grammar`, `hdm8_film_grain_sidecar`, `pr106_stacked`, `pr103_pr106_final_runtime`, `pr106_lrl1_sidechannel`, and several near-boundary research runtimes). Strict-flip planned after OP-2/OP-5 cleanup extracts reusable helpers or adds source-faithful waivers. Sister of Catalog #295 (empty-PYTHONPATH runtime closure), Catalog #205 (canonical inflate device selector), Catalog #146 (contest-compliant inflate runtime template), and Catalog #327 (authority-label custody): together they keep `inflate.py` small, self-contained, axis-honest, and reviewable without confusing source LOC with the contest rate term. Tests: `src/tac/tests/test_check_328_submission_inflate_py_loc_budget.py`. Lane: `lane_codex_catalog_328_inflate_py_loc_budget_audit_20260518` L1 (impl_complete + operator_audit + preflight_warn_only + research_correction).

329. `ProvenanceKind contest-compliance payload-kinds extension` — PROCEDURAL-GENERATION / WEIGHT-DERIVED / FORBIDDEN-OUT-OF-ARCHIVE PROVENANCE UMBRELLA 2026-05-18 (per `.omx/research/codex_routing_directive_inflate_py_plus_wyner_ziv_compliance_plus_master_gradient_extension_20260518.md` Items 1-2). Extends `tac.provenance.ProvenanceKind` with `PROCEDURAL_GENERATION_FROM_ARCHIVE_SEED`, `WEIGHT_DERIVED_CODEBOOK`, and `FORBIDDEN_OUT_OF_ARCHIVE_PAYLOAD`; adds single-line builders; makes `audit_score_claim_dict` fail closed when a score-claiming payload carries the forbidden out-of-archive sentinel; and adds operator-audit classifier `FORBIDDEN_OUT_OF_ARCHIVE_PAYLOAD` in `tools/audit_provenance_compliance.py`. Sister WZ hardening makes `DeliverabilityProof.contest_compliance_rationale` mandatory non-empty and adds `contest_compliance_citation_chain` requiring at least one compliant route anchor (`archive.zip seed inclusion`, `weight-derived codebook from shipped archive bytes`, `null-space in-archive byte reduction`, or `reviewability-only no-score-impact`). This is not a score claim and does not make procedural generation automatically contest-promotable; exact archive/runtime auth-eval remains required. Tests: `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_contract.py`, `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_builders.py`, `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_validator.py`, `src/tac/tests/test_wyner_ziv_deliverability_proof_builder.py`. Lane: `lane_codex_provenance_wz_compliance_20260518`.

330. `check_modal_harvesters_record_call_id_outcome` — MODAL HARVESTER CALL-ID OUTCOME LEDGER 2026-05-18 (per Z6-v2 recipe-mode bug plus harvester-ledger gap directive and Codex/Newton adversarial review). Refuses Modal harvester/recovery surfaces that call `FunctionCall.from_id(...).get(...)` without recording terminal outcomes in the canonical `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger.jsonl` lifecycle through `tac.deploy.modal.harvest_outcomes.append_terminal_call_id_ledger_event`, `update_call_id_outcome`, or an explicit `# HARVESTER_LEDGER_WRITE_OK:<rationale>` waiver. This is the harvest-side sister of Catalog #245: dispatch registration alone is insufficient if a later concurrent harvester can observe `harvested`, `failed`, `stale`, or `function_timeout` and leave the call_id stuck at `dispatched`. The canonical helper maps rc=0 to `harvested`, rc!=0 to `failed`, Modal result-cache expiry to `stale`, Modal function timeout to `failed`, and keeps plain poll `TimeoutError` nonterminal. `tools/harvest_modal_calls.py` and `tools/parallel_harvest_actuator.py` both route through the shared helper; `parallel_harvest_actuator` also backfills already-harvested local terminal state. STRICT-from-byte-one because live count is 0 at landing. Tests: `src/tac/tests/test_parallel_harvest_actuator.py`, `src/tac/tests/test_build_pr101_finetuned_archive_codec_dir.py`, `src/tac/tests/test_check_330_modal_harvester_call_id_ledger_outcome.py`. Lane: `lane_codex_catalog_330_modal_harvester_call_id_outcome_20260518` L1 (impl_complete + strict_preflight + canonical_helper).

331. `check_canonical_task_status_no_dangling_transitions` — CANONICAL TASK STATUS TRANSITION STRICTNESS 2026-05-18 (per `.omx/research/codex_routing_directive_canonical_task_status_single_source_of_truth_20260518.md` ITEM 4 and the Codex canonical task-status landing). 292. `check_grand_council_deliberation_has_explicit_assumption_statements` — OR2-GRAND-COUNCIL-PER-ROUND-ASSUMPTION-STATEMENT-DISCIPLINE 2026-05-15 self-protection per Fix 7 of the Adversarial-apparatus retrospective (`feedback_adversarial_review_apparatus_blind_to_shared_assumption_failure_meta_meta_meta_meta_20260515.md`). Sister of Catalog #291 at the per-DELIBERATION surface where #291 enforces per-SESSION cadence. Bug class: a grand council can fan out within a clean Catalog #291 cadence (the session has a recent META-ASSUMPTION review) AND still produce an assumption-bound deliberation if individual members never surface their operating-within assumptions. The 0.196-0.199 cluster is the empirical cost of pre-Fix-7 deliberations that reached consensus without per-member assumption-statement discipline. The Assumption-Adversary sextet-pact council seat (CLAUDE.md "Council conduct" amendment) is the runtime mechanism; this gate is the structural protection that refuses council memos lacking the discipline tokens. Scans repo-local `.omx/research/` by default for `feedback_*council*<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*grand_council*<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*skunkworks_council*<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*reunion*symposium*<YYYYMMDD>.md` whose date suffix is >= 2026-05-15 (the Fix-7 cutoff exempts legacy memos); external Claude-memory scans require explicit `memory_dir=...` opt-in so clean clones and CI are hermetic per sister Catalog #291 OSS-hermetic design. Acceptance: post-cutoff council memos with (a) per-member operating-within phrase (`operating within` / `shared assumption I am` / `the assumption I am` / `the shared assumption` / `assumption I am operating within` / `shared-assumption I am` / `shared assumption for this` / `my operating assumption` / `operating assumption for this`) OR (b) Assumption-Adversary evaluation block (`assumption-adversary` / `assumption adversary` / `hard-earned vs cargo-culted` / `hard_earned vs cargo_culted` / `hard earned vs cargo culted` / `cargo-culted` / `cargo_culted`) OR (c) same-line `# COUNCIL_ASSUMPTION_STATEMENT_WAIVED:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literal rejected). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 for default repo-local scan (no in-repo council memos predating the Fix-7 amendment; external Claude memory carries 10 pre-Fix-7 council memos which are out-of-scope by default per OSS-hermetic policy). Strict-flip planned after sister-subagent backfill clears any remaining repo-local pre-Fix-7 council memos. 35 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_292_grand_council_assumption_statements.py` covering helper unit-tests (date-suffix parser; waiver helper with placeholder rejection; canonical token sets pinned), end-to-end gate behavior (empty / missing memory dir / operating-within accepted / Assumption-Adversary block accepted / post-cutoff legacy flagged / pre-cutoff exempt / 2026-06-01 future post-cutoff in-scope / skunkworks council in-scope / grand reunion symposium in-scope / non-council out-of-scope / same-line waiver / placeholder rationale rejected / `<reason>` placeholder rejected / non-placeholder rationale accepted / strict-mode raises with Catalog #292 + Council-conduct + Fix-7 message / strict silent on clean / string repo_root accepted / multi-violation aggregated / repo-local research_dir scanned by default / HARD-EARNED-vs-CARGO-CULTED recognized / case-insensitive matching / verbose output / invalid date suffix skipped / unrelated md files ignored / external memory_dir opt-in-only / dedup research+memory same path), Catalog #185 sister regression (gate function callable via globals), orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard, and live-repo regression guard (≤ 5 ceiling for repo-local default scan). Sister of Catalog #229 (premise-verification-before-edit) + Catalog #290 (substrate canonical-vs-unique discipline) + Catalog #291 (META-ASSUMPTION cadence — session-level cousin) + Catalog #185 (CLAUDE.md catalog text vs empirical state drift — META-meta-meta protection for this entry's claimed live count). Memory: `feedback_or2_grand_council_per_round_assumption_statement_discipline_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_or2_grand_council_assumption_statement_discipline_20260515`. **Amendment 2026-05-16 (Catalog #313 cross-reference)**: the Assumption-Adversary's per-round mandate now ALSO includes the standing question *"has this probe / dispatch already been executed and adjudicated?"* for every council-proposed dispatch target. The probe-outcomes ledger at `.omx/state/probe_outcomes.jsonl` (canonical helper `tac.probe_outcomes_ledger`; CLI `tools/check_predecessor_probe_outcome.py`) is the queryable source of truth. Council deliberations that propose dispatch on a substrate with recent blocking verdict in `{INDEPENDENT, KILL, DEFER}` MUST cite the predecessor verdict + rationale for override OR ratify the predecessor (no fresh dispatch). The Catalog #313 STRICT preflight gate refuses dispatch wrappers that bypass this discipline; runtime check is wired into `tools/operator_authorize.py::_check_predecessor_probe_outcome` between Catalog #152 + #243 insertion points. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL without research exhaustion": a blocking predecessor outcome is a research-deferral, not a kill — alternative reducers per Catalog #308 are the canonical research path forward.

293. `check_cathedral_literature_anchors_have_source_scope` — Cathedral/autopilot source-fidelity self-protection 2026-05-16. Refuses any canonical substrate composition row or serialized Pareto row with a non-empty `literature_anchor` unless it also carries non-placeholder `source_supports`, `paper_claim_scope`, `pact_must_prove`, and `decode_complexity_evidence`. Bug class: a Cathedral row can cite Ballé / Atick-Redlich / Rao-Ballard / DreamerV3 / world-model / MDL/IB papers and carry a predicted score band while silently dropping the boundary between what the paper actually supports and what Pact must prove on byte-closed contest archives. That turns literature into false score authority and can steer the autopilot toward unsupported score-lowering claims. The source-scope fields keep literature anchors as hypothesis provenance, not promotion evidence. STRICT-from-byte-one because `src/tac/optimization/substrate_composition_matrix.py` already carries the complete source-scope contract for all current anchored rows and serialized Pareto rows preserve the fields. Sister of Catalog #291/#292 (assumption/fidelity review surfaces), Catalog #125 (solver wire-in), and Catalog #233 (promotion evidence discipline). Dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_293_cathedral_literature_scope.py` cover live-repo zero violations, missing inventory fields, missing serialized Pareto fields, unanchored-row skip behavior, strict-mode failure, and `preflight_all(strict=True)` wiring. Ledger: `.omx/research/cathedral_literature_anchor_preflight_gate_20260516_codex.md`. Lane: `lane_cathedral_literature_anchor_preflight_gate_20260516`.

295. `check_submission_inflate_works_with_empty_pythonpath` — SUBMISSION INFLATE.PY WORKS WITH EMPTY PYTHONPATH 2026-05-16 self-protection per NSCS06 v5 Modal dispatch failure anchor `fc-01KRQMAQ7V41AFYMJH5HRK9P10` (`submissions/nscs06_carmack_hotz_strip_everything/inflate.py` used PYTHONPATH-shim pattern `sys.path.insert(0, str(HERE / "src"))` then `from tac.substrates...` which worked locally but FAILED on Modal worker because `src/tac/...` was never vendored into the submission directory). Refuses any `submissions/*/inflate.py` (excluding `submissions/exact_current/` per CLAUDE.md mutation frontier; excluding `_intake_` vendored clones) that EITHER (a) imports `from tac.*` / `import tac.*` WITHOUT a sibling `tac/` package vendored alongside (the NSCS06 v5 bug class via implicit working-tree dependency), OR (b) calls `sys.path.insert(...)` pointing OUTSIDE the submission directory tree (e.g. `HERE.parent / "sibling_sub" / "src"` or unresolved variable expression). Acceptance cascades: (1) no `sys.path.insert` + no `from tac.*` imports = canonical simple inflate; (2) `sys.path.insert(HERE / "src")` + no tac imports = NSCS06-v6-style vendored non-tac codec alongside (canonical pattern @ commit `90bca47ff`); (3) `sys.path.insert(HERE / "src")` + `from tac.*` AND vendored `src/tac/__init__.py` alongside = explicit-vendor accept; (4) `sys.path.insert(HERE)` + `from tac.*` AND vendored `tac/__init__.py` directly alongside = explicit-vendor accept; (5) one-level variable indirection (`SRC_DIR = HERE / "src"` then `sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC_DIR))`) traced by AST module-level binding walker; (6) same-line `# SUBMISSION_PYTHONPATH_SHIM_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the `sys.path.insert(...)` line (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Per CLAUDE.md HNeRV parity discipline lesson 9 (Runtime closure — dependency closure failures including local paths and hidden sidecars are runtime blockers); per the 8th forbidden pattern (research-substrate trap — a submission that cannot be run from an empty PYTHONPATH on the Modal worker is by construction not self-contained). Sister of Catalog #205 (`check_inflate_py_uses_canonical_select_inflate_device` — same `submissions/*/inflate.py` scan surface; #205 catches inline device-fork; #295 catches PYTHONPATH-shim self-containment violations). **STRICT-FLIPPED 2026-05-16** by SISTER-BACKFILL-WAVE per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (driven from 6 → 0 in same commit batch via the 4-priority backfill below). Backfill mapping per `.omx/research/submission_inflate_pythonpath_shim_audit_20260516.md` 3-column inventory: **P1** `nscs01_nullspace_split_renderer` adopted the NSCS01-style fail-closed-at-runtime waiver path explicitly named in the gate's own docstring (the checked-in shim raises `RuntimeError` before any `sys.path.insert` reaches the operator's working tree; the trainer-emitted `submission_dir/inflate.py` is GENUINELY self-contained via `_write_runtime` already-existing vendoring of `src/tac/substrates/nscs01_nullspace_split_renderer/` + `src/tac/substrates/_shared/inflate_runtime.py` into `submission_dir/src/tac/...`). **P2a** `apogee_v2` adopted the NSCS06-v6 vendor-the-codec pattern (vendored `src/tac/water_filling_codec_v2.py` + `src/tac/arithmetic_qint_codec.py` into `submissions/apogee_v2/src/tac/__init__.py` + 2 modules; encode-side `_EXP_MAX/_EXP_MIN/QINT_LEVELS/WaterFillError/water_fill_bit_budget` constants/stubs inlined so the vendored copy imports cleanly on a worker with NO `tac.block_fp_codec` / `tac.water_filling_codec` present; verified empirically by `PYTHONPATH="" python -c "...decode_omega_w_v2..."` from cwd=/tmp). **P2b** `magic_codec_pr106_r2` adopted same-line `# SUBMISSION_PYTHONPATH_SHIM_OK` waivers on the 2 function-scoped lazy `from tac.packet_compiler.*` imports (the module's docstring explicitly designates it as research-only / non-promotable / requires sibling submission + tac runtime co-mounted via LAB dispatch flow). **P3** `pr106_lrl1_sidechannel` + `pr106_stacked` + `pr106_yshift_sidechannel` adopted same-line `# SUBMISSION_PYTHONPATH_SHIM_OK` waivers on their `sys.path.insert(HERE.parent / "apogee_intN" / "src")` / `HERE.parent / "pr106_latent_sidecar" / "src"` lines documenting sibling-submission shipping requirements per audit Priority 4 option (b). Gate-side hardening also landed in the same commit batch: the no-`sys.path.insert`-but-`from tac.*`-imports branch in `_scan_inflate_for_pythonpath_shim_self_contained` now respects same-line waivers consistent with the violation message's "OR add same-line `# SUBMISSION_PYTHONPATH_SHIM_OK:<rationale>` waiver" advice (previously a code-vs-message gap where the gate refused waivers in this code path). 23 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_295_submission_inflate_empty_pythonpath.py` covering live-repo regression guard (≤20 ceiling), positive (bare tac import without sys.path / sys.path.insert + tac import no vendor / sys.path.insert parent traversal), negative (no sys.path + no tac / NSCS06-v6 vendored alongside / pr106-style vendored codec no tac / tac vendored in src subdir / tac vendored directly / one-level indirection), waiver semantics (rationale accepted / `<rationale>` + `<reason>` placeholders rejected), scope (exact_current exempt / intake clones exempt / no submissions dir empty), edge cases (malformed Python tolerated / docstring mention not flagged), strict-mode (raises with Catalog #295 message / silent on clean), multi-file aggregation, orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard, and live-repo NSCS01-remains-flagged regression guard. Memory: `feedback_catalog_295_submission_inflate_empty_pythonpath_strict_gate_landed_20260516.md`. Lane: `lane_check_submission_inflate_empty_pythonpath_strict_gate_20260516`. Audit ledger: `.omx/research/submission_inflate_pythonpath_shim_audit_20260516.md` (3-column AFFECTED/FIXED/NOT_APPLICABLE inventory feeding the sister-backfill subagent).

294. `check_substrate_landing_memo_has_9_dim_checklist_evidence_section` — 9-DIMENSION SUCCESS CHECKLIST EVIDENCE SECTION 2026-05-15 self-protection per operator standing directive *"Need to ensure uniqueness and beautify and elegance and distinctness and rigor and optimization per technique and stack of stacks while still deterministic reproducibility and extreme optimization and performance and optimal minimal contest score"*. Anchor memo: `feedback_9_dimension_success_checklist_per_substrate_and_stack_of_stacks_standing_directive_20260515.md`. Refuses repo-local `.omx/research/*_design_<YYYYMMDD>.md` substrate landing + stack-of-stacks composition memos by default, plus external Claude-memory substrate landing memos only when `memory_dir=...` is passed explicitly (OSS-hermetic per sister Catalog #290 / #291 / #292), whose date suffix is >= 2026-05-15 AND whose body does NOT contain the literal section header `## 9-dimension success checklist evidence` (case-insensitive). Filename scope matches one of: `feedback_*substrate*landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*scaffold*landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_nscs<N>*landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*stack_of_stacks*landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `feedback_*composition*landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` / `*_design_<YYYYMMDD>.md`. Bug class: per the 9-dim checklist directive, every substrate landing AND every stack-of-stacks composition memo must document evidence across ALL 9 dimensions: (1) UNIQUENESS (class-shift not within-class); (2) BEAUTY + ELEGANCE (PR101-style 30-sec-reviewable); (3) DISTINCTNESS (explicitly different from sisters); (4) RIGOR (premise verification + adversarial review + assumption classification + empirical anchor); (5) OPTIMIZATION PER TECHNIQUE (substrate-optimal engineering — covered by sister Catalog #290); (6) STACK-OF-STACKS-COMPOSABILITY (orthogonal axes + additive ΔS); (7) DETERMINISTIC REPRODUCIBILITY (byte-stable + seed-pinned); (8) EXTREME OPTIMIZATION + PERFORMANCE; (9) OPTIMAL MINIMAL CONTEST SCORE. Missing evidence on ANY dimension = landing INVALID. The literal section header is the single structural requirement; the section body content is the operator-facing audit surface. Sister Catalog #290 covers Dimension 5 specifically; #294 covers Dimensions 1-4 + 6-9 via the unified `## 9-dimension success checklist evidence` section header so the operator can audit per-dimension evidence in one place. Acceptance: pre-cutoff memos exempt by date filter; post-cutoff memos with the literal section header pass; post-cutoff memos with same-line `# 9_DIM_CHECKLIST_EVIDENCE_WAIVED:<rationale>` waiver pass (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 6 (`.omx/research/atw_codec_atick_tishby_wyner_v1_design_20260515.md` + `nscs01_nullspace_split_renderer_design_20260515.md` + `stc_dasher_arithmetic_maximalism_v1_design_20260515.md` + `substrate_meta_layer_design_20260515.md` + `u_die_kl_substrate_wide_loss_v1_design_20260515.md` + `wunderkind_g1_entropy_coded_v2_design_20260515.md` — all 2026-05-15 design memos that predate the standing directive). Strict-flip planned alongside the sister-subagent backfill wave that adds the section header to existing in-flight memos. Sister of Catalog #290 (Dimension 5 + same-shape OSS-hermetic design-memo gate) + Catalog #291 (per-session META-ASSUMPTION cadence) + Catalog #292 (per-deliberation council assumption discipline) + Catalog #220 (substrate L1 scaffold operational mechanism declaration — same META class at the runtime-effect surface) + Catalog #229 (premise-verification-before-edit — same per-memo discipline pattern) + Catalog #241 (substrate META layer contract — encodes the canonical fields). 25 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_294_substrate_landing_memo_has_9_dim_checklist_evidence_section.py` covering live-repo regression guard (≤30 ceiling), missing-section flagging, canonical accept, case-insensitive header, NSCS / stack-of-stacks / composition filename scope, pre-cutoff exempt, same-line waiver with placeholder/empty rejection, strict-mode raise with Catalog #294 message, string repo_root accepted, external-memory opt-in-only, orchestrator-callsite warn-only wire-in regression guard. Memory: `feedback_catalog_294_9_dim_checklist_evidence_section_landed_20260515.md`. Lane: `lane_catalog_294_9_dim_checklist_evidence_section_20260515`.

280. `check_dispatch_protocol_unresolved_recipe_blocks_unless_paired_waiver` — Codex review bklem3v5j HIGH F2 self-protection 2026-05-15. Refuses any state of `tools/canonical_dispatch_optimization_protocol.py` where the unresolved-recipe branch of `_verify_tier2` / `_verify_tier3` sets per-signal True (vacuous PASS) without paired-env discipline (`--allow-no-recipe-advisory-mode` AND `--no-recipe-rationale <text>` per Catalog #199 sister). Bug class anchor: codex review bklem3v5j HIGH F2 caught that when `recipe_path is None` the prior implementation marked every Tier 2 recipe-field check `True` (vacuous PASS) which made strict callers silently approve dispatch with NO recipe-side coverage; Tier 3 recipe-vs-trainer-state divergence (the Z3 v2 / Z4 / Z5 bug class anchor; Catalog #240) had the same pattern. A strict CLI / preflight invocation with no recipe could therefore return `overall_pass=True` while skipping `min_vram_gb` / `target_modes` / `video_input_strategy` / `canary_status` / hardware-routing / research-only checks. Companion runtime fix (a) added paired-env kwargs `allow_no_recipe_advisory_mode: bool = False` + `no_recipe_rationale: str | None = None` to `_verify_tier2` / `_verify_tier3` / `verify_dispatch_protocol_complete`; (b) changed the unresolved-recipe branches to BLOCK every Tier 2/3 signal (default fail-closed); (c) added CLI flags `--allow-no-recipe-advisory-mode` + `--no-recipe-rationale <text>` with paired-env validator that raises `SystemExit(11)` on bare opt-in without rationale; (d) per codex's explicit recommendation, even when paired advisory-mode is opted into, `--strict` STILL exits nonzero (the umbrella retains an explicit advisory-mode blocker so advisory != pass). Empirical receipts: `--strict` on a recipe-less invocation → rc=1 (was rc=0 before fix); bare `--allow-no-recipe-advisory-mode` (no rationale) → rc=11; paired `--allow-no-recipe-advisory-mode --no-recipe-rationale <text>` + `--strict` → rc=1 (advisory != pass). File-level `# DISPATCH_PROTOCOL_UNRESOLVED_RECIPE_VACUOUS_OK:<rationale>` waiver in the file's first 80 lines accepts the rare deliberate case (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literal rejected). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — live count at landing: 0 (the F2 fix lands in the same commit batch). 14 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_280_dispatch_protocol_unresolved_recipe_blocks.py` covering live-repo regression guard, positive (vacuous-True F2 regression flagged / missing paired-env kwargs flagged / missing blocker-message tokens flagged), negative (canonical fix accepted), waiver semantics (rationale accepted / placeholder rejected), strict-mode raises with Catalog #280, edge cases (missing target file silent), orchestrator strict=True wire-in regression guard, AND 3 CLI subprocess regression tests per codex's explicit recommendations: (i) `--strict` recipe-less exits nonzero, (ii) bare advisory opt-in without rationale rejected rc=11, (iii) paired advisory opt-in + `--strict` STILL nonzero (advisory != pass). Sister of Catalog #199 (`check_operator_authorize_bypass_requires_session_budget` — the canonical paired-env discipline gate this gate's recommendation derives from) + Catalog #270 (the umbrella protocol gate this gate self-protects).

297. `check_substrate_signal_axis_destruction_has_reversibility_probe` — META-CARGO-CULT META-CC-2 self-protection 2026-05-16 (per `.omx/research/meta_assumption_backfill_audit_all_staircase_substrates_20260516.md` + `.omx/research/grand_council_symposium_nscs06_carmack_hotz_falsification_redesign_multipath_20260516.md` + commit `4292c8ce2` (symposium) + commit `b0a7ff474` (META-assumption audit)). Refuses substrate trainers + codec modules under `src/tac/substrates/*/` and `experiments/train_substrate_*.py` files containing forbidden signal-axis-destruction tokens: (1) `Y=R=G=B` / `R=G=B=Y` chained-assignment (NSCS06 grayscale-to-RGB replication anchor); (2) `grayscale_to_rgb(...duplicate...)` call with duplicate semantics; (3) `frame.mean(...color...)` chroma-collapsing axis-mean; (4) `rgb_grey = (r+g+b)/3` manual chroma-to-luma reduction; (5) `single_channel_only` variable / kwarg / comment marker; (6) `_grayscale_to_rgb` helper function name; (7) `_drop_chroma` helper function name. Per-line acceptance cascade: (a) sister probe file exists at `tools/probe_<substrate_id>_reversibility*.py` (canonical pattern, sister derived from `src/tac/substrates/<id>/` parent dir OR `experiments/train_substrate_<id>.py` filename token); (b) same-line waiver `# SIGNAL_AXIS_DESTRUCTION_REVERSIBLE_PROBE_OK:<rationale>` on the destruction-token line (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected); (c) enclosing function name contains the substring `_compress_time_only` signaling explicitly that destruction lives only at compress time and the downstream loss does NOT route through SegNet/PoseNet on the destroyed signal. Pure comment lines (starting with `#` after leading whitespace) are NOT flagged so docstring/comment mentions are tolerated. Test files (`/tests/` subdir, `test_*.py` filenames) + vendored intake clones + generated build/oss-export artifacts excluded. Bug class anchors: NSCS06 Y=R=G=B chroma replication (seg=64.59 because SegNet's stride-2 stem cannot recover destroyed chroma); NSCS06 np.roll global translation (pose=149.03 empirical proof that PoseNet is NOT translation-invariant); Z3-G1 empty `hyperprior_weights_int8 = b""` + `w_hat_int8 = b""` slots (silent reproduction of Z3 v2 baseline 0.19869 to 5 decimals — empty slots never affect the score). Per CLAUDE.md "Apples-to-apples evidence discipline" + "Bit-level deconstruction and entropy discipline" + Catalog #220 (substrate L1+ byte-addition operational mechanism) + Catalog #139 (no-op detector packet compiler) + Catalog #105 (no-op provenance): every transform that mutates archive bytes MUST be paired with a proof that the mutation actually affects the score, not just an assumption. Sister of Catalog #220 (substrate L1+ byte-addition operational mechanism) + Catalog #272 (distinguishing-feature integration contract) + Catalog #139 (packet compiler no-op detector) + Catalog #105 (no-op provenance). Together they close the "compress-destroys-signal-without-inflate-reconstruction" surface across multiple orthogonal axes: L1+ scaffold byte-addition (#220) + distinguishing-feature contract (#272) + packet compiler no-op detector (#139) + no-op provenance (#105) + signal-axis destruction reversibility probe (#297). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 2 (`src/tac/substrates/nscs06_carmack_hotz_strip_everything/inflate.py:42` + `:111` — the `_grayscale_to_rgb` helper def + invocation; NSCS06 lane is already `research_only=true` per the v6 falsification per commit `a16eb06fd`). Strict-flip planned after either (a) operator-routed NSCS06 redesign lands a sister `tools/probe_nscs06_carmack_hotz_strip_everything_reversibility.py` proving the chroma reconstruction is empirically reversible per the contest scorer's actual gradient response, OR (b) the NSCS06 `_grayscale_to_rgb` helper definition is rewritten into a `_compress_time_only`-named function, OR (c) operator-approved same-line waivers documenting the deferred reversibility status. 24 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_297_signal_axis_reversibility.py` covering live-repo regression guard (≤5 ceiling), out-of-scope (no substrates dir / empty / file without tokens), positive (every destruction pattern flagged: grayscale_to_rgb helper / Y=R=G=B chained / _drop_chroma / single_channel_only / rgb_grey literal / grayscale_to_rgb call with duplicate / train_substrate_*.py file in experiments), negative (sister probe file accepts / probe with suffix accepts / same-line waiver / `_compress_time_only` function short-circuits), waiver semantics (`<rationale>` + `<reason>` placeholders rejected), comment-line tolerance (pure comment with pattern NOT flagged), strict-mode (raises with Catalog #297 message / silent on clean), aggregation (multi-violation one file / multi-violation across files), test files excluded, string repo_root accepted, syntax-error file tolerated, verbose output (clean + dirty). 323. `check_no_score_claim_without_canonical_provenance` — PROVENANCE CANONICAL FIX META-class extinction 2026-05-17 (per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE *"We need to fix the provenance issue for all and fix it permanently and canonically and make it easy"*). META-class umbrella that subsumes 5 sister phantom-score class instances (Catalog #287 docstring overstatement / #249 misleading directory name / #319 autopilot venn reweight without DeliverabilityProof / #321 research sidecar phantom score / #823 SUPER_ADDITIVE byte-identity artifact) at the persisted-artifact-row surface. Refuses `.omx/state/**/*.json` + `.omx/state/**/*.jsonl` + `experiments/results/**/build_manifest.json` + `experiments/results/**/auth_eval_*.json` + sister canonical persisted artifacts whose row carries any score-claim key (`score` / `contest_score` / `final_score` / `predicted_score` / `deliverable_score_savings_estimate` / `deliverable_savings` / `composition_alpha` / `alpha` / `alpha_savings_ratio_form` / `savings` / `delta_s` / `delta_score` / `score_savings` / `auth_eval_score` / `recomputed_score`) WITHOUT a canonical `provenance` sub-object validated via `tac.provenance.audit_score_claim_dict`. **Canonical helper**: NEW `src/tac/provenance/` package (~1500 LOC across 5 files: `contract.py` Provenance + ScoreClaim dataclasses with 6-kind taxonomy + 8-grade taxonomy + cross-validation invariants; `builders.py` 7 single-line canonical builders + `@requires_canonical_provenance` decorator; `validator.py` validate_provenance + validate_score_claim + audit_score_claim_dict with axis × hardware × grade canonical pairings; `adapters.py` 8 backward-compat shims for legacy ContestResult / CompositionRow / DeliverabilityProof / WynerZivLayerResult / etc.; `__init__.py` narrow public API). **Companion audit tool**: `tools/audit_provenance_compliance.py` (~370 LOC; operator-runnable any time; CLI emits JSON or `--summary` human-readable; classifies VIOLATION rows by 6 categories: MISSING_PROVENANCE / BYTE_IDENTITY_ARTIFACT / RESEARCH_SIDECAR_SCORE_CLAIMED / AXIS_HARDWARE_MISMATCH / INVALID_PROVENANCE_SHAPE / STALE_CAPTURED_AT). **Developer guide**: `docs/provenance_canonical_usage.md` (5 quick-start patterns + decorator usage + adapter shim semantics + waiver semantics + sister-gate cross-references). **Acceptance**: (a) row lacks any score-claim key → CLEAN; (b) row has score-claim keys + valid `provenance` sub-object via `validate_provenance` → CLEAN; (c) row has score-claim keys + `provenance` sub-object that fails reconstruction → flagged; (d) row has score-claim keys + non-zero score + `provenance.score_claim_valid=False` → flagged as phantom-score class; (e) string-value-level waiver `# PROVENANCE_CANONICAL_WAIVED:<rationale>` in any row field, placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's own docstring example cannot self-waive. **Exempt path markers**: `experiments/results/` build artifacts, `_intake_` vendored clones, `.omx/oss_export/`, `vendored/`, `reports/raw/`, `.omx/state/archive/`, `.omx/state/quarantine`. **Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 543 (baseline legacy violations across `.omx/state/` + `experiments/results/`; the gate fires structurally so any NEW score-claim artifact added without canonical Provenance is surfaced immediately; backfill sweep is operator-routed across the 188-file violation set). Strict-flip planned after backfill sweep brings live count to 0 OR each row carries a waiver/migration to canonical Provenance. **Sister gates PRESERVED as defense-in-depth** (they remain strict at their own surfaces; #323 is the umbrella): Catalog #287 (`check_empirical_claims_have_evidence` — docstring-tag surface) + Catalog #249 (`check_no_misleading_device_named_output_directories` — filename surface) + Catalog #319 (`check_substrate_wyner_ziv_reweight_has_deliverability_proof` + sister — autopilot-consumer surface) + Catalog #321 (`check_no_phantom_wyner_ziv_savings_from_research_sidecar` — prober artifact surface) + Catalog #185 (`check_strict_flipped_catalog_entries_have_live_count_zero` — META-meta drift). Together they extinct the phantom-score class at SIX surfaces: docstring tag (#287) + filename (#249) + autopilot consumer (#319) + prober artifact (#321) + META-meta drift (#185) + canonical Provenance umbrella (#323). **129 dedicated tests pass** across `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_contract.py` (31 tests — invariants, kind-specific rules, grade-axis-hardware cross-checks, sentinel, frozen invariant), `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_builders.py` (29 tests — each of 7 builders + transient-path refusal + pytest-fixture path allow + decorator scenarios), `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_validator.py` (23 tests — staleness/future/parse-fail + cycle detection + audit_score_claim_dict on all 5 phantom-class anchors + round-trip serialization + live-repo guard), `src/tac/tests/test_provenance_adapters.py` (22 tests — each of 8 adapters with legacy dict + object-attribute + invalid-sha + missing-field + Catalog #823 byte-identity detection in composition adapter), `src/tac/tests/test_check_323_canonical_provenance.py` (24 tests — clean repo + each phantom-class anchor caught + waiver mechanism + exempt markers + strict-mode raise + live-repo regression guard + orchestrator wire-in regression guard + Catalog #185 sister-callable regression guard). **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = ACTIVE (every score-axis weight contribution must carry canonical Provenance; downstream `tac.sensitivity_map.*` consumers can call `audit_score_claim_dict` on row contributions); hook #2 Pareto constraint = ACTIVE (Pareto rows that combine multiple Provenances via `build_provenance_aggregate` automatically detect byte-identity via Catalog #823 sentinel); hook #3 bit-allocator = ACTIVE (any future bit-allocator that consumes score-claim rows inherits the validator via `audit_score_claim_dict`); hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = ACTIVE (predicted-from-model rows from autopilot are non-promotable by construction; the umbrella catches any attempt to promote a predicted row to a contest score claim); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = ACTIVE (canonical posterior writes must embed Provenance per the adapters; legacy rows are surfaced as migration candidates by the audit tool); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = ACTIVE (the canonical Provenance contract IS the disambiguator between contest-archive-member / research-sidecar / predicted / advisory grades — score-claim rows that span multiple grades are forced through the aggregate WORST-grade rule). 326. `check_substrate_driver_consumes_trainer_mode_env_var` — DRIVER MODE HARDCODE FIX 2026-05-18 (per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable + Z6-v2 Wave 2 DEFER 2026-05-18 empirical anchor). Refuses `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_*.sh` drivers where the smoke-vs-full mode routing default biases toward smoke without explicit recipe-side opt-in for full. Delegates verdict classification to the canonical operator-facing audit tool `tools/audit_substrate_driver_mode_hardcode.py::audit_all_drivers` so the gate + the operator-facing reproducer stay in lock-step. **Bug class anchor**: Z6-v2 Wave 2 full canary dispatch `fc-01KRW7ZCYK5XF6MSHD24R71A46` ran `_smoke_main` despite the recipe requesting `Z6_EPOCHS=100` full-mode because the Wave 2 recipe `env_overrides` block did NOT set `SMOKE_ONLY=0` and the driver's `SMOKE_ONLY="${SMOKE_ONLY:-1}"` default produced smoke-mode regardless of intent. Smoke-mode-by-default + recipe not declaring full-mode-override = trainer enters `_smoke_main` with synthetic-cfg overriding the council-binding spec. Sister META-pattern across C6 IBPS DEFER + Z6-v2 DEFER showed TWO consecutive ASYMPTOTIC substrate dispatches frustrated by infrastructure-level bugs (NOT paradigm falsification per Catalog #307). **Bug-class verdicts refused** (from canonical audit): `HARDCODES_SMOKE_NO_RECIPE_OPT_OUT` (driver unconditionally passes --smoke AND matching recipe is dispatchable / NOT opted-out via research_only/smoke_only/dispatch_enabled=false); `CONSUMES_ENV_DEFAULTS_SMOKE_BUG_CLASS` (driver consumes mode env var defaulting to smoke AND at least one matching recipe does NOT override env to full AND does NOT opt out — the Z6-v2 anchor pattern). **Other verdicts** (`NO_SMOKE_FLAG`, `HARDCODES_SMOKE_RECIPE_OPTED_OUT`, `CONSUMES_ENV_DEFAULTS_FULL`, `CONSUMES_ENV_DEFAULTS_SMOKE_RECIPE_OK`, `CONSUMES_ENV_MULTI_KEY_DEFAULT_RECIPE_OK`, `CONSUMES_ENV_NO_HARDCODE`, `CONSUMES_ENV_UNKNOWN_DEFAULT`) are structurally legitimate or pending manual review and not refused. **Acceptance**: (a) same-line waiver `# DRIVER_MODE_HARDCODE_OK:<rationale>` on the --smoke line (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected); (b) file-level waiver `# DRIVER_MODE_HARDCODE_OK_FILE:<rationale>` in the driver's first 30 lines for rare drivers intentionally smoke-only that cannot afford even a per-line waiver. **Recipe-side fix**: the canonical fix for `CONSUMES_ENV_DEFAULTS_SMOKE_BUG_CLASS` is to either (i) set the mode env var explicitly (`Z6_TRAINER_MODE: "full"` + `SMOKE_ONLY: "0"`) in the recipe's `env_overrides` block, OR (ii) declare the recipe opted-out via `research_only: true` / `smoke_only: true` / `dispatch_enabled: false`, OR (iii) refactor the driver to support multi-key mode resolution (e.g. `Z6_TRAINER_MODE > SMOKE_ONLY > default`) with fail-loud warning when neither key is set. **Companion fixes landed in same commit batch**: Z6 driver `scripts/remote_lane_substrate_time_traveler_l5_z6.sh` now supports `Z6_TRAINER_MODE` env var (smoke|full) with explicit precedence over SMOKE_ONLY; Z6-v2 Wave 2 recipe `env_overrides` block now sets `Z6_TRAINER_MODE: "full"` + `SMOKE_ONLY: "0"`. Together they extinct the "driver + recipe + trainer wired but DON'T align on smoke vs full mode" bug class structurally at FIVE surfaces: driver/recipe mode-routing (#326) + trainer-side optimization (#270) + recipe-state vs trainer-impl (#240) + NVML env block (#244) + Tier-1 flag threading (#151) + required-input validation (#152). **Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (Z6 driver fixed + Z6-v2 Wave 2 recipe env_overrides set in same commit batch; cross-substrate audit JSON at `.omx/state/substrate_driver_mode_hardcode_audit_<utc>.json` confirms 0 bug-class drivers across 47 scanned substrate drivers). 324. `check_no_predicted_band_without_post_training_tier_c_validation` — META-FIX 2026-05-17 (per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE + empirical bug class anchor: C6 IBPS 22× miss). Refuses substrate operator-authorize recipes at `.omx/operator_authorize_recipes/*.yaml` that declare a `predicted_band` field WITHOUT one of: (a) `predicted_band_validation_status: validated_post_training` + post-training Tier-C density artifact path; (b) `predicted_band_validation_status: pending_post_training` + reactivation criteria pinned; (c) `research_only: true` OR `dispatch_enabled: false` (explicit non-promotable per CLAUDE.md "Substrate scaffolds MUST be COMPLETE or RESEARCH-ONLY"); (d) same-line `# PREDICTED_BAND_RANDOM_INIT_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the `predicted_band:` line (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). **Bug class anchor**: 2026-05-17 C6 IBPS recipe declared `predicted_band: [0.113, 0.163]` derived from Tier-C ACROSS_CLASS density `2.67e-5` measured via `tools/mdl_scorer_conditional_ablation.py --tier c` BEFORE training; actual 50ep Modal A10G smoke (call_id `fc-01KRW353MJJ9A6QW8H99QWZEMH`) landed `final_score = 3.04` — **22× outside** the predicted upper bound. Mechanism: 24-dim IB bottleneck destroys segmentation (score_seg=2.60 dominates 86% of total). Post-training Tier-C may differ" + "$0.76 sufficient is only UPPER bound on disconfirmation"); sister #836 EMPIRICALLY FALSIFIED. The R1 council Assumption-Adversary verdict was HARD-EARNED-EMPIRICALLY-VERIFIED. **Canonical helper** at `src/tac/optimization/tier_c_density_post_training_validator.py` (~600 LOC) provides `TierCDensityWithProvenance` + `PredictedBandWithValidation` frozen dataclasses with `__post_init__` invariants (random-init source auto-derives `phantom_random_init` status; UNKNOWN_PROVENANCE source REFUSED at construction; OPERATOR_WAIVED requires substantive rationale ≥8 chars; epochs_trained consistency with source). Premise-verifier arithmetic (`0.193 baseline - 0.47 claimed = -0.28`) is undefined and caught the bug class structurally per Catalog #229. Q4 HALT saved $0.70 GPU spend + prevented phantom anchor pollution of `reports/latest.md` + cost-band posterior + lane registry. **Companion runtime fix** at `tools/pre_entropy_substrate_pivot_prober.py` adds `_validate_substrate_bytes_ship_in_contest_archive` (default-active validator that rejects research-sidecar extensions + off-canonical-root .zip files + corrupt .zip files) + threads `validation_status` (`VALIDATED_CONTEST_MEMBER` / `REJECTED_RESEARCH_SIDECAR` / `UNVALIDATED`) + `validation_reason` + `evidence_grade_per_row` (`predicted` / `invalid_target`) through every `SubstrateProbeResult` + adds `probe_substrate_archive_member(archive_zip_path, member_name, ...)` as the canonical apples-to-apples method per Q4 Option B (probe an actual contest archive.zip member rather than a standalone sidecar) + adds `--skip-contest-member-validation` CLI flag for diagnostic-only opt-in + emits per-row evidence tag `[empirical:lzma_ratio_on_actual_member=<ratio>]` (validated) / `[invalid_target:research_sidecar_not_contest_archive_member]` (rejected) / `[unvalidated:diagnostic_only]` (opt-in skip) per Catalog #287. **Corrected artifact** at `.omx/state/wyner_ziv_deliverability/pre_entropy_candidate_substrates_corrected_20260517T215345.json` shows 11 REJECTED_RESEARCH_SIDECAR + 8 VALIDATED_CONTEST_MEMBER rows ALL at `deliverable_score_savings_estimate=0.0`; `recommended_q4_target_substrate=None`. **Acceptance**: (a) `validation_status=VALIDATED_CONTEST_MEMBER` for any positive deliverable; (b) per-row inline `# RESEARCH_SIDECAR_PHANTOM_SCORE_OK:<rationale>` waiver in `validation_reason` or sister `_phantom_score_waiver` field (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals + rationales <4 chars rejected so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive); (c) zero deliverable; (d) row absent from the artifact. 318. `check_master_gradient_raw_byte_authority_not_landed` — MASTER-GRADIENT RAW BYTE AUTHORITY self-protection 2026-05-17 (per the May 17 L5/Rule #6 rebaseline + `.omx/research/master_gradient_partner_wip_false_authority_review_20260517_codex.md`). Refuses authority-bearing source surfaces under `src/tac/master_gradient.py`, `tools/extract_master_gradient.py`, and `tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py` that expose raw archive-byte / bit master-gradient APIs incompatible with the contest's ZIP + entropy-coded packet contract. **Forbidden patterns**: `finite_difference_bit_flip` (raw bit/byte FD method); `(N_archive_bytes, 3)` / `(N_bytes, 3)` (raw byte response tensors); `gradient_array_path` (raw sidecar path); `byte_modifications: Mapping[int, float]` (raw byte modification API); `predict_delta_s(byte_modifications` (raw byte delta projection API); `latest_anchor_for_archive` (archive-sha anchor-presence rerank hook). **Bug class**: raw archive-byte / bit finite differences over a ZIP + entropy-coded packet are NOT local score derivatives — flipping a single byte of an LZMA-compressed stream corrupts the inflate path far beyond a single semantic edit. The L5/Rule #6 rebaseline (May 17 2026) flagged this as a partner-WIP false-authority class: helpers that *name* themselves master-gradient authority while exposing byte-level APIs cannot produce contest-faithful score derivatives. **Valid routing**: typed `CandidateModificationSpec` + `grammar_aware_operator` response rows that rebuild packet metadata (ZIP CRC / headers / lengths), prove inflate closure, prove byte-consumption closure, and emit axis-labelled result review per Catalog #127. Same-header waiver in the file's first 80 lines: `# MASTER_GRADIENT_RAW_AUTHORITY_OK:<rationale>`. Placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected so the gate's own docstring cannot self-waive. Acceptance cascade: (a) file lacks any forbidden token → PASS; (b) file has forbidden tokens AND has both `CandidateModificationSpec` + `grammar_aware_operator` AND no `finite_difference_bit_flip` → PASS (the typed-operator-spec path); (c) file carries the explicit waiver in the header → PASS for the deliberately diagnostic / non-authority artifact case. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — orchestrator callsite at `src/tac/preflight.py::preflight_all` is `strict=True`. Live count: 0. Sister of Catalog #127 (per-call-site custody routing — typed result review surface) + Catalog #270 (canonical dispatch optimization protocol — the umbrella that consumes the typed operator response rows) + Catalog #220 (substrate L1+ scaffold operational mechanism — the runtime-effect surface that proves the typed operator's bytes actually move score) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta drift detection — refuses CLAUDE.md `Live count: 0` claims when the underlying gate function actually returns >0). Together they extinct the partner-WIP false-authority master-gradient class structurally at FOUR surfaces: source-text byte-API ban (#318) + per-call-site custody routing (#127) + operational mechanism declaration (#220) + dispatch protocol umbrella (#270). Memory: `feedback_master_gradient_canonical_helper_landed_with_cathedral_autopilot_wirein_20260517.md` + cross-ref `.omx/research/master_gradient_partner_wip_false_authority_review_20260517_codex.md`. 315. `check_substrate_at_optimal_form_before_paid_dispatch` — META-FRAMING-CORRECTION self-protection 2026-05-17 (per operator directive of the same date + cumulative lessons from the 2026-05-13 → 2026-05-17 substrate dispatch waves). The structural failure this gate prevents: **empirically dispatching substrates at LIFTED-TRAINER form when operator's standing directives required OPTIMAL FORM at implementation**. Empirical receipts: (a) NSCS06 v6 → v7 = **44% improvement** (105.15 → 58.89, contest-CUDA) via cargo-cult-unwind methodology — the ONLY substrate that got iterated to optimal form before next paid dispatch wave; (b) **4-of-5 distinguishing-feature dispatch failures** this session (Wunderkind G1 v2 reducer / ATW v2 D4 cooperative-receiver / Z6 FiLM ego-motion / NSCS01 nullspace-split / NSCS06 v8 Path B) tested at lifted-trainer form, falsifying SPECIFIC IMPLEMENTATIONS not novel concepts; (c) operator's UNIQUE-AND-COMPLETE-PER-METHOD operating mode + 9-dim checklist + PR95-lesson-at-META-level + HNeRV parity discipline directives are enforced at the DESIGN-MEMO surface (Catalogs #290 + #294 + #303 + #305) but BYPASSED at the IMPLEMENTATION + DISPATCH surface. The gate scans `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` for in-scope substrate lanes (id-substring match against the canonical set mirroring Catalog #220 / #272 / #298 + extended with `time_traveler` / `wunderkind` / `atw_` / `z6_` / `z7_` / `z8_`) at L1+ with `impl_complete=true`; for each, looks up the latest council deliberation in `.omx/state/council_deliberation_posterior.jsonl` via the canonical `deferred_substrate_id` field (with optional `substrate_alias` / `substrate_aliases` lane-registry support for surface-name mapping); refuses any lane whose latest verdict is `PROCEED_WITH_REVISIONS` AND has no chronologically-later PROCEED-unconditional anchor AND has no opt-out. Acceptance cascade per CLAUDE.md "Substrate MUST be at OPTIMAL FORM before paid empirical dispatch" non-negotiable: (a) iteration anchor: chronologically-later PROCEED-unconditional council deliberation on same substrate (e.g. after cargo-cult-unwind iteration per NSCS06 v6→v7 pattern); (b) `research_only=true` (top-level field OR notes-token OR `target_modes` includes `research_only`/`research_substrate`); (c) `lane_class=substrate_engineering` (top-level field OR notes-token); (d) `archived=true` (top-level field OR notes-token `lane_state=archived` / `terminal_verdict`); (e) same-line waiver `# OPTIMAL_FORM_DISPATCH_OK:<rationale>` in lane notes / evidence (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected). 314. `check_no_subagent_files_touched_absorption_in_bare_commits` — COMMIT-SWAP-INVESTIGATION self-protection 2026-05-16 (per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable + 2 empirical anchors today: (a) WAVE-D 2c957c31e forensic finding 2026-05-15 (CODEX-FIX-WAVE absorbed DISPATCH-OPTIMIZATION's preflight.py + CLAUDE.md edits via DROP-FLAG-AND-RETRY pattern; closed by Catalog #289); (b) STC v2 FIX 2026-05-16 (commits `89d89c27e` "Harden L5 sideinfo and dispatch probe gates" + `c09c6e1c8` "Preserve probe outcomes landing memo" + `5562afc3c` "Preserve L5 v2 TT5L audit signal" absorbed STC v2 FIX subagent's edits to `src/tac/preflight.py` + `src/tac/tests/test_check_152_modal_mounted_input_extension.py` + `CLAUDE.md` + 3 driver scripts (`scripts/remote_lane_substrate_stc_v2.sh` / `a1_plus_lapose` / `a1_plus_wavelet_residual`) BEFORE STC v2 FIX's canonical serializer call ran; Catalog #157 `--expected-content-sha256` did NOT fire rc=4 because the absorbed content IS the working-tree state at lock-acquire time)). Refuses bare commits whose file list intersects an in-flight subagent's declared `files_touched` checkpoint within the preceding 60-minute window (excluding common-shared exempt files). Root cause: the operator's `/commit` slash command (commit-commands plugin at `~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/commit-commands/commands/commit.md`) does bare `git add` + `git commit` directly (NOT through `tools/subagent_commit_serializer.py`). When invoked while a sister subagent has uncommitted edits in the shared working tree, the bare `git add` packages whatever the LLM thinks is relevant — which can include the sister's still-in-flight edits. The resulting commit carries a terse subject like "Preserve probe outcomes landing memo" or "Harden L5 sideinfo and dispatch probe gates" and the sister's work is silently attributed to the wrong commit body. WHY existing protections did not extinct this: Catalog #117 (subagent commit serializer must be used) IS detecting the bare commits (12 violations in last 50 today, including all 3 absorption commits) but is wired warn-only per legacy backlog. It DETECTS but does NOT BLOCK; #157 / #174 / #216 / #289 (--expected-content-sha256 family) protect against sister-edits-during-lock-wait + drop-flag-and-retry, not bare-commit-absorbs-files-before-serializer; #230 (bulk-rewrite ownership map) covers the COMMIT-MESSAGE surface; not the bare-commit-with-terse-subject case; #302 (sister subagent scope overlap via checkpoint JSONL) covers the SIMULTANEOUS-EDIT surface (2+ subagents both editing same files); not the BARE-COMMIT-ABSORBS-IN-FLIGHT-FILES case. Catalog #314 closes the absorption-pattern surface as a dedicated detector scoped to the empirical fingerprint: a bare commit (not in serializer log, no absorption-specific waiver) containing files declared as `files_touched` by an in-flight subagent (status=in_progress) within a ~60-minute window preceding the commit timestamp. The detection is POST-HOC (the commit already landed). This is by design: we cannot block the `/commit` slash command from within preflight because preflight runs before commit, not before edit. The structural value is operator-facing alert + machine-readable evidence for the next subagent wave so absorption is detected within the same session rather than discovered weeks later when the operator wonders "why did THIS commit include files I never asked about?". Acceptance: (a) commit went through `tools/subagent_commit_serializer.py` (matched by serializer log via prefix-aware sha lookup mirroring Catalog #117 / #235 semantics); (b) commit message body carries `# ABSORPTION_PATTERN_OK:<rationale>` waiver (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected; rationale must be ≥4 chars). Exempt files (commonly multi-subagent state): `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` / `.omx/state/lane_maturity_audit.log` / `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger.jsonl` / `.omx/state/active_lane_dispatch_claims.md` / `.omx/state/commit-serializer.log` / `.omx/state/catalog-claim.log` / `.omx/state/subagent_progress.jsonl` / `.omx/state/continual_learning_posterior.jsonl` / `.omx/state/cost_band_posterior.jsonl` / `.omx/state/next_catalog_number.txt` / `.omx/state/probe_outcomes.jsonl` / `MEMORY.md`. Together they extinct the multi-subagent edit/commit collision class at SEVEN surfaces: edit-time-checkpoint (#302) + edit-time-bulk-op (#230) + commit-time-pre-pre-lock (#157) + commit-time-staged (#216) + commit-time-lock-arbitration (#117) + post-resolution-residual-marker (#248) + bare-commit-absorbs-in-flight-files (#314). Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — initial live count at landing: 9 absorption-signature violations across 6 unique bare commits in last 50; follow-up review tightened status scope to in_progress only, current count 8. Strict-flip pending operator-routed audit + waiver backfill on absorption-anchor commits OR an absorption-clean window (50 fresh commits all going through canonical serializer). 38 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_314_subagent_files_touched_absorption_in_bare_commits.py` covering live-repo regression guard (≤30 ceiling), helper unit tests (ISO-UTC parser / waiver token detection with placeholder rejection / exempt-files set / waiver-tokens tuple), loader unit tests (serializer hash loader / malformed-JSON tolerance / missing-file fail-OPEN / in-flight loader with exempt filtering / space-separated legacy format / multiple rows per subagent), end-to-end (clean repo / canonical absorption pattern detected / serializer-log commit skipped / absorption-pattern waiver accepted / no-serializer waiver rejected / placeholder waiver rejected / commits outside 60-min window skipped / commits before checkpoint skipped / no-file-overlap skipped / exempt-files-overlap skipped / dedup per commit-subagent pair / multi-subagent collisions counted separately), strict-mode (raises with Catalog #314 message / silent on clean), orchestrator wire-in warn-only regression guard + Catalog #185 sister-gate-callable regression guard, anchor-specific live-repo regression guard pinning today's STC v2 + probe_outcomes + L5 + Z6 absorption pattern empirical effectiveness at landing. **Lane maturity registry (2026-04-30, STRICT @ 0):**

90. `check_lane_registry_consistent` — `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` must parse, schema_version must match, no duplicate lane ids, every lane has all 7 gates present, stored `level` matches computed-from-gates, every file-path-looking evidence string points to a real file. Mutations only via `tools/lane_maturity.py mark/unmark/add-lane`. Memory: `project_lane_maturity_harness_landed_20260430` + `feedback_production_hardened_standard_definition_20260430`. Live count: 0.

**Promotion path:** A new check starts `strict=False`, the live violation count is fixed across the codebase, then it is flipped `strict=True` in `preflight_all()`. The promotion pattern is documented in commit 7f2740e4 (the strict-flip of checks 1-11). Reverting any strict check will fail at commit/PR/run time.

## Lane maturity registry — non-negotiable

Every lane MUST be registered in `.omx/state/lane_registry.json` via `tools/lane_maturity.py`. Claiming Level 2 or Level 3 without a corresponding `mark` command is FORBIDDEN. Subagents shipping a lane MUST run `python tools/lane_maturity.py mark <lane> --gate <gate> --evidence <path>` for each gate they hit. Preflight Check 90 (`check_lane_registry_consistent`) fails STRICT if the registry is inconsistent (level/gates mismatch, duplicate ids, missing gates, file-path evidence pointing to non-existent files).

The 7 gates and their meaning are defined in `feedback_production_hardened_standard_definition_20260430`:

1. `impl_complete` — production code lands; no `NotImplementedError`; all CLI flags wired
2. `real_archive_empirical` — real-archive empirical measurement on Lane G v3 anchor (or equivalent); tagged `[empirical:<artifact>]`
3. `contest_cuda` — actual `[contest-CUDA]` score (Vast.ai 4090 / Modal A100); NEVER MPS, NEVER `[contest-CPU advisory]`
4. `strict_preflight` — STRICT preflight check covering the lane's bug class
5. `three_clean_review` — 3-clean-pass adversarial review counter at 3/3
6. `memory_entry` — memory file documenting the empirical result + cross-refs
7. `deploy_runbook` — `scripts/remote_lane_<id>.sh` + heartbeat + watchdog + harvest

Computed level: 0 = 0 gates, 1 = ≥1 gate, 2 = `impl_complete` AND `real_archive_empirical` true, 3 = ALL 7 true. A lane with 4 gates true but missing `impl_complete` is still Level 1 (not Level 2). The CLI computes this for you — never set `level` by hand.

CLI usage:

```bash
# Audit table
python tools/lane_maturity.py audit

# Mark a gate satisfied
python tools/lane_maturity.py mark lane_g_v3 --gate contest_cuda \
    --evidence "1.05 [contest-CUDA] reports/raw/2026-04-29-..."

# Validate (preflight Check 90 also runs this)
python tools/lane_maturity.py validate

# Regenerate reports/lane_maturity.md
python tools/lane_maturity.py report

# Register a new lane at Level 0
python tools/lane_maturity.py add-lane lane_new --name "New Lane" --phase 2
```

Every mutation appends a JSONL record to `.omx/state/lane_maturity_audit.log` for forensics.

**Lifecycle discipline (non-negotiable):**

- **Pre-registration is mandatory.** The moment a lane has a name and a council/design verdict — even if it's only a sketch — it MUST be `add-lane`'d at Level 0. This includes in-flight subagent lanes, future-design lanes, and forensic-investigation lanes. Pre-registration enables the audit table to distinguish IN-FLIGHT vs LANDED vs SKETCH.
- **Mark gates as evidence is produced, NOT after-the-fact.** The moment a council Round-N CLEAN landing happens, mark `three_clean_review`. The moment a remote_lane script lands, mark `deploy_runbook`. Batch-backfilling stale evidence is a code smell.
- **KILLED lanes get registry entries too.** Mark with `--gate three_clean_review --evidence "<council ref>"` and add `--notes "Reactivation: <criteria>"`. Do NOT just exclude killed lanes — the registry is the single source of truth for what we have considered, including kills.
- **Backfill-when-discovered is acceptable.** When this rule is violated by an earlier subagent, a maturity-discipline pass that backfills evidence is the correct remedy. The audit log records the backfill timestamp; no harm done.
- **Lifecycle: SKETCH (L0) → SCAFFOLD (L1) → INTEGRATION (L2) → FULL PRODUCTION HARDENED (L3).** The ONLY currently-Level-3 lane is `lane_g_v3` (1.05 [contest-CUDA]). That fact is the standard-bearer for the rest of the registry.
- **Audit before commit.** Before any commit that adds a lane or marks a gate, run `python tools/lane_maturity.py validate` — Check 90 STRICT enforces this at commit time, but catching it earlier is cheaper than a re-stage.

339. `check_modal_dispatcher_registers_call_id_before_successful_exit` — SILENT-NO-SPAWN-STRUCTURAL-EXTINCTION self-protection 2026-05-19 (per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE *"such bugs must be permanently fixed and self-protected against"* + 3 empirical anchors today: Z6 Wave 2 4c dispatch / STC v2 dispatch / STC sister dispatch — ALL exhibited the same silent-no-spawn pattern). **Bug class anchor**: `experiments/modal_train_lane.py::main` called `fn.spawn()` successfully (paid GPU meter started on Modal account) THEN the canonical `register_dispatched_call_id` call was wrapped in `try/except Exception as exc: print(WARNING)` at lines 1921-1960 — silently swallowing any fcntl-lock contention / disk-full / sister-subagent edit / `CallIdLedgerCorruptError`. The wrapper exited rc=0 with no ledger row → harvester invisible per CLAUDE.md "Modal `.spawn()` HARVEST OR LOSE" non-negotiable → operator effort wasted (recipe flips + operator-frontier-override + dispatch invocations all landed with zero downstream effect). Refuses any state of `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` where the post-spawn registration call sits inside a silent-swallow `try/except` handler (broad `Exception` / `BaseException` / bare `except`) that lacks `raise` / `sys.exit` / re-raise. Also refuses absence of registration call entirely. **Companion 4-layer fix**: (1) NEW canonical helper `register_dispatched_call_id_fail_closed(...)` in `src/tac/deploy/modal/call_id_ledger.py` raises new `LedgerRegistrationFailedError` on any failure; on failure, best-effort writes a `recover_<call_id>_<uuid>.json` dump to `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger_recovery_tmp/` so `tools/harvest_modal_calls.py --recover-from-tmp` can replay the dispatched event later. (2) `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` wrapper replaced silent swallow with `register_dispatched_call_id_fail_closed` + explicit `except LedgerRegistrationFailedError: sys.exit(13)` per Catalog #339 fail-closed contract. (3) NEW sister mitigation in `tools/operator_authorize.py::_dispatch_modal`: after `_modal_output_indicates_spawned_call` confirms `.spawn()` happened, extract `call_id=fc-...` from stdout AND poll the canonical ledger via new `poll_ledger_for_call_id(...)` helper (10s window); raise `SystemExit` citing Catalog #339 if the ledger row never appears. (4) THIS STRICT preflight gate AST-scans `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` for the silent-swallow regression pattern. **Acceptance**: (a) canonical fail-closed helper used + no try/except OR try/except handler invokes `raise` / `sys.exit` / `SystemExit`; (b) narrow `except` (e.g. `except RuntimeError`) accepted as not-broad-swallow; (c) same-line `# SILENT_NO_SPAWN_LEDGER_SWALLOW_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the `def main` line OR `try:` line with non-placeholder rationale ≥4 chars (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` / short rationales rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). **STRICT-from-byte-one** per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (the 4-layer fix lands in same commit batch; the wrapper now calls `register_dispatched_call_id_fail_closed` and explicitly catches+sys.exit(13)).
