Together they extinct the orphan-paid-Modal-dispatch bug class STRUCTURALLY at FOUR surfaces: register pending row BEFORE submit (#143) + canonical ledger source of truth (#245) + fcntl-locked + strict-load discipline (#131/#138) + fail-closed registration AFTER spawn (#339 — THIS gate). Plus 15 sister tests in `src/tac/tests/test_modal_train_lane_silent_no_spawn_fix.py` covering canonical helper happy path / re-raises on failure / last-resort tmp dump written / diagnostic when dump also fails / exception chaining / poll returns True when row present / poll timeout / poll tolerates corrupt ledger / wrapper uses fail-closed helper / wrapper sys.exit(13) on failure / no legacy silent swallow / operator_authorize has poll helper + extract helper + polls ledger + raises SystemExit. 35/35 tests pass. **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = N/A; hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE** (THIS gate ensures no dispatch can land an orphan-paid-Modal-call invisible to the harvester); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE** (canonical ledger remains the SINGLE source of truth for every Modal dispatch's lifecycle); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = N/A. 341. `check_cathedral_consumer_mps_prescreen_routing_carries_canonical_markers` — CATALOG-317-SCOPE-NARROWING-MPS-OPT-IN self-protection 2026-05-19 (per operator's earlier paradigm-shift directive + today's empirical MPS-VIABLE landing per `feedback_mps_phase_b_options_b_plus_c_completion_landed_20260519.md` 3-component aggregate gap 0.072% / 69x below 5% LOCAL_MPS_TRAIN_VIABLE threshold + the new MPS-prescreen cathedral consumer landed today commit `a753b70d5` per `feedback_mps_prescreen_cathedral_consumer_wire_in_landed_20260519.md`). **SCOPE-NARROWING SISTER OF Catalog #317**: Catalog #317 covers the DIRECT `_dispatch_local_mps` / `_dispatch_local_cpu` surface in `tools/operator_authorize.py`; Catalog #341 covers the cathedral-consumer-recommended routing surface — refuses any `src/tac/cathedral_consumers/*` package that exposes a routing recommendation (in-scope established by `recommended_route` field OR `ROUTE_LOCAL_MPS_PRESCREEN` / `ROUTE_PAID_CUDA_AUTHORITATIVE` constants) WITHOUT all 3 canonical non-promotable markers in every routing-branch return value: (1) `predicted_delta_adjustment=0.0` (routing is NOT a score signal per `CathedralConsumerContract`); (2) `promotable=False` (per Catalog #127/#192/#317 non-promotable-by-construction); (3) `axis_tag="[predicted]"` (per Catalog #287/#323 canonical Provenance). Pure diagnostic consumers (`mps_diagnostic_consumer` / `mps_gap_experiment_consumer`) have no routing recommendation field and are out-of-scope by construction. Bug class: a routing recommender that omits the canonical markers can silently bias the cathedral autopilot ranker to promote a routing recommendation to a score signal OR to dispatch a routed candidate through a contest-axis surface without paired Linux x86_64 + NVIDIA evidence (Catalog #192 violation). Per CLAUDE.md "MPS auth eval is NOISE" + "Submission auth eval — BOTH CPU AND CUDA" non-negotiables: the markers are STRUCTURAL protection that the routing recommendation cannot leak into promotion. 356. `check_per_axis_decomposition_carries_canonical_provenance` — WAVE-1-DIM-3-PROTOCOL-AND-HELPER 2026-05-20 (operator blanket approval 2026-05-20 per CATHEDRAL-SMARTER-DESIGN-MEMO Dimension 3 Steps 3.1-3.3). Sister of Catalog #335 at the per-axis emission sub-surface. Where #335 enforces the canonical Protocol contract at the package level, #356 enforces the canonical Provenance shape on the OPTIONAL `predicted_axis_decomposition` field a consumer MAY emit when it has per-axis (seg, pose, archive bytes) signal. **Bug class anchor**: a cathedral consumer that emits `predicted_axis_decomposition` without canonical Provenance can silently leak a per-axis prediction into the autopilot ranker's downstream consumers (Pareto polytope solver per Dim 1 Phase 4, bit-allocator) WITHOUT the canonical axis_tag + hardware_substrate + evidence_grade triple per Catalog #323. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden empirical-claim-without-evidence-tag" + the meta-class extinction at Catalog #323, the canonical Provenance is the structural protection that per-axis predictions cannot be silently promoted to score signals or used without proper non-promotable markers per Catalog #341. **The 3-deliverable bundle**: (1) NEW frozen dataclass `AxisDecomposition` at `src/tac/cathedral/consumer_contract.py` carrying `predicted_d_seg_delta` + `predicted_d_pose_delta` + `predicted_archive_bytes_delta` (signed int) + `axis_tag` + `canonical_provenance: Mapping[str, Any]` with `__post_init__` invariants (NaN / inf rejection / Mapping enforcement / int-not-bool archive bytes) + canonical `as_dict` / `from_dict` round-trip; (2) NEW canonical helper module `tac.score_composition` (~440 LOC at `src/tac/score_composition/__init__.py`) exposing `compose_score_from_axes(decomposition, *, current_archive_bytes, current_d_pose, per_axis_residual=None) -> ComposedScoreDelta` (applies canonical contest formula `S = 100 * d_seg + sqrt(10 * d_pose) + 25 * archive_bytes / 37545489`; the pose contribution is the DIFFERENCE of two `sqrt(10 * d_pose)` calls because the formula's pose term is non-linear) + `compose_scalar_delta(...)` backward-compat scalar API + `load_baseline_pose_from_canonical_frontier_pointer(...)` Catalog #343 sister + canonical constants (`CANONICAL_SEG_MULTIPLIER=100.0` / `CANONICAL_POSE_SQRT_INNER=10.0` / `CANONICAL_RATE_MULTIPLIER=25.0` / `CANONICAL_RATE_DENOM_BYTES=37_545_489`); (3) ranker per-axis composition wire-in extension at `tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py::_invoke_consumer_safely` adding `_compose_per_axis_decomposition_if_present(contribution, candidate)` helper that auto-detects + composes per-axis decompositions (consumers without per-axis emit None per Catalog #341 backward compat; malformed emissions surface as `error` field rather than crashing the ranker). **Acceptance for the gate**: (a) consumer's `__init__.py` does not reference the trigger token `predicted_axis_decomposition` (out-of-scope); (b) consumer's source contains ANY canonical Provenance token (`build_provenance_for_predicted` / `build_provenance_for_archive_member` / `build_provenance_for_macos_cpu_advisory` / `build_provenance_for_mps_proxy` / `build_provenance_for_research_sidecar` / `build_provenance_aggregate` / `from tac.provenance` / `provenance_to_dict` / `canonical_provenance=` kwarg assignment); (c) same-line `# PER_AXIS_DECOMPOSITION_PROVENANCE_OK:<rationale>` waiver on the trigger-token line with non-placeholder rationale (≥4 chars; placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Detection scans `src/tac/cathedral_consumers/*/  __init__.py`; `__pycache__` + `tests` subdirs exempt. **Initial wire-in is WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (no production consumer migrated to Tier B yet per Dim 3 Step 3.4 / Dim 6 Step 6.5; the canonical reference fixture `_example_consumer` does not yet emit per-axis decomposition). Strict-flip atomic with the first NEW per-axis emission landing (which by construction will carry canonical Provenance because the gate fires structurally before commit). Sister of Catalog #335 (canonical Protocol contract at package surface; #356 is the per-axis sub-surface) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection) + Catalog #323 (canonical Provenance umbrella) + Catalog #125 (6-hook wire-in non-negotiable) + Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT callsites have CLAUDE.md row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: 0 verified empirically) + Catalog #341 (Tier-A canonical-routing-markers; #356 preserves Tier-A backward-compat by gating on TRIGGER TOKEN presence, not forcing emission). Together they enable Dim 3 Step 3.4 (convert 3-5 high-EV consumers to per-axis emission) + Dim 1 Phase 4 (Dykstra alternating-projections on Pareto polytope) + Dim 6 Step 6.5 (Tier B score-contributing canonical contract sister). **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = **ACTIVE** (per-axis decomposition IS the canonical sensitivity surface at the consumer boundary); hook #2 Pareto constraint = **ACTIVE** (per-axis enables Dykstra alternating projections to operate on actual seg/pose/rate polytope; Dim 1 Phase 4 enabler); hook #3 bit-allocator = **ACTIVE** (`predicted_archive_bytes_delta` IS the bit-allocator's primary signal); hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE PRIMARY** (composition happens at dispatch decision via the helper extension); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE** (per-axis posterior anchors enable per-axis Bayesian updates); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = **ACTIVE** (per-axis residuals disambiguate which axis a prediction was wrong on vs scalar residual which hides axis attribution — PR97 anti-pattern empirical anchor). Tests: 39 in `src/tac/tests/test_score_composition.py` (AxisDecomposition contract / canonical formula round-trips / scalar API match / Provenance propagation / frontier-pointer integration / backward-compat ranker helper with None handling / malformed emission surfaced as error) + 18 in `src/tac/tests/test_check_356_per_axis_decomposition_provenance.py` (live-repo regression / synthetic violation / canonical Provenance accepted / waiver semantics with placeholder rejection / exempt subdirs / multi-violation aggregation / strict raise / orchestrator wire-in regression / Catalog #185 sister-callable). Mission contribution per Catalog #300: `frontier_breaking_enabler` (the per-axis primitive is the foundation Dim 1 Phase 4 + Dim 3 Step 3.4 + Dim 6 Step 6.5 build on). 357. `check_cathedral_consumer_tier_b_declares_canonical_contract` — DUAL-TIER ARCHITECTURE Dim 6 Step 6.3 self-protection 2026-05-20 (per CATHEDRAL-SMARTER-DESIGN-MEMO `.omx/research/cathedral_autopilot_smarter_design_blueprint_20260520T130325Z.md` Dimension 6 + operator blanket approval 2026-05-20). Resolves the operator-mental-model gap surfaced by WIRE-IN-RIGOR audit: 44+ cathedral consumers fire per loop iteration but per Catalog #341 ALL return `predicted_delta_adjustment=0.0`. The dual-tier system (Tier A observability-only vs Tier B score-contributing) makes this distinction STRUCTURAL rather than tribal-knowledge. Refuses any `src/tac/cathedral_consumers/*` package that declares `CONSUMER_TIER = ConsumerTier.TIER_B_SCORE_CONTRIBUTING` at module level WITHOUT all of: (1) import of `ConsumerTier` from `tac.cathedral.consumer_contract` (required for the declaration to resolve); (2) `provenance` substring (Catalog #323 canonical Provenance must be threaded into every contribution return value); (3) `promotable` substring AND `False` literal (Tier B contributes to RANKING but NEVER to PROMOTION per CLAUDE.md "Submission auth eval — BOTH CPU AND CUDA"); (4) NO forbidden `axis_tag="[predicted]"` / `axis_tag='[predicted]'` / `'axis_tag': '[predicted]'` / `"axis_tag": "[predicted]"` (Tier B requires empirically-grounded axis token; `[predicted]` re-collapses into Tier A semantics). File-level waiver `# CATHEDRAL_CONSUMER_TIER_B_DEFERRED_OK:<rationale>` in `__init__.py` first 60 lines for Tier B scaffolds awaiting Phase 2 wire-in per Dim 6 Step 6.5 (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` / rationales <4 chars rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Reference packages with `_`-prefix names exempt by construction. Tier A consumers (default; omit `CONSUMER_TIER` OR set to `TIER_A_OBSERVABILITY_ONLY`) are out of scope — covered by sister Catalog #341 routing-markers gate. STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" + "Strict-flip atomicity rule" non-negotiables — live count at landing: 0 (no Tier B consumers exist yet; the dual-tier landing is foundation-only). The gate fires structurally the moment a Tier B consumer lands without the canonical contract. Sister of Catalog #341 (Tier A canonical-routing-markers gate; #357 is the Tier B sister extension), Catalog #335 (canonical Protocol contract auto-discovery), Catalog #323 (canonical Provenance umbrella that #357 enforces threading into Tier B contributions), Catalog #356 (Tier B per-axis decomposition prerequisite; depends on Dim 3 Step 3.1 landing), Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection), Catalog #125 (6-hook wire-in non-negotiable; #357 is the structural protection that hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch can SAFELY accept non-zero adjustments only from Tier B canonical-contract-compliant consumers), Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT callsites have CLAUDE.md row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate), Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: 0 verified empirically). Together they extinct the Tier-B-without-canonical-contract bug class STRUCTURALLY at FIVE surfaces: canonical Protocol (#335) + Tier A routing-markers (#341) + Tier B contract (#357) + per-axis Provenance (#356) + canonical Provenance umbrella (#323). Companion canonical helper `tac.cathedral.consumer_contract.validate_tier_b_contribution(contribution_dict)` provides the runtime per-call validator (sister of the per-source STRICT preflight gate); together they close the Tier B canonical-contract bug class at BOTH the per-call and per-source surfaces. **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = N/A; hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE PRIMARY** (THIS gate IS the structural protection that hook #4 ranker can SAFELY accept non-zero score deltas from Tier B consumers without leaking speculation into promotion); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = N/A; hook #6 probe-disambiguator = **ACTIVE** (the canonical Tier A vs Tier B classification IS the disambiguator between observability-only-routing vs empirically-grounded-ranking-signal). Mission contribution per Catalog #300: `apparatus_maintenance` (operator-mental-model gap closure; enables future score-mutating consumers without violating canonical contract; the immediate score-lowering value is N/A but the structural foundation unblocks Dim 6 Step 6.5 + Dim 3 Step 3.4 sister landings). 358. `check_recipe_workspace_output_path_canonical_or_modal_aware` — WAVE-3-HARDEN-1-MASTER-GRADIENT-TMP-PATH-BUG-CLASS-EXTINCTION self-protection 2026-05-20 (operator NON-NEGOTIABLE "must fix and harden all"). Sister of Catalog #204 (per-driver Modal-aware OUTPUT path fix) at the recipe-level surface. Refuses tool-dispatch recipes (`dispatch_kind: tool` per Catalog #270) whose `env_overrides` block emits an OUTPUT env var (`*_NPY` / `*_OUTPUT_DIR` / `*_OUTPUT_*` / `*_OUT_*`) with a value under `/workspace/pact/` UNLESS the corresponding `lane_script` / `remote_driver` implements the canonical Catalog #204 3-branch Modal-aware override (`if [ "${MODAL_RUNTIME:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -d "/modal_results" ] && [ -n "${INSTANCE_JOB_ID:-}" ]; then ...=/modal_results/${INSTANCE_JOB_ID}/output/...; fi`) OR the env_var line carries a `# WORKSPACE_OUTPUT_PATH_OK:<rationale>` waiver. **Bug class anchor**: WAVE-3-OP3 dispatch `fc-01KS2Z2WJQW532A9226JAVQM8Y` (2026-05-20T15:11:22Z) failed rc=1 at 9.74s because the recipe's `MASTER_GRADIENT_OUTPUT_NPY=/workspace/pact/.omx/state/...` resolved to `/tmp/pact/.omx/state/...` on the Modal worker (Modal mounts working tree under `/tmp/pact/`, NOT `/workspace/pact/`) and triggered `tools/extract_master_gradient.py:2369-2373` /tmp refusal per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden /tmp paths in any persisted artifact" (Catalog #220 transient-evidence trap). Sister cross-driver expansion landed at OP3-RETRY commit `75d39f32e` for the CUDA T4 recipe; THIS lane lands the L2 sister CPU recipe driver fix + mps_gap driver fix + L3 META gate (THIS catalog #) so the bug class is STRUCTURALLY extinct at the recipe-level surface. Detection: parses `env_overrides:` block for OUTPUT-suffix env vars with `/workspace/pact/` values; for each, loads the driver via `lane_script` / `remote_driver` and checks for the canonical pattern within ±10 lines of the env var reassignment. Acceptance cascade: (a) no `/workspace/pact/.../<OUTPUT>` env var; (b) all such OUTPUT env vars covered by canonical 3-branch driver override OR `/modal_results/` reassignment; (c) recipe is `dispatch_enabled: false` OR `research_only: true` (transparent non-dispatchable per CLAUDE.md "Substrate scaffolds MUST be COMPLETE or RESEARCH-ONLY"); (d) same-line waiver `# WORKSPACE_OUTPUT_PATH_OK:<rationale>` with non-placeholder rationale ≥4 chars (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). The equation's mathematical predicate is REPLACEMENT savings `ΔS = -25 * (N_codebook - K_seed) / 37_545_489`; residual-hybrid contexts ADD bytes via residual encoding rather than REMOVE bytes via direct substitution — the equation's `_INCLUDED_CONTEXTS` set (`intermediate_transform_quantizer` / `chroma_lut_replacement` / `nscs06_v8_chroma_lut` / etc.) explicitly enumerates the LOOKUP-TABLE-replacement contexts the equation predicts for. 360. `check_modal_dispatcher_pre_spawn_fatal_observability` — PRE-SPAWN-FATAL-OBSERVABILITY-EXTINCTION self-protection 2026-05-21 (per OVERNIGHT-J STC v2 5th consecutive silent-no-spawn anchor `.omx/research/stc_v2_ratify_or_defer_path_b_dispatch_landed_20260521.md` + CLAUDE.md "Modal `.spawn()` HARVEST OR LOSE" + "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiables + OVERNIGHT-HH bug-audit-fix-cascade memory `feedback_overnight_hh_comprehensive_bug_audit_fix_cascade_landed_20260521.md`). Sister of Catalog #339 at the **PRE**-spawn surface. Where #339 catches silent-swallow registration failures AFTER `fn.spawn()` returns, #360 catches the upstream silent-no-spawn pattern: every `sys.exit(...)` FATAL path inside `experiments/modal_train_lane.py::main()` (decorated with `@app.local_entrypoint()`) that fires BEFORE `fn.spawn()` queues a task leaves Modal in "stopped/0 tasks" state with NO canonical-ledger row and NO recovery dump. **Bug class anchor**: STC v2 dispatched 5 consecutive times 2026-05-16 → 2026-05-21 all crashed silent-no-spawn while every OTHER substrate dispatched cleanly via the same `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` + `tools/operator_authorize.py` code path — empirical proof the bug is upstream of `fn.spawn()` in main's sys.exit paths NOT in the post-spawn registration surface that Catalog #339 protects. AST-scans `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` for every `sys.exit(...)` / `exit(...)` / `quit(...)` Call node inside the `@app.local_entrypoint() def main()` function body upstream of `fn.spawn(...)`; refuses any that is NOT preceded (within ±10 lines) by a `register_pre_spawn_fatal(...)` or `_pre_spawn_fatal(...)` (inline wrapper) Call. **Canonical helper**: NEW `tac.deploy.modal.call_id_ledger.register_pre_spawn_fatal(label, lane_id, fatal_reason, *, exit_code, sys_exit_line_number, sys_exit_helper_source, ...)` writes a `pre_spawn_fatal` event (new `EVENT_PRE_SPAWN_FATAL` taxonomy member + new `STATUS_PRE_SPAWN_FATAL` terminal status) to the canonical `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger.jsonl` BEFORE the caller's sys.exit. Synthetic call_id format `pre_spawn_fatal_<label_first_64>_<utc_compact>` so 5 consecutive failures on the same label produce 5 distinct rows. Helper is fail-OPEN (best-effort tmp-dump fallback at `.omx/state/modal_call_id_ledger_recovery_tmp/`) so observability cannot block the FATAL exit it instruments. Same-line waiver `# PRE_SPAWN_FATAL_OBSERVABILITY_OK:<rationale>` on the sys.exit line (rationale ≥4 chars; placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). Sister of Catalog #339 (post-spawn registration fail-closed) + Catalog #245 (canonical Modal call_id ledger this gate's helper writes to) + Catalog #131/#138 (fcntl-locked + strict-load discipline the canonical ledger inherits) + Catalog #143 (paid-job register-before-submit at the operator-authorize sister surface) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection sister discipline) + Catalog #176 (META-meta STRICT-callsite-has-CLAUDE.md-row that THIS entry satisfies) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta Live count: 0 verified empirically). Together they extinct the silent-no-spawn bug class STRUCTURALLY at TWO orthogonal surfaces: pre-spawn-sys.exit (#360) + post-spawn-registration-swallow (#339). **STRICT-from-byte-one** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 0 (the fix routes 9 HIGH-RISK pre-spawn sys.exit paths in main() — lane script existence + gpu validation + cost-band trainer/epochs/batch-size validation + git custody + require_clean_head dirty tree + trainer_module_path normalization + trainer_module conflict + substrate_trainer missing + extra-mount-payload collection + dispatch_claims missing — through the inline `_pre_spawn_fatal` helper which delegates to `register_pre_spawn_fatal`). 17 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_360_pre_spawn_fatal_observability.py` covering live-repo regression / synthetic bare-sys.exit-flagged / multi-violation aggregation / register_pre_spawn_fatal Call accepted / inline `_pre_spawn_fatal(...)` accepted / sys.exit-after-spawn NOT flagged (Catalog #339 scope) / no-local-entrypoint silent / missing target silent / same-line waiver / placeholder rationale rejected / short rationale rejected / strict-mode raises with Catalog #360 message / strict-silent-on-clean / orchestrator wire-in strict=True regression / Catalog #185 sister-callable / register_pre_spawn_fatal exists + canonical kwarg signature. **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = N/A; hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE** (every pre-spawn fatal becomes a canonical ledger row queryable by the cathedral autopilot ranker + harvester); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE** (pre-spawn-fatal rows feed the canonical posterior so the operator-routable diagnostic + the cost-band reconciliation surface inherits the signal); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = **ACTIVE** (pre-spawn-fatal event-type IS the canonical disambiguator between silent-no-spawn vs cleanly-dispatched-but-failed). 370. `check_no_pr_submission_without_canonical_compliance_verdict` — CANONICAL-SUBMISSION-PIPELINE PHASE 8 (Layer 6) STRICT preflight gate per `.omx/research/canonical_submission_pipeline_specification_memo_20260526.md` §3 Phase 8 + operator NON-NEGOTIABLE 9th amendment Layer 6 + Phase 5 op-routable Option A resolution (catalog # #370 claimed at session start commit `82206aec8` reserved for THIS gate). Layer 6 STRICT gate closes the canonical-submission-pipeline 7-layer architecture at the structural enforcement surface; sister of Phase 7 paired_auth_eval (Layer 5) + Phase 6 compliance (Layer 4) + Phase 5 linter (Layer 3) + Phase 4 builder (Layer 2) + Phase 3 archive_grammar (Layer 1) + Phase 2 compression_pipeline (Layer 0). Refuses any `submissions/*/` directory (excluding `submissions/exact_current/` per CLAUDE.md mutation frontier; excluding `_intake_` vendored clones) containing PR-facing artifacts (`PR_BODY*.md` / `PR_DESCRIPTION.md` / `README.md` carrying PR-title or PR-body sentinel tokens like `## Submission` / `## Score` / `## Reproducibility` / `## Attribution` / `## Citations` / `[contest-CPU]` / `[contest-CUDA]` / `commaai/comma_video_compression_challenge`) WITHOUT all 4 canonical verdict sidecars present + clean: (1) **Phase 4 builder** `SubmissionBundleResult` (overall_pass=true); (2) **Phase 5 linter** `LintVerdict` (overall_clean=true); (3) **Phase 6 compliance** `ComplianceVerdict` (overall_clean=true); (4) **Phase 7 paired_auth_eval** `PairedAuthEvalVerdict` (verdict=PAIRED_PASS). Search cascade per phase: (i) inside `submissions/<sub>/`; (ii) inside `experiments/results/<sub>*/`; (iii) inside `reports/pr_pre_submission/` (Phase 6 convention); most-recent-mtime wins. Acceptance cascade: (a) all 4 sidecars present + clean (the canonical default-path); (b) same-line `# PR_SUBMISSION_NO_CANONICAL_COMPLIANCE_OK:<rationale>` waiver in README.md or PR_BODY*.md first 30 lines (non-placeholder rationale ≥4 chars; placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive); (c) submission is not PR-facing (no PR_BODY*.md AND README.md lacks PR-facing sentinel tokens). Bug class anchor: 2026-05-19 PR101 submission_dir was hand-edited across 4 sister subagents (Slot K + L + M + J) over ~3h wall-clock with no canonical pipeline structurally enforcing the 4-verdict chain. Per Phase 1 spec memo §1 Why: "6+ phases × multiple iterations × manual hand-editing per surface = the canonical anti-pattern." The structural extinction at this gate prevents future PR111+ submissions from shipping without all 4 canonical verdicts being present + clean. Per CLAUDE.md "Operator gates must be wired and used" non-negotiable: every missing canonical verdict raises with a named blocker citing WHICH verdict is missing AND the canonical CLI command to generate it (operator-actionable remediation hint per Catalog #270 sister discipline). Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" + Phase 1 spec memo §3 Phase 8 acceptance contract; strict-flip pending Phase 10 PR101 baseline migration + first NEW submission both PACKET-CLEAN. Live count at landing: 4 (`submissions/a1/` + `submissions/pr106_latent_sidecar_r2/` + `submissions/pr106_latent_sidecar_r2_pr101_grammar/` + `submissions/robust_current/` — the 5 known submission packets with README.md + PR-body shape that predate the canonical pipeline; matches Phase 1 spec memo prediction). Operator-routable resolution per submission: (a) invoke each canonical helper to emit the 4 verdict sidecars; (b) add the substantive waiver if the submission predates the canonical pipeline AND will not be re-submitted; (c) strip PR-facing sentinel tokens from README.md if the submission is research-artifact-only. Sister of Catalog #146 (contest-compliant inflate runtime template; design surface) + Catalog #205 (canonical select_inflate_device; per-file surface) + Catalog #208 (docs no-local-absolute-paths; per-file surface) + Catalog #295 (PYTHONPATH self-containment; per-file surface) + Catalog #335 (canonical cathedral consumer contract; companion sister) + Catalog #341 (Tier A canonical-routing markers; sister consumer) + Catalog #361 (Modal artifact filter preserves submission_dir; META surface) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection) + Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT-callsites-have-CLAUDE.md-row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: warn-only baseline verified empirically) + Catalog #299 (catalog quota brake under 400; current 370 well under) + Catalog #348 (retroactive sweep for new gate; companion memo emitted). Together they close the canonical-submission-pipeline 7-layer architecture at Layer 6 (STRICT gate); Phase 10 (operator-runbook end-to-end CLI) consumes THIS gate's verdict via the canonical 4-verdict-chain validation at pre-`gh pr create` time. Per the 12th canonicalization × standardization × ease-of-contest-compliance trinity + the 13th OPTIMAL-TRIO standing directive: THIS gate IS the structural enforcement of the 4-layer-verdict-chain default-path so future PR111+ submissions are STRUCTURALLY incapable of shipping without going through the canonical Phase 4 + 5 + 6 + 7 verdict chain. **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = N/A; hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE PRIMARY** (THIS gate IS the structural protection that PR submissions cannot ship without the full 4-verdict chain; companion cathedral consumer `tac.cathedral_consumers.pr_submission_compliance_consumer` surfaces per-candidate readiness verdicts via Catalog #335 auto-discovery); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE** (per-submission PR-readiness verdict feeds the canonical posterior so Phase 10 first-PR111-candidate landings inherit the apriori canonical-compliance signal); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = **ACTIVE** (PR_READY vs BLOCKED_ON_<phase> IS the canonical disambiguator between full-canonical-pipeline-completion vs operator-routable blocker at the cathedral ranker surface). Mission contribution per Catalog #300: `apparatus_maintenance` (closes the canonical-submission-pipeline 7-layer architecture at Layer 6; THIS gate is structural protection of the canonical default-path so future PR111+ submissions are STRUCTURALLY incapable of shipping ad-hoc-hand-edited; unblocks the Phase 10 operator-runbook end-to-end CLI which consumes THIS gate's verdict at pre-`gh pr create` time). 367. `check_substrate_inflate_emits_expected_frame_count_or_fail_closed` — INFLATE RAW BYTES FAIL-OPEN extension to Catalog #146 self-protection 2026-05-26 per FIX-WAVE-CASCADE-C-PRIME-ALL-BUGS-PERMANENT-FIX-AND-SELF-PROTECT directive + CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable + Cascade C' WAVE-3 empirical anchor (commit `39e1db080` + fix commits `5bcb53070` + `d0c4517ea`). Sister of Catalog #146 at the FRAME-EMISSION-COUNT sub-surface; refuses inflate.py files that emit raw bytes AND reference the contest output contract but lack a fail-closed check on raw_bytes / frame_count / byte_count. Bug class anchor: Cascade C' WAVE-3 Modal T4 dispatch `fc-01KSKB4B30DCYTCP883XYV5BNV` 2026-05-26 rc=0 elapsed=11.62s with stage 1-6 SUCCESS but stage 7 contest_auth_eval crashed with `RuntimeError: [inflate] WRONG-SIZE .raw file(s): 0.raw=707788800B (expected 3662409600B). Each must be 3,662,409,600 bytes (1164x874x1200x3). Likely truncated mid-decode.` Empirical ratio: 707788800 / 3662409600 = 19.33%. Root cause: WAVE-3 inflate.py at commit `aaf0b1eb6` emitted 1200 frames at 384×512×3 resolution (1200 × 384 × 512 × 3 = 707,788,800) instead of 1200 frames at 1164×874×3 (contest output resolution). Fix landed Cascade C' commits `5bcb53070` + `d0c4517ea`; current inflate.py at HEAD has `CONTEST_OUT_H=874` + `CONTEST_OUT_W=1164` + `CONTEST_NUM_FRAMES=1200` + `CONTEST_RAW_BYTES = CONTEST_OUT_W * CONTEST_OUT_H * CONTEST_NUM_FRAMES * 3` + fail-closed `if raw_bytes != CONTEST_RAW_BYTES: raise AssertionError(...)`. This META-class self-protect catches ANY future regression where a substrate inflate.py emits raw bytes that don't match the contest 1164×874×1200×3 = 3,662,409,600 byte contract. Detection: scans `submissions/*/inflate.py` (excluding `submissions/exact_current/` per CLAUDE.md mutation frontier) + `src/tac/substrates/*/inflate.py` (120 files total) for files that emit raw bytes (`.raw` / `raw_bytes` / `with_suffix` tokens) AND reference the contest output contract (`CONTEST_RAW_BYTES` / `CONTEST_NUM_FRAMES` / `CONTEST_OUT_H` / `CONTEST_OUT_W` / `1164` / `3662409600` / `3_662_409_600` tokens); refuses files lacking ANY fail-closed token (`raise AssertionError` / `raise ValueError` / `raise RuntimeError` / `assert raw_bytes` / `!= CONTEST_RAW_BYTES` / `wrong-size` / `WRONG-SIZE` / etc.). Acceptance: (a) fail-closed token present in file; (b) file-level `# INFLATE_FRAME_COUNT_FAIL_OPEN_OK:<rationale>` waiver anywhere in file (rationale ≥4 chars; placeholder rejected per Catalog #287). Initial wire-in is **WARN-ONLY** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: 5 pre-existing inflate.py files (`pr101_lc_v2_clone` / `pr95_lora_dora` / `sabor_boundary_only_renderer` / `factorized_hnerv_v1` / `pr103_pr106_final_runtime` — all L1 scaffolds or submitted-runtime variants that predate Catalog #367). Strict-flip pending operator-routed backfill sweep that EITHER adds the canonical fail-closed check OR adds an explicit waiver per file. Sister of Catalog #146 (contest-compliant template at design surface; #367 is the runtime-effect surface) + Catalog #205 (canonical select_inflate_device) + Catalog #220 (L1+ scaffold operational mechanism) + Catalog #272 (distinguishing-feature integration contract) + Catalog #295 (PYTHONPATH self-containment). Together they extinct the inflate-emits-wrong-bytes bug class at FIVE surfaces: contract template (#146) + device selection (#205) + operational mechanism (#220) + distinguishing-feature contract (#272) + PYTHONPATH self-containment (#295) + raw-bytes fail-closed check (#367 — THIS gate). 17 dedicated tests in `src/tac/tests/test_check_365_366_367_cascade_c_prime_bug_class_extinction.py`. 6-hook wire-in per Catalog #125: hooks N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = ACTIVE (prevents future Modal dispatches from silently emitting wrong-sized raw bytes and crashing contest_auth_eval WRONG-SIZE post-train, wasting paid GPU spend); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = ACTIVE (the canonical fail-closed check IS the disambiguator between correctly-sized contest raw output vs resolution/frame-count mismatch). Retroactive sweep per Catalog #348: `.omx/research/retroactive_sweep_for_catalog_367_20260527T002931Z.md`. Memory: `feedback_fix_wave_cascade_c_prime_all_bugs_permanent_fix_and_self_protect_against_landed_20260526.md`. Lane: `lane_fix_wave_cascade_c_prime_all_bugs_permanent_fix_and_self_protect_against_20260526` L1.

373. `check_compound_stack_proposal_acknowledges_known_anti_patterns` — CANONICAL-ANTI-PATTERNS REGISTRY 2026-05-28 Layer 3 self-protection per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE verbatim *"learning anti-patterns is upser important too for compounding continual learning, like the canonical equations bu netgative and a higher layer of abstraction"* + canonical anti-patterns design memo (`.omx/research/canonical_anti_patterns_registry_design_20260528.md` commit `37b5a0184`) §"Layer 3" + Layer 1+2 landing memo (`.omx/research/canonical_anti_patterns_registry_layer_1_plus_2_landed_20260528.md`) + Wave N+2 mandate. Sister of Catalog #344 (canonical equations memo-reference enforcement; #344 is the POSITIVE registry surface, #373 is the NEGATIVE registry surface) at the memo-acknowledgment surface. Refuses post-cutoff (date suffix >= 2026-05-29) landing memos under `.omx/research/*_landed_<YYYYMMDD>.md` (Wave N+1 + earlier exempt per "Strict-flip atomicity rule") that propose a compound stack (regex triggers: `compound stack` / `compounding order` / `stacking` / `stack-of-stacks` / `stack of stacks` / `combined codec`) whose body matches one or more registered anti-patterns via `tac.canonical_anti_patterns.match_stack_against_anti_patterns` WITHOUT one of: (a) Citing the matched `anti_pattern_id` verbatim AND referencing the canonical unwind path via one of the canonical acknowledgment tokens (`anti_pattern_id=` / `anti-pattern: ` / `canonical_unwind_path` / `matched anti-pattern` / `ANTI_PATTERN_MATCH_RATIFIED`); (b) Same-line waiver `# ANTI_PATTERN_MATCH_INTENTIONAL_OK:<rationale>` with non-placeholder rationale ≥4 chars (placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive); (c) Empirical falsification ratified to the canonical registry via `tac.canonical_anti_patterns.append_empirical_falsification` (covered structurally via citation of `ANTI_PATTERN_MATCH_RATIFIED` token + sister `EVENT_FALSIFICATION_APPENDED` row). **Layer 5 sister-integration** (same commit batch): `tac.dykstra_pareto_solver.AntiPatternConstraint` + extended `DykstraParetoSolver` accepts `anti_pattern_constraints: tuple[AntiPatternConstraint, ...]` field; `_apply_anti_pattern_constraints` projection helper appends per-constraint dual variables to `ParetoSolverVerdict.per_axis_dual_variables` with canonical prefix `anti_pattern_<id>`; binding constraints revoke feasibility per the design memo §"Mathematical compounding identity" MAX-aggregation identity (one violated anti-pattern can corrupt the entire stack); `tools/cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop.py::invoke_dykstra_pareto_solver_on_candidates` auto-derives constraints per candidate via `_derive_anti_pattern_constraints_for_candidate` (which delegates to `match_stack_against_anti_patterns`) and surfaces per-candidate `matched_anti_patterns` + `binding_anti_pattern_ids` + `canonical_unwind_paths_recommended` + aggregate `anti_pattern_binding_histogram` in the invocation payload per Catalog #305 observability surface. **Canonical equation** `anti_pattern_polytope_exclusion_dykstra_compounding_v1` registered via `tac.canonical_equations.register_canonical_equation` per Catalog #344 with Boyd-Vandenberghe (2004) Chapter 5 (Duality) Lagrangian-dual derivation; producers list `tac.dykstra_pareto_solver.anti_pattern_constraint.aggregate_anti_pattern_duals` + `AntiPatternConstraint.dual_variable` + `tac.canonical_anti_patterns.match_stack_against_anti_patterns`; consumers list `DykstraParetoSolver._apply_anti_pattern_constraints` + `solve_pareto_polytope_intersection` + `tools.cathedral_autopilot_autonomous_loop._derive_anti_pattern_constraints_for_candidate` + `invoke_dykstra_pareto_solver_on_candidates`. Bug class: pre-Layer-3, the canonical anti-patterns registry existing at `tac.canonical_anti_patterns` (Layer 1+2 landed 2026-05-28) was necessary but not sufficient; landing memos proposing compound stack work could re-discover a registered anti-pattern by burning paid GPU dispatch WITHOUT the structural memo-level acknowledgment gate at the proposal surface. Layer 5 (this commit batch's sister integration) makes anti-patterns ACTIVE Pareto polytope feasibility constraints; THIS Layer 3 gate makes them REVIEWABLE memo-level obligations BEFORE the compound stack work is proposed. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL": a matched anti-pattern is NOT a kill verdict on the proposed technique; the gate routes the canonical unwind path so the operator chooses (a) apply the canonical alternative, (b) waive with substantive rationale, or (c) ratify the anti-pattern with empirical falsification. Per the design memo §"Mathematical compounding identity": this gate enforces the LEFT side of `NextCycleAttackDirection = argmax_axis (PredictedΔS_axis_i × λ_axis_i_tight_from_Dykstra) subject to (NOT any (proposed_stack matches AntiPattern_j AND not waived))`; Layer 5 implements the RIGHT side via ACTIVE polytope-exclusion constraints. Together they extinct the "compound stacking work re-discovers a known anti-pattern via paid GPU dispatch" failure mode at TWO surfaces: memo-level acknowledgment (THIS gate) + runtime polytope feasibility (Layer 5 via Catalog #372 invoker). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — live count at landing: **0** (cutoff exempts pre-2026-05-29 memos; Wave N+1 + earlier exempt). Sister of Catalog #344 (canonical equations memo-reference enforcement) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection) + Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT callsites have CLAUDE.md row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: 0 verified empirically) + Catalog #372 (sister Slot 1 Dykstra Pareto solver invoker; Layer 5 sister-integration gate at the runtime surface) + Catalog #335 (canonical cathedral consumer contract for `anti_pattern_lookup_consumer`) + Catalog #341 (Tier A canonical-routing markers; Layer 2 consumer carries them) + Catalog #324 (post-training Tier-C validation; sister negative-constraint surface at the recipe-emit surface) + Catalog #125 (6-hook wire-in non-negotiable). **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = ACTIVE (anti-pattern severity contributes to per-substrate sensitivity ranking via canonical severity weights); hook #2 Pareto constraint = **ACTIVE PRIMARY** (Layer 5 IS canonical conversion of anti-patterns → ACTIVE polytope exclusion via `_apply_anti_pattern_constraints` MAX-aggregation per the design memo §"Mathematical compounding identity"); hook #3 bit-allocator = ACTIVE (anti-pattern unwind paths route bit-allocator AWAY; Slot 1 Compound C heterogeneous bit allocation consumes the surface); hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = ACTIVE (Layer 3 STRICT at memo surface; Layer 5 at dispatch routing via cathedral autopilot invoker per-candidate payload surface); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = ACTIVE (solver verdicts with anti-pattern duals append to canonical posterior; canonical equation `anti_pattern_polytope_exclusion_dykstra_compounding_v1` anchors accumulate; Catalog #371 auto-recalibrator + Layer 4 sister auto-recalibrator at `tac.canonical_anti_patterns.registry.auto_recalibrate_from_continual_learning_posterior` refit); The cathedral autopilot IS the canonical META-orchestrator (operator-caught canonical anti-pattern: do NOT build parallel package; EXTEND existing canonical surface). **Wave N+46 META-orchestrator extension** closes 4 missing gaps: (1) 3-metric trichotomy ranking via `tac.cathedral_autopilot.rank_candidates_via_three_metric_trichotomy`; (2) operator-correction META-pattern formalization via `tac.cathedral_autopilot.register_operator_binding_correction`; (3) per-turn main-thread spawn-decision helper via `tac.cathedral_autopilot.select_canonical_next_spawn_for_main_thread`; (4) no-ad-hoc/signal-loss/rediscovery/duplicate/drift invariants via `tac.cathedral_autopilot.validate_no_ad_hoc_no_signal_loss_no_rediscovery_no_duplicate_no_drift_invariants`. **5 canonical equations registered** per Catalog #344 (`meta_orchestrator_highest_ev_shortest_wall_clock_metric_v1` / `meta_orchestrator_three_metric_trichotomy_orthogonality_v1` / `meta_orchestrator_variance_acceptance_dominance_at_low_probability_high_leverage_v1` / `meta_orchestrator_lesson_set_completeness_lower_bound_v1` / `meta_orchestrator_cap_window_discipline_per_turn_variance_amortization_v1`). **5 canonical anti-patterns registered** per Catalog #344 sister discipline (`manual_main_thread_orchestrator_ranking_drift_across_turns_via_ad_hoc_priority_assignment_v1` / `operator_correction_canonical_apparatus_mutation_lag_v1` / `spawn_prompt_boilerplate_duplication_across_subagent_waves_v1` / `hygiene_ev_vs_frontier_breaking_ev_vs_highest_ev_shortest_wall_clock_conflation_canonical_rediscovery_v1` / `metric_orthogonality_inversion_safest_incremental_as_default_when_operator_wants_variance_acceptance_v1`). **Cathedral consumer** `tac.cathedral_consumers.meta_orchestrator_consumer` auto-discovered per Catalog #335 canonical contract (Tier A canonical-routing markers per Catalog #341 + #357; hooks 4+5+6 ACTIVE). Acceptance: (a) any of the 2 acceptance tokens appears as a Call node in main() body via AST scan; (b) same-line waiver `# META_ORCHESTRATOR_THREE_METRIC_TRICHOTOMY_INVOKER_WAIVED:<rationale>` on the `def main` line with non-placeholder rationale (≥4 chars; placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). **STRICT-from-byte-one** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — the Wave N+46 extension lands the invoker callsite in same commit batch driving live count to 0 (verified empirically). 377. `check_module_existence_check_is_case_sensitive` — MODAL DISPATCH RUNTIME-TREE-HASH LOCAL vs WORKER PARITY self-protection 2026-05-28 (per Wave N+9 Slot 4 PR111-candidate paired-CUDA RATIFICATION DEFER `6bc74e074` empirical anchor + CLAUDE.md "Apples-to-apples evidence discipline" + "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiables + CLAUDE.md "Modal `.spawn()` HARVEST OR LOSE" non-negotiable). Refuses any state of `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py` that drops the canonical `_path_exists_case_sensitive` helper OR drops the `iterdir` token in `_module_exists` / `_module_paths`. **Bug class anchor**: PR111 composite (NSCS06 v8 chroma_lut + Compound C heterogeneous bit, sha `dfff1358638ef7f7`, 1.92 MB) paired-CUDA RATIFICATION 4× DEFER 2026-05-28 (Modal call_ids `fc-01KSQVET...` + `fc-01KSQVFG...` + `fc-01KSQVM7...` + `fc-01KSQVMY...`; cumulative paid Modal spend ~$0.06 producing ZERO score evidence). Empirical receipts: pre-fix LOCAL projector computed CUDA=`efa31c12...` / CPU=`c886528c...` but Modal worker actual was CUDA=`1e9bf123...` / CPU=`60256159...`; deterministic mismatch across 2 attempts × 2 axes. Root cause: `_module_exists` / `_module_paths` used `Path.exists()` which case-folds on macOS HFS+/APFS / Windows NTFS; LOCAL projector picked up phantom `tac.dykstra_pareto_solver.Polytope` (capital P) via `polytope.py` case-fold match; Linux Modal worker correctly returned False. `module_count` diverged 37 (LOCAL) vs 36 (WORKER); `file_count` diverged 40 vs 39; `repo_local_tac_import_manifest` dict-payload diverged cascading into `runtime_tree_sha256` mismatch failing dispatch pre-validation at `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py::_validate_expected_runtime_tree` with `RuntimeError("inflate runtime tree hash mismatch: expected=... actual=...")`. **Canonical fix** (companion landing in same commit batch): NEW `_path_exists_case_sensitive(path)` helper walks `parent.iterdir()` and requires exact basename match for every component; symlinks + missing paths handled conservatively (return False); `_module_exists` + `_module_paths` route through the new helper. Empirical proof: post-fix LOCAL projector produces CUDA=`1e9bf123e8eac353591c2fa57af96d3eb330855d34d375faa886dc9c32026afb` + CPU=`60256159c7d65405fca5139d8ffd4a81a8444c13ed0dd804fd0aecb6097b55e6` = EXACT MATCH with Modal worker actual hashes; phantom Polytope module eliminated from `repo_local_tac_import_manifest.root_import_modules`; `module_count` correctly 36; `file_count` correctly 39. **Acceptance**: (a) both required tokens (`_path_exists_case_sensitive` + `iterdir`) present in `experiments/contest_auth_eval.py`; (b) target file missing is silent (sister gates own existence). NO waiver mechanism — the canonical helper IS the contract. **STRICT-from-byte-one** per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — the canonical fix lands in same commit batch driving live count to 0. Sister of Catalog #146 (contest-compliant inflate runtime template; design surface) + Catalog #205 (canonical `select_inflate_device`; per-file inflate.py device-fork surface) + Catalog #229 (premise-verification-before-edit; pre-edit discipline) + Catalog #270 (canonical dispatch optimization protocol umbrella) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection) + Catalog #307 (paradigm-vs-implementation falsification; this incident was IMPLEMENTATION-LEVEL not paradigm-level per the Catalog #307 classification) + Catalog #313 (probe-outcomes ledger; sister PROCEED row supersedes the DEFER row) + Catalog #323 (canonical Provenance umbrella) + Catalog #344 (canonical equations registry; canonical equation `modal_dispatch_runtime_tree_hash_local_vs_worker_parity_v1` registered in same commit batch + canonical anti-pattern `modal_dispatch_local_projector_vs_worker_extraction_root_divergence_v1` registered via `tac.canonical_anti_patterns.register_anti_pattern`) + Catalog #348 (retroactive sweep for new gate; companion memo emitted) + Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT-callsite-has-CLAUDE.md-row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: 0 verified empirically) + Catalog #299 (catalog quota brake under 400; current 377 well under). Together they extinct the LOCAL-projector-vs-Modal-worker runtime-tree-hash divergence bug class STRUCTURALLY at FOUR orthogonal surfaces: source-text helper presence (#377 — THIS gate) + canonical equation positive invariant (#344) + canonical anti-pattern negative recurrence (#344 sister) + probe-outcomes ledger PROCEED disambiguation (#313). **Catalog #299 quota brake decision**: NEW gate (NOT scope extension of #205 / #146 / #270) because the macOS case-fold filesystem-semantics surface is structurally distinct from device-selection (#205) / contest runtime contract (#146) / Tier 1/2/3 hardware optimization (#270) surfaces; the canonical 2-landing pattern (immediate fix + sister STRICT gate) is the cathedral-friendly pattern per CLAUDE.md "Beauty, simplicity, and developer experience" + the 13th OPTIMAL-TRIO standing directive. **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = ACTIVE via NEW canonical equation `modal_dispatch_runtime_tree_hash_local_vs_worker_parity_v1` Pareto polytope constraint (hash parity invariant); hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE** (canonical equation IS the infrastructure-layer disambiguator; future dispatch wrappers cannot silently re-introduce the macOS case-fold sub-class because the STRICT gate refuses regression structurally); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE** (canonical equation + EmpiricalAnchor row + canonical anti-pattern + EmpiricalFalsification row all land in the canonical posterior); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = **ACTIVE** (hash parity IS the canonical infrastructure-layer disambiguator between LOCAL-projector-matches-worker vs LOCAL-projector-diverges-from-worker). Mission contribution per Catalog #300: `frontier_protecting` (extincts the silent-paired-dispatch-pre-validation-failure bug class structurally so future PR111-candidate / sister composite RATIFICATIONS cannot lose paid Modal spend to macOS case-fold; preserves apples-to-apples evidence discipline per CLAUDE.md). 371. `check_no_orphan_auto_trigger_stub_with_satisfied_condition` — STUB-AUDIT-AND-FIX wave 2026-05-27 (operator directive "fix all stubs and continue iterating and optimizing and auditing") + CLAUDE.md "Results must become system intelligence" (a no-op consumer of landed evidence is the orphan-work failure mode) + "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable. **Bug class anchor**: `tac.canonical_equations.registry.auto_recalibrate_from_continual_learning_posterior` shipped as a no-op stub (`equations_recalibrated=0,  # stub; auto-refit comes in a follow-on landing`) that returned WITHOUT refitting even when its OWN documented trigger condition (`when_3+_new_empirical_anchors_in_domain`) was ALREADY satisfied. Empirical receipt: 11 of 64 canonical equations carried stale `predicted_vs_empirical_residual` summaries that no longer matched their own landed `EmpiricalAnchor` rows (including stale NaN sentinels + synthetic `count`/`max_abs_residual`/`amplification_factor` keys polluting `is_well_calibrated`), and the stub no-op'd on all of them. canonical equation #2 (`hinton_kl_distill_enables_qat_catalyst_composition_savings_v1`) held 3 anchors falsifying the closed-form alpha=0.15 lift toward an empirical ~0 (2nd anchor IMPLEMENTATION_LEVEL_FALSIFIED catalyst-NOT-improving-over-baseline; 3rd PARTIAL_CONFIRMATION delta=0.0055 not 0.15), yet its summary + `last_calibration_utc` never moved because the recalibrator no-op'd even with its trigger satisfied. **The fix** (companion landing same commit batch): the recalibrator now, for every equation whose `next_recalibration_trigger` is `when_3+_new_empirical_anchors_in_domain` AND anchor count >= 3, re-derives `predicted_vs_empirical_residual` directly from the equation's own landed anchors (latest-wins per `measurement_method` axis, mirroring `with_new_anchor`), and if the recomputed map differs from the stored map, appends a canonical `EVENT_RECALIBRATED` row (bumping `last_calibration_utc`). NOT a Catalog #287 violation: no anchor synthesized + no empirical_output fabricated — only the residual SUMMARY is re-derived from anchors that already landed via signed `update_equation_with_empirical_anchor` calls. Idempotent (2nd run recalibrates 0). Equations on other triggers (`when_residual_drift_exceeds_2x` / `when_operator_invokes` / `never_auto`) reported but NOT auto-refit. **This gate** refuses re-introduction of the orphan-auto-trigger-stub: an `auto_recalibrate_*` function that hardcodes `equations_recalibrated=0` / carries the follow-on-stub marker WITHOUT wiring the canonical `EVENT_RECALIBRATED` emission path. Acceptance: (a) the recalibrator references `EVENT_RECALIBRATED` (refit emission wired); OR (b) the no-op marker line carries a same-line `# DEFERRED_STUB_OK:<reactivation-criteria>` waiver with a substantive non-placeholder criterion (>= 4 chars; `<rationale>` / `<reason>` / `<reactivation-criteria>` placeholders rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline). STRICT-from-byte-one per CLAUDE.md "Strict-flip atomicity rule" — the working recalibrator lands in the same commit batch driving live count to 0. Sister of Catalog #335 / #336 / #337 / #355 (orphan-signal / facade-wire-in extinction at the cathedral consumer + master-gradient + meta-Lagrangian surfaces; #371 is the auto-trigger-stub sub-surface) + Catalog #344 (canonical equations registry — the surface this recalibrator keeps coherent) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection) + Catalog #176 (META-meta: STRICT callsites have CLAUDE.md row — THIS entry satisfies that META gate) + Catalog #185 (META-meta-meta: Live count: 0 verified empirically) + Catalog #299 (catalog quota brake under 400; current 371 well under). **6-hook wire-in declaration** per Catalog #125: hook #1 sensitivity-map = N/A (defensive validator gate); hook #2 Pareto constraint = N/A; hook #3 bit-allocator = N/A; hook #4 cathedral autopilot dispatch = **ACTIVE** (canonical equation residual summaries the cathedral consumer reads stay evidence-faithful so the autopilot ranker cannot consume a stale prior); hook #5 continual-learning posterior = **ACTIVE PRIMARY** (the recalibrator IS the consumer that absorbs landed continual-learning anchors into the canonical equation residual summary); hook #6 probe-disambiguator = N/A. Mission contribution per Catalog #300: `apparatus_maintenance` (extincts the orphan-auto-trigger-stub class; the canonical-equation registry residual summaries now stay coherent with landed evidence so future agents inherit the improved system rather than tribal-knowledge stale priors). Tests: `src/tac/canonical_equations/tests/test_auto_recalibrate_refit.py` (15 tests covering refit-fires-on-stale-map / EVENT_RECALIBRATED emission / APPEND-ONLY prior-payload preservation / idempotency / no-write-when-already-matches / NaN-sentinel drop / no-refit-below-3-anchors / no-refit-for-drift-trigger / no-refit-for-operator-only-trigger / zero-anchor-reported-not-refit / report-summary-flags / absorbed-count / full-scan-eligible-only / no-synthesized-anchors / end-to-end-after-real-append) + `src/tac/tests/test_check_371_orphan_auto_trigger_stub.py` (16 tests covering live-repo regression / no-op-without-emission-flagged / placeholder-waiver-rejected / missing-function-flagged / strict-raise / wired-accepted / real-waiver-accepted / wired-with-residual-comment-accepted / missing-target-silent / string-repo-root / body-extractor unit / verbose / orchestrator-wires-strict-true / Catalog #185 sister-callable). Retroactive sweep per Catalog #348: `.omx/research/retroactive_sweep_for_catalog_371_20260527T140000Z.md`. Memory: `feedback_stub_audit_and_fix_wave_landed_20260527.md`. 363. `check_council_deliberation_has_empirical_verification_status` — RECURSIVE-SELF-REFLECTION PROTOCOL self-protection 2026-05-26 (per operator NON-NEGOTIABLE directive 2026-05-26 verbatim *"the grand council is providing valuable information but perhaps the grand council itself must be instructed to deliberate and self reflect recursively"*). Lifts the CLAUDE.md "Recursive adversarial review protocol — close paths" 3-clean-pass counter from the training-code review surface to the **council deliberation surface**: every T2+ council deliberation MUST recursively self-reflect on its OWN deliberation process, classifying each per-member surfaced assumption (already required by Catalog #292) into the canonical 4-value `empirical_verification_status` taxonomy (`VERIFIED_VIA_SOURCE_INSPECTION` / `VERIFIED_VIA_EMPIRICAL_ANCHOR` / `INFERRED_FROM_DOMAIN_LITERATURE` / `ASSUMED_AWAITING_VERIFICATION`). Verdicts depending on `INFERRED` / `ASSUMED`-class assumptions must EITHER empirically verify before landing OR downgrade verdict-status to `PROVISIONAL-PENDING-VERIFICATION`. **Empirical receipts (3+ instances within <2h on 2026-05-26)**: (1) T3 grand council `7d04474cb` M3 RULED-OUT empirically falsified by sister TIER1-T3-OP1-OP4 source-inspection `5b87fae77` (Z6 uses MLX AdamW not stateless SGD-with-EMA as the council assumed); (2) T3 council M2 ~0.7-0.9 α dominance prediction empirically falsified by sister TIER1-T3-OP2-OP3 Carmack smoke `05c07aa40` (canonical Kahan-EMA shadow wrapper provides 0× empirical mitigation at Z6 L2 fp32 1000ep); (3) my own n=2 super-linear α∝epochs^1.45 extrapolation empirically falsified by sister DRIFT-VS-DEPTH-CHAR-D-Z6 `60a9de751` (5-anchor fit yielding α=0.47 sub-linear saturating at ~2000ep); (4) K=COIN++ 5e-3 drift claim earlier 2026-05-26 empirically falsified by sister R1''-K independent verification commits leading to `2d59283d4` (actual O(1e-2) abs / O(1e-3) rel). All 4 verdicts achieved quorum-met + Catalog #346 complete=True + Catalog #292 assumption-statement-surfacing satisfied. **The structural blindness recurs at the sister surface**: existing gates enforce that assumptions ARE surfaced + classified HARD-EARNED-vs-CARGO-CULTED, but NOT that each surfaced assumption carries explicit `empirical_verification_status` AND that verdicts depending on `INFERRED` / `ASSUMED`-class assumptions are gated. **3-clean-pass counter discipline** (lifted from training-code Recursive adversarial review protocol): Round 1 (topic deliberation; each per-member assumption carries explicit `empirical_verification_status` field) → Round 2 (self-reflection; council SELF-REFLECTS on Round 1; re-classifies each assumption's status after dedicated verification cycle attempt; emits `council_self_reflection_round_N` canonical posterior anchor) → Round 3 (resolution; material unverified assumptions trigger empirical verification before landing OR verdict-status downgrade to `PROVISIONAL-PENDING-VERIFICATION` OR `ESCALATE_TO_OPERATOR` per Catalog #300). **Cycle bounds** (per R12-D meta-finding lens-coverage): `MAX_SELF_REFLECTION_ROUNDS = 5`; SEAL when 3 consecutive rounds produce zero material unverified-assumption findings. **Required at T2+ deliberations**; T1 working-group recommendations are exempt (T1 findings feed downstream T2/T3 which inherit the discipline). Acceptance: (a) post-2026-05-26 council memos carry the canonical 4-value taxonomy + Round 2/3 discipline tokens; (b) same-line `# COUNCIL_EMPIRICAL_VERIFICATION_STATUS_WAIVED:<rationale>` waiver with non-placeholder rationale (≥4 chars; placeholder literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline). WARN-ONLY at landing per "Strict-flip atomicity rule"; strict-flip planned after backfill brings live count to 0. **Canonical surfaces**: `tac.council_continual_learning.EmpiricalVerificationStatus` (4-value sentinel constants) + `AssumptionEmpiricalVerification` (frozen dataclass) + `classify_assumption_verification_status_from_evidence` / `extract_unverified_assumptions` / `verdict_status_requires_provisional_marker` / `query_self_reflection_history_for_deliberation` canonical helpers. Sister of Catalog #292 (per-deliberation assumption-statement-surfacing axis; #363 enforces the sister per-assumption empirical-verification-status axis at a distinct sub-surface) + Catalog #291 (META-ASSUMPTION cadence — session-level cousin) + Catalog #300 (v2 frontmatter — canonical posterior schema; #363 records propagate via the existing `council_assumption_adversary_verdict` field structure extended with the 4-value taxonomy) + Catalog #340 / #314 (PREVENT vs DETECT sister gate pattern precedent that #363 lifts to the council-deliberation surface) + Catalog #346 (canonical roster validation — structurally distinct axis) + Catalog #287 (placeholder-rationale rejection sister discipline) + Catalog #344 (canonical equations registry — Round 2 verification path may register the verified assumption as a canonical equation when generalizable). Canonical design memo: `.omx/research/council_recursive_self_reflection_protocol_design_20260526T133600Z.md`. Landing memo: `feedback_council_recursive_self_reflection_protocol_landed_20260526.md`. Lane: `lane_council_recursive_self_reflection_protocol_landed_20260526` L1. **Numbered row backfilled 2026-05-29 via SLOT P** (Slot N Round 1 M5 SOFT-LOW corrective action closure; per Catalog #176 META-meta gate discipline `check_strict_preflight_callsites_have_claude_md_catalog_row` even though #363 wire-in remains WARN-ONLY pending strict-flip; the numbered row provides operator-facing surface coherence per Catalog #299 catalog table integrity).

361. `check_modal_artifact_filter_preserves_submission_dir` — OVERNIGHT-GG META-fix self-protection 2026-05-21 (per CLAUDE.md "Bugs must be permanently fixed AND self-protected against" non-negotiable + OVERNIGHT-CC `99d06f967` IMPLEMENTATION-LEVEL falsification per Catalog #307). Refuses any state of `experiments/modal_train_lane.py` where the artifact harvester applies `mtime_floor` to files under `output/submission/` without the canonical `under_submission` bypass. **Bug class anchor**: OVERNIGHT-CC 99d06f967 4 paired DP1 auth_eval Modal dispatches (fc-01KS5KW2EZBYYKV8DP1H8T0X2D + 3 sister CPU/CUDA arms) returned rc=1 in ~3s each with `ModuleNotFoundError: tac.substrates.pretrained_driving_prior.inflate` because the DP1 trainer's `_write_runtime` correctly invoked `shutil.copy2` to vendor 8 module bodies (architecture.py / archive.py / codebook.py / inflate.py / prior_application.py / procedural_codebook_inflate.py / seed_derived_codebook.py / _shared/inflate_runtime.py) into `output/submission/src/tac/.../`, but the harvester silently DROPPED all 8 via `artifact_mtime_floor = time.time() - 5.0` filter. Root cause: `shutil.copy2` preserves source mtime; source files at `/tmp/pact/src/tac/...` carry old local-repo mtimes (days/weeks) propagated through Modal's `copytree(symlinks=True)` mount staging. `write_text` / `write_bytes` (used for `__init__.py` stubs + top-level inflate.py string emission) produce current-mtime files that DID pass the floor, while `shutil.copy2` bodies did not. The mtime_floor's purpose is to filter STALE pre-existing artifacts from prior runs; it is WRONG to apply to `output/submission/` since that subtree is the trainer's atomic submission emission per Catalog #146 inflate runtime contract. Canonical fix bypasses the mtime_floor for `output/submission/` paths specifically. Per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden premature KILL without research exhaustion": this is IMPLEMENTATION-LEVEL fix, NOT paradigm change; DP1 substrate paradigm INTACT. Acceptance: (a) harvester loop contains canonical bypass tokens (`under_submission` variable + `parts[0] == "output"` + `parts[1] == "submission"` + `if not under_submission and st.st_mtime < artifact_mtime_floor`); (b) file-level waiver `# CATALOG_361_HARVESTER_FILTER_WAIVED:<rationale>` in first 80 lines with non-placeholder rationale (≥4 chars; placeholder `<rationale>` / `<reason>` literals rejected per Catalog #287 sister discipline so the gate's docstring example cannot self-waive). **Sister-extinction architecture**: companion defense-in-depth `tac.substrates._shared.trainer_skeleton.vendor_module_with_fresh_mtime` (canonical helper combining `shutil.copy2` + `os.utime(dst, None)`) makes substrate trainers robust against this filter even WITHOUT the META-layer fix; substrate trainers SHOULD route through the canonical helper instead of bare `shutil.copy2`. DP1 trainer's `_write_runtime` migrated to canonical helper in same commit batch.
